symptom: Failed to delete SAML app: appId/identifierUris invalid or trailing slash/unverified domain errors
rootCause: App ID URI has trailing slash, unverified domain, or SAML app misconfigured as public client
solution: Fix App ID URI format. For public client: disable Allow public client flow in Authentication settings.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: AADSTS50012 error and auth loop in Edge with AlternateID enabled
rootCause: Bug when AlternateIdLogin enabled and multiple credentials present (Bug 2914950)
solution: Use different browser or clear Edge cookies. PG tracking fix.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: Approving admin consent automatically adds approving admin to Users and Groups
rootCause: Intended product behavior confirmed by PM. Not configurable.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Azure AD Applications KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: AADSTS650059: The application is not configured for use in tenant. SignInAudience AzureADMyOrg limits use outside home tenant. App was converted from multi-tenant to single-tenant but service principals still exist in external tenants.
rootCause: App registration signInAudience was changed from multi-tenant to single-tenant (AzureADMyOrg), but SPs in external tenants still try to obtain tokens. ESTS now blocks single-tenant apps from being used outside home tenant.
solution: If app should support multiple tenants: update signInAudience to AzureADMultipleOrgs or AzureADandPersonalMicrosoftAccount. If single-tenant is intended: no action needed. Whitelisted apps deadline: July 14, 2025.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Authentication Method Policy

symptom: Failed to add identifier URI: All newly added URIs must contain a tenant verified domain, tenant ID, or appId, as per tenant policy. Occurs after May 19, 2025 enforcement of identifier URI protection.
rootCause: Tenant app management policy enforces identifier URI protection (uriAdditionWithoutUniqueTenantIdentifier or nonDefaultUriAddition restriction). New URIs must contain unique tenant identifier.
solution: Options: 1) Use default URI api://{appId} or api://{tenantId}/{appId}. 2) Switch to v2 tokens (requestedAccessTokenVersion=2). 3) Configure as SAML SSO app. 4) Get per-app exemption via custom appManagementPolicy. 5) Get per-actor exemption via custom security attribute. Admin can run DisableIdentifierUriProtection.ps1 to opt out.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Authentication Method Policy

symptom: App Management Policy portal error: The restriction have been modified outside of this interface. Editing disabled until restrictions synchronized.
rootCause: Policy was configured via Graph API or PowerShell, causing settings across passwordCredentials/keyCredentials collections to be misaligned with what the Entra portal UX expects.
solution: Use Graph Explorer to manually align settings across all four collections (applicationRestrictions.passwordCredentials, servicePrincipalRestrictions.passwordCredentials, etc.). Ensure restrictForAppsCreatedAfterDateTime and maxLifetime values match.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Authentication Method Policy

symptom: App management policy maxLifetime restriction not enforced: application can create secrets with any lifetime despite passwordLifetime policy being configured.
rootCause: App was created BEFORE restrictForAppsCreatedAfterDateTime. Also if maxLifetime > 730 days, portal options for secrets < 24 months will not be blocked.
solution: Verify app createdDateTime >= restrictForAppsCreatedAfterDateTime. Adjust date to cover older apps. Ensure maxLifetime <= 730 days. Check with Get-MgApplication.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Authentication Method Policy

symptom: SignInAudience value AzureADMultipleOrgs not allowed as per assigned policy. Failed to create multi-tenant application.
rootCause: Tenant or custom app management policy has audiences restriction (azureAdMultipleOrgs disabled) blocking creation/promotion of multi-tenant apps.
solution: Contact tenant admin. Create custom appManagementPolicy with azureAdMultipleOrgs state=enabled and assign to specific app. Or use single-tenant AzureADMyOrg.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Authentication Method Policy

symptom: AADSTS65001: The user or administrator has not consented to use the application. User sees Need admin approval during sign-in.
rootCause: Required permissions not consented. Causes: User.Read missing, user consent disabled, user assignment required, SP missing for client/resource app, or scopes in request not consented.
solution: 1) Add/consent User.Read. 2) Check tenant user consent settings. 3) Verify SP exists for client and resource apps. 4) Verify all scopes consented. 5) Admin consent via V2 endpoint with prompt=consent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Troubleshooting/Troubleshooting Consent in Azure AD

symptom: AADSTS650056: Misconfigured application. Client has not listed any permissions for AAD Graph, or admin has not consented in tenant.
rootCause: App registration missing required permissions configuration, or permissions don't match what app requests at sign-in.
solution: Review app registration API permissions. Ensure all required permissions listed. Grant admin consent via Enterprise Applications > Permissions. Verify application identifier matches.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Troubleshooting/Troubleshooting Consent in Azure AD

symptom: AADSTS900941: Admin consent required, app considered risky. Or AADSTS900981: Admin consent request for risky app warning.
rootCause: Microsoft Identity Protection flagged app as potentially risky based on behavior, permissions, or publisher characteristics.
solution: Review risk detection in Audit Logs. If legitimate, admin can approve. Check publisher verification status. Note: Identity Protection not available in sovereign clouds.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Troubleshooting/Troubleshooting Consent in Azure AD

symptom: Application consent prompt appearing twice during sign-in. User accepts, redirected to login, prompted again. Some scopes missing in first prompt.
rootCause: Application sends dynamic scopes. By design, during first call only existing graph delegations evaluated; dynamic scopes evaluated during second call.
solution: By-design behavior. Workaround: pre-configure all required permissions in app registration and grant admin consent upfront to avoid dynamic consent flow.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Troubleshooting/Troubleshooting Consent in Azure AD

symptom: AADSTS650052: The app needs access to a service that your organization has not subscribed to or enabled.
rootCause: Resource application (API) service principal does not exist in the tenant. Tenant has not subscribed to or enabled the service.
solution: Provision resource SP in tenant via admin consent. If Microsoft service, may need to be enabled/subscribed first.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Troubleshooting/Troubleshooting Consent in Azure AD

symptom: AADSTS500011: The resource principal named {App-Uri-Id} was not found in the tenant. Application not installed by admin or consented to by any user.
rootCause: Resource identified by App-Uri-Id does not exist as SP in specified tenant. URI invalid, app is single-tenant accessed from wrong tenant, or resource SP never provisioned.
solution: Verify App-Uri-Id and Tenant-Id correct. If resource in different tenant, app registration must be multi-tenant. Provision resource SP via admin consent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Troubleshooting/Troubleshooting Consent in Azure AD

symptom: AADSTS90094/AADSTS90095: Admin consent required. 90094 when admin consent workflow disabled, 90095 when enabled. Non-admin user cannot consent.
rootCause: Four causes: 1) Request includes prompt=consent. 2) Risky permissions detected. 3) User assignment required without admin consent. 4) Permissions in scope not admin consented (Files.Read.All, Sites.Read.All require admin consent even if AdminConsentRequired=false).
solution: Cause 1: Remove prompt=consent. Cause 2: Review risk in audit logs. Cause 3: Disable user assignment or grant admin consent. Cause 4: As Global Admin, use /authorize endpoint with prompt=consent, check Consent on behalf of org. Do NOT use /adminconsent (only pre-registered permissions).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Troubleshooting/Dynamic Consent. AADSTS90094 or AADSTS90095 error

symptom: AADSTS50000: Error issuing a token when regex parse fails during claims transformation using RegexReplace in SAML claims customization.
rootCause: Regex expression in claims transformation failed to parse or match input. Pattern invalid or doesn't match source attribute value.
solution: Verify regex pattern valid. Check test input matches regex. Ensure input parameters unique and all group names in replacement pattern have sources. Check source attribute populated on user object.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Migration Features Update

symptom: TraceCode:25729 - Group Transform overage limit hit, groups not emitted. Or TraceCode:25721 - Multiple Custom Groups Claims not permitted.
rootCause: User has 1000+ group memberships causing overage. Or more than one custom groups claim configured via claims mapping policy.
solution: For overage: reduce group membership or use group filtering. For multiple claims: ensure only one custom claim sourced from groups. Token group limits still apply.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Migration Features Update

symptom: TraceCode:29934 - Multi-value transform limited by token size, only first value transformed. Or TraceCode:34016 - Claims transformation failed with multiple multi-valued inputs.
rootCause: JWTs on implicit grant have limited token size. Join transformations cannot have multiple multi-valued inputs (no matrix operations).
solution: For token size: use auth code flow instead of implicit. For multi-multi join: ensure only one input attribute marked as multi-valued.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Migration Features Update

symptom: NameID appears as persistentId after Join() transform instead of expected format.
rootCause: Either both source attributes not populated on user object, or transform resulted in illegal NameId (email format but domain not in verified domains).
solution: Check both source attributes populated via Graph Explorer. Verify domain portion of resulting NameID is a verified domain in tenant.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Migration Features Update

symptom: AADSTS70001: Application {GUID} is disabled. Users cannot sign in to app disabled by Microsoft due to malicious activity detection.
rootCause: Microsoft Identity Protection detected app as malicious. disabledByMicrosoftStatus set to DisabledDueToViolationOfServicesAgreement. Protection only in Public clouds.
solution: File IcM: Identity Protection Service > Identity Reputation Management and Abuse Prevention > Triage. Check disabledByMicrosoftStatus via Graph. Disabled apps cannot be deleted. Not available in sovereign clouds.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD Malicious OAuth App Disablement

symptom: Application deactivated (isDisabled=true): cannot access protected resources or obtain new tokens. Changes may take up to 60 minutes.
rootCause: App registration isDisabled set to true via Entra portal, Graph API, or App lifecycle manager. Three disable mechanisms: accountEnabled (SP), disabledByMicrosoftStatus (Microsoft), isDisabled (owner).
solution: Re-enable: PATCH /beta/myorganization/applications/{appObjectId} with {isDisabled:false}. Or use Entra portal. Requires Application.ReadWrite.All. For lifecycle manager issues: verify owner consent, check medenia trace logs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Application Deactivation (Disablement)

symptom: Graph API $select parameter returns no data on /groups navigation property endpoints (e.g. /groups/{id}/members?$select=displAYname) when property name casing doesn't match CSDL schema
rootCause: For navigation properties typed as directoryObject, properties are dynamic per OData spec and forwarded as-is to backend (case-sensitive). Only properties defined in the entity schema (e.g. on user/group directly) are case-insensitive.
solution: Use exact CSDL-defined casing for $select on navigation properties (e.g. 'displayName' not 'displAYname'). This affects /groups/{id}/members, /users/{id}/memberOf, /applications/{id}/owners and similar navigation properties.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: HTTP 403 Access Denied when adding guest user to M365 group via Graph API. MSODS log shows 'Group does not allow guests in group' and 'Guests users are not allowed to join this Unified Group due to policy setting'
rootCause: AllowToAddGuests group setting is set to false either at tenant level (directory settings) or at the specific group level, blocking guest additions regardless of API permissions.
solution: 1) Check tenant-level directory settings: GET group settings and inspect AllowToAddGuests value. 2) If tenant-level is true, check group-level setting. 3) Update setting via Graph: follow MS Learn docs for reading/updating directory settings at tenant or group level. Use PowerShell Connect-MgGraph or Graph API PATCH to set AllowToAddGuests=true.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: 422 MissingPrefixSuffix error when creating M365 group from My Groups Portal (myaccount.microsoft.com/groups) when naming policy prefix contains / character (e.g. department 'ITE/ITO')
rootCause: directoryObject/validateProperties API strips / character from mailNickname during validation, causing mismatch with the group naming policy prefix. Bug under investigation - docs list invalid chars but / is not included.
solution: Workaround: Create M365 groups via Outlook Desktop Application or Outlook Web Access instead of My Groups Portal. Investigation ongoing: Bug 2840960 tracking whether docs need update or code needs fix. Related CRI: ICM 469840735.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: Group overview page in Azure portal shows incorrect member count for extremely large groups. Graph /groups/{id}/members/$count returns 503 Service Unavailable.
rootCause: Backend service limitation: Mezzo service times out when processing membership count queries for extremely large groups. Error: 'DirectoryServiceUnavailableException: Timeout getting response from Mezzo'. Cross-team work item in progress.
solution: Use PowerShell to enumerate and count members: Connect-MgGraph -Scopes 'Directory.Read.All'; $members = Get-MgGroupMember -GroupId {id} -All -Select displayName,mail,userType; $members.Count. May take several minutes for very large groups. Related ICM: 457793643.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: Directory_ExpiredPageToken error when using skipToken from Graph /groups?$filter=assignedLicenses/any(x:x/skuId eq {guid}) query. Token expires immediately after first page.
rootCause: Known bug in Graph/directory backend. Tracked at Bug 1728776.
solution: No workaround currently available. Bug 1728776 is tracking the fix. Consider using alternative approaches: enumerate all groups then filter client-side, or use PowerShell Get-MgGroup with -Filter.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: Graph v1.0 /groups/{id}/transitiveMembers and /groups/{id}/owners endpoints do not return Service Principal objects in results
rootCause: Service Principal support not yet rolled out to Graph v1.0 endpoint for these APIs. Epic 3277234 tracking the work. As of Dec 2025 no ETA for List Owners; transitiveMembers also affected.
solution: Use Graph beta endpoint instead of v1.0 to get Service Principals in transitiveMembers/owners results. Alternative for transitiveMembers: manually enumerate nested groups using /groups/{id}/members for each group. Related: Epic 3277234, CRI ICM 649954905.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: Non-admin group owner gets HTTP 403 when calling Graph /groups/{id}/members if the group has another group as a nested member
rootCause: Group owner has permission to read direct members but lacks permission to read nested child group objects. The API fails when it encounters a member object (nested group) the caller can't access.
solution: Grant owner additional directory read permissions (e.g. Directory.Read.All), or use admin-level credentials for the API call. Reference: Troubleshooting 403 incidents TSG at eng.ms. ICM: 424578693.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/TSG: 403 returned by Groups Graph API

symptom: Internal server error (500) when PATCH-ing group settings DefaultClassification value via Graph API. MSODS shows KeyNotFoundException in ValidateAndTrimDefaultClassification.
rootCause: ValidateAndTrimDefaultClassification function validates the PATCH-ed DefaultClassification value against the ClassificationList. If the value is not present in ClassificationList, it throws KeyNotFoundException causing 500 error.
solution: When setting DefaultClassification, ensure the value exists in ClassificationList. Set both DefaultClassification and ClassificationList together in the same PATCH request body to avoid validation failure.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/TSG - Failed to set groupSetting DefaultClassification value

symptom: Privileged admin roles (Helpdesk Admin, Authentication Admin) cannot perform sensitive actions (reset password, revoke tokens) on users who are members/owners of role-assignable groups
rootCause: By design security hardening: members of role-assignable groups are treated as protected principals. Even non-admin users become protected when added to a role-assignable group. Only Global Administrators can perform sensitive actions on these users.
solution: Use Global Administrator role to perform the action. This is by design per MS Learn: 'Who can perform sensitive actions' documentation. Consider removing user from role-assignable group if admin operations are needed by lower-privilege admins.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: Group-based licensing (GBL) fails with 'Conflicting service plans' error when assigning E5 license to group while users already have E3 license (direct or from another group)
rootCause: Service plans Exchange Online Plan 2, SharePoint Online Plan 2, Skype for Business Online Plan 2 cannot coexist from different SKUs (E3 and E5 both include them). Azure AD will not auto-deconflict.
solution: 1) Disable conflicting service plans on E3 direct assignment before applying E5 via group. 2) Or modify group E5 license assignment to disable overlapping plans. 3) Or remove E3 license if E5 is superset. Use ASC Tenant Explorer > Audit Logs with filter Activity='Set group license', Result='Failure' to diagnose. Check violation details with MSODS Kusto query filtering tagId=='9ln1' and message has 'violation'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Azure AD Group Based Licensing

symptom: License seats not released after on-premises synced group with GBL is removed from sync scope and deleted from cloud. License count remains consumed.
rootCause: When synced group is deleted, the DeletingLicensedGroupNotAllowed protection may be bypassed in certain scenarios. After group deletion, the GBL processing doesn't properly release the license seat count.
solution: Reprocess individual users via Entra portal (small scale) or use Invoke-MgLicenseUser cmdlet for bulk reprocessing. PowerShell scripting recommended for larger groups of affected users.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Azure AD Group Based Licensing

symptom: Graph API select parameter returns no data on /groups navigation property endpoints when property name casing does not match CSDL schema
rootCause: For navigation properties typed as directoryObject, properties are dynamic per OData spec and forwarded as-is to backend (case-sensitive). Only schema-defined properties are case-insensitive.
solution: Use exact CSDL-defined casing for select on navigation properties. Affects /groups/{id}/members, /users/{id}/memberOf, /applications/{id}/owners.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: HTTP 403 Access Denied adding guest user to M365 group via Graph API. MSODS shows Group does not allow guests in group
rootCause: AllowToAddGuests policy set to false at tenant or group level blocks guest additions regardless of API permissions.
solution: Check and update AllowToAddGuests setting at directory or group level via Graph API or PowerShell.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: 422 MissingPrefixSuffix error creating M365 group from My Groups Portal when naming policy prefix contains slash character
rootCause: validateProperties API strips slash from mailNickname during validation, causing mismatch with naming policy. Bug 2840960.
solution: Create M365 groups via Outlook Desktop or OWA instead of My Groups Portal. CRI: ICM 469840735.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: Group overview page shows wrong member count for extremely large groups. Graph members count returns 503.
rootCause: Backend Mezzo service times out for large group membership count queries.
solution: Use PowerShell: Get-MgGroupMember -GroupId {id} -All then check Count. ICM: 457793643.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: Directory_ExpiredPageToken error when using skipToken from groups filter assignedLicenses query. Token expires immediately.
rootCause: Known bug in directory backend pagination. Bug 1728776.
solution: No workaround. Bug 1728776 tracking fix. Alternative: enumerate all groups then filter client-side.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: Graph v1.0 transitiveMembers and owners endpoints do not return Service Principal objects
rootCause: SP support not rolled out to v1.0. Epic 3277234. No ETA as of Dec 2025.
solution: Use beta endpoint. For transitiveMembers: enumerate nested groups manually. ICM 649954905.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: Non-admin group owner gets 403 listing members if group contains nested group as member
rootCause: Owner lacks permission to read nested child group objects.
solution: Grant Directory.Read.All or use admin credentials. ICM: 424578693.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/TSG: 403 returned by Groups Graph API

symptom: 500 error PATCH-ing group settings DefaultClassification value. MSODS: KeyNotFoundException in ValidateAndTrimDefaultClassification.
rootCause: ValidateAndTrimDefaultClassification checks value against ClassificationList. Missing value throws KeyNotFoundException.
solution: Set DefaultClassification and ClassificationList together in same PATCH request body.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/TSG - Failed to set groupSetting DefaultClassification value

symptom: Privileged admin roles (Helpdesk/Auth Admin) cannot reset password or revoke tokens for members of role-assignable groups
rootCause: By design: members of role-assignable groups are protected principals. Only Global Admin can act.
solution: Use Global Administrator. By design per MS Learn sensitive actions docs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: GBL fails with Conflicting service plans error when E5 assigned to group while users have E3
rootCause: Service plans from E3 and E5 (Exchange/SharePoint/Skype Plan 2) cannot coexist. Azure AD will not auto-deconflict.
solution: Disable conflicting plans on E3 before applying E5, or modify E5 group assignment, or remove E3. Diagnose via ASC Audit Logs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Azure AD Group Based Licensing

symptom: License seats not released after on-prem synced group with GBL removed from sync scope and deleted
rootCause: Deletion bypasses DeletingLicensedGroupNotAllowed protection. GBL does not release license count.
solution: Reprocess users via Entra portal or Invoke-MgLicenseUser cmdlet for bulk reprocessing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Azure AD Group Based Licensing

symptom: User account locked out when signing in to Microsoft Entra Domain Services (MEDS) managed domain
rootCause: 5 bad password attempts within 2 minutes triggers automatic account lockout for 30 minutes. No manual unlock mechanism available.
solution: Wait 30 minutes for automatic unlock. Investigate source of bad password attempts. See TSG: Azure AD Domain Services Account Lockout Troubleshooting.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Services

symptom: SamAccountName in Microsoft Entra Domain Services has random GUID characters appended (e.g. CN=John Doe (0eFEDF225))
rootCause: SamAccountName is based on MailNickname property. When MailNickname is >20 chars or conflicts with another user's SamAccountName, sync agent appends 11-char GUID suffix. Common with guest users sharing same MailNickname.
solution: 1) Find conflicting users via Get-EntraUser filtering by mailNickname. 2) Temporarily change both users' MailNicknames to unique values. 3) Wait for sync to MEDS. 4) Change desired user's MailNickname back to original. 5) Verify SamAccountName is correct after 5-10 min sync. For >20 char issue: shorten MailNickname to <20 chars.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Services

symptom: Cloud-only user unable to sign in to Microsoft Entra Domain Services managed domain after enabling MEDS
rootCause: Password hash not available in MEDS. Cloud-only users must change their password after MEDS is enabled (or re-enabled) so that the password hash is synced to MEDS.
solution: Have user change their password in Entra ID, wait 15 minutes, then retry login. Use UPN format (user@domain.com) instead of SAMAccountName format (DOMAIN\user). If issue persists after password change, escalate via ICM with Tenant ID, Domain Name, and username.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Services

symptom: External/guest user cannot sign in to Microsoft Entra Domain Services managed domain
rootCause: MEDS does not have credentials for external user accounts. External users authenticating to MEDS is not a supported scenario.
solution: Inform customer this is not supported. External users cannot sign in to the managed domain. Only internal member users (cloud-only or synced) are supported.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Services

symptom: Customer reports being billed for Microsoft Entra Domain Services after disabling the service
rootCause: MEDS may still be active in another tenant linked to the same billing subscription. MEDS billing is tied to the subscription owning the VNet, and a co-admin could have enabled MEDS in a separate Entra tenant using that subscription's VNet.
solution: 1) Get billing subscription ID. 2) Use ACIS (AD Fabric DCAAS ARM PROD → GetActiveTenants) to find active MEDS deployments. 3) If found, customer must sign in as subscription admin to disable MEDS. 4) If subscription expired and customer can't access, open ICM to PG to disable/delete deployment from backend.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra Domain Services

symptom: Workforce login page displayed instead of CIAM/External ID sign-up page (shows 'Can't access your account?' instead of 'No account? Create one')
rootCause: Replication delay for the user flow configuration. User flow data not yet replicated to all ESTS nodes.
solution: Refresh browser after 1-2 minutes. Verify user flow is linked to the application matching the client ID in the login URL. Check app registration and user flow configuration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra External ID (CIAM)/Entra External ID Troubleshooting

symptom: AADSTS50011: Redirect URI mismatch when running CIAM sample app - 'The redirect URI specified in the request does not match the redirect URIs configured for the application'
rootCause: Replication delay updating the redirect URI in app registration, or multiple samples downloaded in same wizard run overriding each other's redirect URIs.
solution: 1) Wait a few minutes and retry. 2) Verify redirect URI in app registration matches the one in error. 3) If using wizard, exit/restart between sample runs to use separate app registrations.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra External ID (CIAM)/Entra External ID Troubleshooting

symptom: AADSTS7000215: Invalid client secret provided when running ASP.NET Core CIAM sample
rootCause: Replication delay updating the client secret in app registration, or multiple samples in same wizard run creating conflicting secrets.
solution: Wait a few minutes and retry (may need up to next day in some cases). Verify client secret exists in app registration and matches the app configuration value.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra External ID (CIAM)/Entra External ID Troubleshooting

symptom: Unable to delete CIAM/External ID tenant - deletion fails even after removing all resources
rootCause: The b2c-extensions-app was not deleted. This app is hidden under 'All applications' and users skip it because the name says 'do not modify'.
solution: Delete the b2c-extensions-app found under 'All applications' in the Entra portal, then retry tenant deletion. Note: user flows deletion checklist never shows green tick even after all flows deleted - this can be ignored.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra External ID (CIAM)/Entra External ID Troubleshooting

symptom: CIAM tenant deleted but Azure resource stuck - cannot remove resource from subscription, redeployment with same domain name fails with conflict
rootCause: Product defect: when following official deletion docs, tenant is erased before the resource on subscription side can send its cleanup signal, leaving an orphaned ARM resource.
solution: 1) Confirm tenant is deleted in backend (use DSXplorer, not CMAT). 2) Request ARM EEE team to delete lingering resource (ICM to Support/EEE ARM with resource URI). 3) After deletion, have customer delete from portal UI if still visible. 4) Wait a few hours before redeployment. Workaround: Delete Azure resource from subscription BEFORE deleting the tenant via Entra portal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra External ID (CIAM)/Entra External ID Troubleshooting

symptom: JWT decode error after July 2025: 'Signed JWT rejected: Another algorithm expected, or no matching key(s) found' when authenticating through Entra External ID
rootCause: Application is using login.microsoftonline.com openid-configuration endpoint instead of ciamlogin.com. Starting July 2025, PG introduced distinct signing keys for ciamlogin.com, breaking apps using the wrong OIDC discovery endpoint.
solution: Configure the application to use https://{tenant}.ciamlogin.com/{TENANT-ID}/.well-known/openid-configuration instead of https://login.microsoftonline.com/{TENANT-ID}/.well-known/openid-configuration. See: Authority URL in OpenID Connect and OAuth2 flows docs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra External ID (CIAM)/Entra External ID Troubleshooting

symptom: Disable sign-up feature not working correctly when home tenant has Alt Login ID enabled - local account guest users redirected to federated login
rootCause: Bug 3043172: When signup is disabled in user flow AND home tenant has Alternative Login ID enabled, ESTS incorrectly redirects local account guest users to federated login instead of showing sign-in only.
solution: Known PG bug (Epic 2678087 / Bug 3043172). Escalate via ICM to CPIM/CIAM-CRI-Triage. No customer-side workaround available.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra External ID (CIAM)

symptom: Email OTP users cannot sign in and see blank screen when Identity Protection MFA registration policy is enabled on Entra External ID tenant
rootCause: Identity Protection should NOT be enabled for EEID tenants. When MFA authentication registration policy is enabled, Email/password users get silently registered for MFA, but Email OTP users fail with blank screen.
solution: Disable Identity Protection MFA registration policy on the EEID tenant. Identity Protection is not supported for External ID tenants.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Microsoft Entra External ID (CIAM)

symptom: SuccessFactors to AD provisioning fails with 'Could not calculate the distinguished name' for new users without displayName populated
rootCause: Default CN mapping uses displayName from SuccessFactors. Many users do not populate displayName, relying on firstName/lastName instead, causing DN calculation failure.
solution: Change CN attribute mapping from displayName to expression: Join(" ",[firstName],[lastName]). Also verify the default OU provided in app config is valid.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/SyncFabric/Inbound provisioning (HR)/SuccessFactors support case guidance

symptom: SuccessFactors custom entity attributes (e.g. BadgeInfoNav) cannot be retrieved via JSONPath in provisioning attribute mappings
rootCause: Provisioning does not support expansion of custom entities (navigation entities) as every expansion is costly. Only entities documented in the integration reference are expanded.
solution: Ask customer to: 1) Map desired attribute value to a custom property in userNav or employmentNav using SAP HRIS sync. 2) Then sync the custom attribute to AD/Entra ID via standard mapping.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/SyncFabric/Inbound provisioning (HR)/SuccessFactors support case guidance

symptom: SuccessFactors Writeback fails with SuccessFactorsWritebackRequiredPropertiesMissing: Required property status is missing
rootCause: In SuccessFactors, personIdExternal and userId may have different values. Default API call uses personIdExternal, causing failure when values differ.
solution: Ask customer to incorporate userId attribute value during writeback as documented: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/app-provisioning/sap-successfactors-integration-reference#enabling-writeback-with-userid
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/SyncFabric/Inbound provisioning (HR)/SuccessFactors support case guidance

symptom: Certain Workday attribute values (e.g. businessTitle) not syncing to AD/Azure AD despite other attributes flowing correctly
rootCause: Workday Web Services (WWS) version mismatch or incorrect XPATHs. Customer may be using WWS v21.1 with modified XPATHs incompatible with their tenant version.
solution: 1) Check WWS version via Kusto: cluster('idsharedwus.kusto.windows.net').database('AADSFProd') GlobalIfxAuditEvent where eventName == 'WorkdayAuditEventVersionSelection'. 2) Extract attribute mapping schema for the execution. 3) Verify XPATHs are compatible with the WWS version in use.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/SyncFabric/Inbound provisioning (HR)/Workday support case guidance

symptom: DuplicateTargetEntries error during Workday worker conversion (CW to FTE or vice versa): two Workday entries matching the same AD entry
rootCause: When CW converts to FTE retaining the same WorkerID, provisioning engine gets two user profiles with same WorkerID but different WID (anchor), breaking one-to-one mapping. Note: post 5/5/2021 fix, active worker profile auto-assumes ownership.
solution: Post-2021: active worker auto-assumes ownership. If still occurring: Option 1: Assign different WorkerIDs for CW/FTE. Option 2: Set Skip-Out-of-Scope-Deletions=true, exclude inactive worker via WID scoping filter, clear metadata and restart via Graph API.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/SyncFabric/Inbound provisioning (HR)/Workday support case guidance

symptom: HR to AD provisioning fails with InsufficientAccessRights error (DSID-03150F94, problem 4003) for some users while working for most
rootCause: Two causes: 1) Target OU does not inherit domain-level permissions. 2) User belongs to protected AD group governed by AdminSDHolder - SDProp resets permissions every 60 minutes.
solution: Cause 1: Enable inheritance on OU (AD Users & Computers > OU Properties > Security > Advanced > Enable Inheritance). Cause 2: Option 1: Remove user from protected group. Option 2: Modify AdminSDHolder permissions via DSACLS. Option 3: Grant Full Control to provAgentgMSA$. Option 4: Skip GMSA, use manual service account (temporary).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/SyncFabric/Inbound provisioning (HR)/Common HR support case guidance

symptom: HR to Azure AD provisioning fails with AzureActiveDirectoryCannotUpdateObjectsOriginatedInExternalService when updating extensionAttributes
rootCause: extensionAttributes (1-15) in Azure AD can originate from on-prem Exchange, Exchange Online/M365 Admin Center, or Azure AD. Updates fail by design if attributes were originally set from Exchange sources.
solution: Check if impacted users were originally created from external source. If yes: manually update extension attributes, OR create separate provisioning job that omits extension attributes mapping for affected users.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/SyncFabric/Inbound provisioning (HR)/Common HR support case guidance

symptom: Test connection fails after provisioning agent installation/upgrade with HybridSynchronizationActiveDirectoryCredentialValidationUnavailable
rootCause: Three causes: 1) Agent installed but GMSA wizard setup not completed. 2) Agent configured but Azure AD not receiving bootstrap signals. 3) Upgraded from non-GMSA agent (<v1.1.281.0) and GMSA setup failing.
solution: Check 'View on-premises agents' link: active = rule out causes 1&2. Unhealthy/missing = reconfigure agent, verify bootstrap signals via Kusto. For upgraded agents: set registry to skip GMSA configuration and reconfigure.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/SyncFabric/Inbound provisioning (HR)/Common HR support case guidance

symptom: Installed provisioning agents not showing up in Azure Portal - only some agents visible
rootCause: Each agent sends bootstrap signals to Azure AD. If agent cannot send signals (service down/network issue), Azure AD removes it from the healthy agents list.
solution: Check last bootstrap via Kusto: cluster('idsharedwus.kusto.windows.net').database('AADHIS') BootstrapRootOperationEvent where agentFeature == 'Sync Fabric' summarize arg_max(env_time,*) by connectorId. For network issues: configure proxy in AADConnectProvisioningAgent.exe.config. Reinstall/reconfigure unhealthy agents.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/SyncFabric/Inbound provisioning (HR)/Common HR support case guidance

symptom: Provision-on-demand shows 'Encountered Exception' with no details when testing HR provisioning for certain users
rootCause: Occurs when the user's manager is in scope of the provisioning job. This is a UX bug specific to provision-on-demand, not the backend sync. User is actually provisioned successfully.
solution: Ignore the 'Encountered Exception' message. Verify actual provisioning status via Provisioning Logs / Audit Logs where user creation/update shows as successful.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/SyncFabric/Inbound provisioning (HR)/Common HR support case guidance

symptom: Azure AD Connect Provisioning Agent service fails to start on the server
rootCause: Group Policy prevents the local NT Service Logon Account (NT SERVICE\AADConnectProvisioningAgent) from having permissions to start the service.
solution: Open Services editor, change the Logon Account for provisioning agent service to a domain admin account, restart the service.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/SyncFabric/Inbound provisioning (HR)/Azure AD HR Provisioning

symptom: Provisioning Agent registration fails with Timeout error during wizard setup
rootCause: Agent unable to connect to Hybrid Identity Service. HTTP proxy required for outbound communication.
solution: Configure HTTP proxy in AADConnectProvisioningAgent.exe.config: add <system.net> section with <defaultProxy enabled='true'> and proxy address before closing </configuration> tag.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/SyncFabric/Inbound provisioning (HR)/Azure AD HR Provisioning

symptom: Provisioning Agent registration fails with Security Error during wizard setup
rootCause: Agent cannot execute PowerShell registration scripts due to restrictive execution policies. Occurs when policy is set to Unrestricted.
solution: Fix via gpedit.msc: Computer/User Configuration > Administrative Templates > Windows Components > Windows PowerShell > Turn on Script Execution = 'Allow local Scripts and remote signed scripts' or Undefined. Also check registry HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\PowerShell\1\ShellIds > ExecutionPolicy = AllSigned. Rerun wizard.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/SyncFabric/Inbound provisioning (HR)/Azure AD HR Provisioning

symptom: Provisioning Agent fails with LdapException: The operation was aborted because the client side timeout limit was exceeded
rootCause: Provisioning Agent unable to get timely responses from AD domain controller, exceeding default LDAP connection timeout.
solution: Increase timeout via registry: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Azure AD Connect Agents\Azure AD Connect Provisioning Agent > New String Value 'LdapConnectionTimeoutInMilliseconds' = '60000'. Restart Provisioning Agent Service.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/SyncFabric/Inbound provisioning (HR)/Azure AD HR Provisioning

symptom: Workday attribute mapping fails with SchemaInvalid error due to unsupported XPATH function (e.g. sibling::node())
rootCause: Provisioning engine supports limited XPATH/XSLT functions. Functions like sibling::node() are not in the supported set.
solution: Replace unsupported XPATH functions. Supported: Name, last, Position, String, Substring, Concat, substring-after, starts-with, string-length, Contains, Translate, normalize-space, substring-before, Boolean, True, Not, False, Number, Ceiling, Sum, Round, Floor.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Sync Provisioning/SyncFabric/Inbound provisioning (HR)/Azure AD HR Provisioning

symptom: RDP reconnect to AADJ machine shows 'username or password incorrect' error
rootCause: PKU2U credential provider incorrectly maps user account from client during reconnect (Windows Bug 31608330).
solution: Use AzureAD\user@contoso.com format when connecting instead of just UPN.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Device Registration/Window Devices/Microsoft Entra Join/Troubleshooting RDP to Azure AD Joined machines

symptom: $select parameter in Graph /groups endpoints is case sensitive for dynamic properties on directoryObject navigation properties
rootCause: Properties NOT defined in schema are dynamic per OData spec and forwarded as-is to RDS without case normalization
solution: Use exact CSDL-defined casing for property names. Schema-defined properties are case-insensitive; dynamic properties on directoryObject are not.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: HTTP 403 AccessDenied when adding guest user to M365 group via Graph API
rootCause: Group-level or tenant-level AllowToAddGuests setting is False
solution: Check tenant-level Group.Unified directory settings for AllowToAddGuests. If True at tenant level, check group-level settings. Update via Graph PowerShell.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: Cannot change proxyAddresses on a cloud-only group - EXO changes not synced to AAD
rootCause: Group mastered in AAD (ExchangeMastered=false); no public API to modify proxyAddresses for groups in AAD
solution: For on-prem synced groups: modify in on-premises AD. For cloud-only: no workaround other than recreating the group.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: Cannot delete last user owner from security group even when SP owner exists
rootCause: Owner removal check only validates user owners, not SP owners (by design as of Oct 2024)
solution: Add a second user owner then remove both. Or create temp user as owner, remove original, delete temp.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: Graph API cannot return group members for HiddenMembership groups even with correct permissions
rootCause: Group visibility is HiddenMembership - non-members cannot see members regardless of standard Graph permissions
solution: Check group visibility. Only admin roles (global/company/user/helpdesk) can view HiddenMembership group members.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: Privileged admin (Helpdesk Admin) cannot reset password or revoke tokens for users in role-assignable groups
rootCause: By design: members of role-assignable groups are protected from sensitive actions by non-GA admins
solution: Use Global Admin. See docs: Who can perform sensitive actions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: directoryObject/validateProperties returns 422 when group naming policy prefix/suffix contains / character
rootCause: The / in mailNickname is stripped during validation causing mismatch with naming policy
solution: Create M365 Groups through Outlook Desktop or OWA instead of My Groups Portal. Bug under investigation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: Member count in group overview incorrect for extremely large groups - 503 error
rootCause: Backend Mezzo service timeout counting members of very large groups
solution: Use PowerShell: Get-MgGroupMember -GroupId {id} -All then $members.Count.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: Directory_ExpiredPageToken when listing groups with assignedLicenses filter using skipToken
rootCause: Bug: skipToken expires immediately after first page with assignedLicenses filter
solution: No workaround. Bug tracked at IdentityDivision Bug 1728776.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: Graph transitiveMembers endpoint does not return Service Principals on v1.0
rootCause: Known limitation: v1.0 does not include SPs in transitiveMembers results
solution: Use beta endpoint or manually enumerate nested groups calling groups/{id}/members for each.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: List Group Owners API (v1.0) does not return service principals
rootCause: Staged rollout of SP support to v1.0 not complete. No ETA as of Dec 2025.
solution: Use beta endpoint. Epic 3277234 tracking v1.0 support.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: Regular users unable to read group expiration policies via Entra portal UI
rootCause: Bug: code requires admin role to read expiration policies even for member users
solution: PG fix in progress. WorkItem 3408706.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Known issues and By design behavior

symptom: Group owner gets 403 listing members when group contains nested group as member
rootCause: Owner lacks permission to read nested group object (directoryObject) causing 403 on entire members list
solution: Owner needs admin role or permission on nested group. Ref: Troubleshooting 403 Access Denied Incidents.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/TSG: 403 returned by Groups Graph API

symptom: 500 Internal Server Error when PATCHing DefaultClassification in group settings
rootCause: ValidateAndTrimDefaultClassification throws KeyNotFoundException when value not in ClassificationList
solution: Set DefaultClassification and ClassificationList together in one PATCH call.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/TSG - Failed to set groupSetting DefaultClassification value

symptom: Sensitivity labels cannot be changed after applied to Entra Security Group - must recreate group
rootCause: Known limitation: label change on Security Groups not yet supported
solution: Recreate group with desired label. Also: labels cannot be applied to Mail Enabled or Dynamic Security Groups.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Groups Graph API/Entra Sensitivity Labels for Entra Security Groups and Microsoft 365 Groups

symptom: Groups removed from expiration policy still have expirationDate populated
rootCause: Bug: removing policy does not clear residual expirationDate (Bug 2315818)
solution: Group will NOT be deleted. To clear: re-add to policy, wait 1 day, remove.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/SSGM - Self Service Group Management/Known issues and by design behavior

symptom: Roles assigned via role-assignable group do not work for dynamic rule validation or expiration policy editing
rootCause: Indirect role assignment through dynamic membership groups not supported for sensitive operations
solution: Assign roles directly to users. Epic 3002938.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/SSGM - Self Service Group Management/Known issues and by design behavior

symptom: M365 groups auto-renewed despite no Office activities (historical 2021 bug)
rootCause: ShouldGroupBeAutoRenewed flag not reset after renewal during 2021 window
solution: Bug fixed. Affected groups auto-renew one more cycle then stop.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/SSGM - Self Service Group Management/Known issues and by design behavior

symptom: Groups not deleted after expiry - expiration notification emails missed
rootCause: Intermittent: some 30d/15d/1d notifications not sent; without all 3 group not deleted, extended 35 days
solution: Groups safe from accidental deletion. Extended groups can be manually deleted. PG adding logging PBI 2701451.

symptom: Removing sensitivity label from group does not revert AllowToAddGuests - guests still blocked
rootCause: By design: Groups team does not maintain pre-label state for AllowToAddGuests
solution: Manually set AllowToAddGuests=True via PowerShell per MS docs per-group-guest-access.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/SSGM - Self Service Group Management/Known issues and by design behavior

symptom: Custom role cannot create Unified Group within Administrative Unit
rootCause: Bug: only built-in roles (Global Admin, Groups Admin) work for AU-scoped Unified Group creation
solution: Use built-in roles. Bug 2923321.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/SSGM - Self Service Group Management/Known issues and by design behavior

symptom: Script to enable/disable M365 group creation sometimes fails - GroupCreationAllowedGroupId not updated
rootCause: Script cannot resolve group ObjectId from group name in some environments
solution: Paste commands directly in PowerShell using group ObjectID instead of name.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/SSGM - Self Service Group Management/Known issues and by design behavior

symptom: Last user owner on Role Assignable Group CAN be removed (should be prevented)

symptom: Targeted group expiration policy incorrectly reports 500 groups - deleted groups count against limit
rootCause: Deleted targeted groups still count against 500-group limit after hard deletion
solution: Turn off policy, wait 24 hours, re-enable and re-select groups. Bug 3086395.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/SSGM - Self Service Group Management/Known issues and by design behavior

symptom: Role Assignable Groups ignore expiration policies entirely
rootCause: Bug: expiration policies cannot manage role-assignable groups (Bug 2292676)
solution: No workaround. RAGs will not expire.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/SSGM - Self Service Group Management/Known issues and by design behavior

symptom: Join policy change from My Groups portal not applied to Unified Groups - reverts on refresh
rootCause: Code bug: restrict-to-owner-only option fails silently for Unified Groups
solution: No workaround. Bug 3269726.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/SSGM - Self Service Group Management/Known issues and by design behavior

symptom: Join policies only changeable via My Groups portal not through Graph API
rootCause: One-way sync: My Groups writes visibility to Entra but Graph changes do not update join policy
solution: Use My Groups portal individually. No bulk Graph update available.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/SSGM - Self Service Group Management/Known issues and by design behavior

symptom: Security Groups can incorrectly get expirationDate set enabling deletion by expiration policy
rootCause: Bug: expirationDate can be set on security groups (deleted security groups currently not restorable)
solution: Critical risk. Bug 3331094.

symptom: Cloud-only group in restricted management AU cannot be edited even by Global Admin
rootCause: By design: IsManagementRestricted=True requires role scoped to specific AU
solution: Remove from AU or assign role scoped to the restricted AU.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/SSGM - Self Service Group Management/Known issues and by design behavior

symptom: Intune fails to migrate soft-deleted group membership during device state change
rootCause: Device migration (Autopilot/Hybrid Join/WPJ) copies memberships but skips soft-deleted group memberships
solution: Known limitation. Soft-deleted group memberships not migrated to new device object.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/SSGM - Self Service Group Management/Cloud Security Groups Soft Deletion and Restoration

symptom: AD Connect Health Service reflects status as unhealthy; Connectivity Test fails with 401 Unauthorized / 'Invalid credentials' error when uploading blob data to adhybridhealth.azure.com
rootCause: Required endpoints (login.microsoftonline.com or login.windows.net) are blocked on the AD Connect machine over port 5671 or 443, preventing the agent from obtaining an access token
solution: 1) Restart 'Azure AD Connect Health Sync Insights Service' and 'Azure AD Connect Health Monitoring Service'; 2) From elevated PowerShell, run Register-AzureADConnectHealthSyncAgent to re-register the agent; 3) Verify connectivity with Test-AzureADConnectHealthConnectivity -Role Sync. Ensure all required endpoints listed in the public docs are reachable.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/AAD Connect - Synchronization/AD Connect Health Agent Connectivity Troubleshooting

symptom: Entra Connect upgrade fails at 'Connect to Microsoft Entra' step with MSAL error: 'authentication_ui_failed - The browser-based authentication dialog failed to complete. The server or proxy was not found'
rootCause: PKCS registry key exists under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\KeyExchangeAlgorithms\PKCS with Enabled=0 or ClientMinKeyBitLength value, causing a TLS fatal error (Schannel Event ID 36871, internal error state 10011) during authentication
solution: 1) Back up the registry; 2) Delete the 'PKCS' key at HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\SCHANNEL\KeyExchangeAlgorithms\PKCS; 3) Verify Cryptographic Services is running (services.msc); 4) Ensure 'System Cryptography: Use FIPS compliant algorithms' group policy is disabled; 5) Retry the upgrade
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/AAD Connect - Synchronization/Entra Connect Upgrade error: Unable to validate credentials due to an unexpected error

symptom: Mobile phone attribute for an AAD user does not get updated via AD Connect sync even though attribute changes are reported as successful by AD Connect
rootCause: When an admin uses MsOnline/AzureAD PowerShell or the user updates mobile in MOP, Azure AD sets DirSyncOverrides=Mobile(1) on the object. From that point, any on-premises update to the Mobile attribute is ignored by Azure AD as it is considered cloud-managed
solution: Use the BypassDirSyncOverrides feature (released Nov 2022) to turn off DirSyncOverrides at the tenant level and allow on-premises mobile attribute to sync again. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/hybrid/how-to-bypassdirsyncoverrides
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/AAD Connect - Synchronization/Mobile phone attribute does not get updated to AAD Object via AD Connect

symptom: Password writeback service down after previously working; unable to enable/reconfigure Password Reset Service in AD Connect wizard; Event ID 31034 in event viewer; Set-ADSyncAADPasswordResetConfiguration fails with error
rootCause: AD Connect is not using TLS 1.2; .NET Framework not configured for strong cryptography; connection to ssprdedicatedsbprodncu.servicebus.windows.net is dropped because the client and server cannot agree on a common algorithm
solution: Add registry keys to force TLS 1.2: under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\.NETFramework\v2.0.50727 and v4.0.30319 set SystemDefaultTlsVersions=dword:1 and SchUseStrongCrypto=dword:1; then restart Microsoft Azure AD Sync Service and verify Password Writeback can be enabled
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/AAD Connect - Synchronization/Password Writeback Service impacted due to TLS 1.2 not being enabled

symptom: AD Connect Sync Service not running after swing migration or new installation; Synchronization Service Manager shows 'stopped-extension-dll-exception'; Event ID 6801: 'Schema could not get attribute reasonFiltered'
rootCause: Group Writeback was previously enabled on the source server (causing Entra ID to return the reasonFiltered attribute in import steps), but the new server does not have the Group Writeback feature enabled so the AAD Connector schema does not include reasonFiltered
solution: Enable the Group Writeback feature on the new/migrated Entra Connect server. This allows the connector to retrieve the reasonFiltered attribute and resolves the stopped-extension-dll-exception error
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/AAD Connect - Synchronization/Sync Service not running at all due to Group Writeback feature misconfiguration

symptom: Azure AD group with a leading space in the displayName (e.g. ' GroupName') cannot be found via RBAC role selector, portal group search, or ObjectId lookup after creation; portal shows the group in the list but without the space
rootCause: Known bug: Azure AD portal and PowerShell both allow a space prefix in group displayName without error or trimming; the stored displayName includes the leading space which causes the group to be unfindable by any search method
solution: This is a known PG issue to be documented publicly. Advise customer not to add unintentional white space prefix to group displayName. Affected groups may need to be deleted and recreated without the space prefix
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Azure AD Account Management/A prefix space in group display name is not supported when creating groups

symptom: Unable to invite an external user via Azure AD B2B; invitation fails due to proxy conflict error
rootCause: ProxyAddress collision between the invited external user's email and (Scenario 1) an existing local user's proxyAddresses, or (Scenario 2) an existing contact object's proxyAddresses in the tenant directory
solution: Scenario 1 (conflict with local user): direct the user to sign in with their existing account. Scenario 2 (conflict with contact object): remove the conflicting email from the contact object's proxyAddresses and delete the associated contact object, then retry the invitation. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/external-identities/troubleshoot
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Azure AD Account Management/Azure AD B2B Invitation failures due to Proxy Conflict

symptom: IT admin sees mass 'Update User' events in Azure AD audit logs with no Actor information, causing confusion about what triggered the bulk user changes
rootCause: Backend ProxyCalc (Proxy Address sanitization) task triggered by VerifiedDomainChange event. Adding/removing a verified domain triggers this async task to ensure all UPNs and ProxyAddresses are consistent; audit logs do not yet display Actor info correlating the user updates to the triggering VerifiedDomainChange event.
solution: Check audit logs for an 'Add verified domain' or 'Remove verified domain' event 5-10 minutes before the mass 'Update User' events — this is the trigger. ProxyCalc-driven updates are by-design behavior ensuring UPN/ProxyAddress consistency across the tenant. No action needed unless actual UPN/proxy address changes are unintended.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Azure AD Account Management/What is ProxyCalc and how does it affect Azure AD users

symptom: Customer unable to add advanced group transformation options or regex rules for Header Based SSO configured on Azure AD Application Proxy applications
rootCause: Engineering bug — group transformation rules for Header Based SSO in App Proxy are not supported through the standard Claims configuration blade; feature is on the engineering roadmap.
solution: Workaround using the Claims (preview) private preview blade: navigate to https://aad.portal.azure.com/?feature.claimeditorpreview=true, go to the App Proxy application > Claims (Preview), add a new claim with conditions/group membership. Prefix the claim Name with 'appproxy_<ClaimName>' so App Proxy sends it as a header. Set expectations with customer about private preview status.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Azure AD Application Proxy/Group Transformation Rule for Header based SSO for Proxy Applications

symptom: Customers unable to determine which applications are associated to a specific Application Proxy Connector Group via Portal or PowerShell
rootCause: Portal and PowerShell do not expose a direct Connector Group to application mapping view. As of April 2021 this is also not available in Graph Explorer via ASC — only the customer can run the required Graph API calls.
solution: Use Microsoft Graph beta API: (1) GET https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/onPremisesPublishingProfiles/applicationProxy/ConnectorGroups to retrieve connector group IDs; (2) GET https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/onPremisesPublishingProfiles/applicationProxy/ConnectorGroups/{id}/applications to list all apps assigned to a specific connector group. Navigate to https://developer.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/graph-explorer and switch to beta version.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Azure AD Application Proxy/How to find which applications are configured to a specific Application Proxy Connector Group

symptom: Expired Active Directory user accounts can still sign in to Azure AD and Office applications (Outlook, Teams) when the environment uses Password Hash Synchronization (PHS)
rootCause: The on-premises accountExpires attribute is NOT synchronized to Azure AD. In PHS, authentication occurs against Azure AD (not on-premises AD), so AD account expiration is invisible to Azure AD — the cloud account remains active even after the on-premises account expires.
solution: Use a scheduled PowerShell script with Set-ADUser cmdlet to disable (not just expire) AD accounts when they expire. When removing the expiration, re-enable the account. For ADFS or PTA environments, authentication happens on-premises so expiration is enforced normally. Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/hybrid/how-to-connect-password-hash-synchronization#account-expiration
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Azure AD Authentication/An expired user on AD still can sign-in to Azure AD or Office Applications if using PHS

symptom: Customers see two unrecognized SIDs (S-1-12-1-2519061733-1113399595-3556733861-1346131856 and S-1-12-1-3326773636-1284407173-1587498120-92587398) added to the Local Administrators group on Azure AD Joined devices
rootCause: These SIDs are automatically added during Azure AD device join. SID 1 corresponds to the Global Administrator role (Object GUID: 9625d4e5-212b-425d-a573-ffd390573c50) and SID 2 corresponds to the Azure AD Joined Device Local Administrator role (Object GUID: c64a8984-7f85-4c8e-884c-9f5e86c58405).
solution: Expected behavior by design. To confirm: run the script at https://github.com/okieselbach/Intune/blob/master/Convert-AzureAdSidToObjectId.ps1 to convert SIDs to Object ID GUIDs, then verify against Get-MgRoleManagementDirectoryRoleDefinition output. Only users assigned these roles will have local admin rights on the device.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Azure AD Authentication/Looking to find two SID's added in Azure AD Joined Devices local administrator group

symptom: SAML authentication fails with error: 'SAML assertion is invalid, error: Date/Time verification failed. Check assertion date/time values and clock skew between IDP and SP'
rootCause: Time/Date mismatch between Azure AD (acting as SAML IDP) and the target SAML application (SP). Clock skew outside acceptable tolerance causes SAML assertion date/time validation to fail.
solution: (1) Check the target app SAML configuration and verify its date/time settings. (2) If date/time is incorrect on the app, update it to match Azure AD time. (3) If updating time is not possible, update the clock skew tolerance settings on the target SAML application to accommodate the time difference between the app and Azure AD.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Azure AD Authentication/SAML Auth error due to clock skew issue

symptom: Unable to download 'User Registration details' report from Entra ID portal (Authentication methods > User registration details blade)
rootCause: Portal download fails due to throttling when the tenant has a high number of users — the portal report download endpoint hits rate limits for large datasets.
solution: Use MS Graph PowerShell instead: (1) $RequiredScopes = @('Directory.AccessAsUser.All','Directory.ReadWrite.All','AuditLog.Read.All'); (2) Connect-MgGraph -Scopes $RequiredScopes; (3) Select-MgProfile -Name 'beta'; (4) Get-MgReportAuthenticationMethodUserRegistrationDetail -All | select UserPrincipalName,UserDisplayName,IsMfaCapable,IsPasswordlessCapable,IsSsprCapable,IsSsprRegistered,DefaultMfaMethod | Export-Csv -Path C:\output.csv
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Azure AD Authentication/Unable to download "User Registration details" from EntraID portal

symptom: Setting up SAML SSO for an app returns AADSTS7500525: 'There was an XML error in the SAML message at line X, position Y. Verify that the XML content of the SAML messages conforms to the SAML protocol specifications.'
rootCause: The SAMLRequest generated by the application vendor is malformed or invalid and does not conform to SAML protocol specifications. Can also occur when compressed SAML Authentication Requests incorrectly apply compression to the POST binding instead of Redirect binding only.
solution: Work with the app vendor to fix their SAMLRequest XML per the SAML AuthnRequest RFC (https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/develop/single-sign-on-saml-protocol#authnrequest). Ensure compression is only used for Redirect binding, not POST binding. Use Fiddler or SAML Tracer to capture and validate the SAMLRequest. Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/active-directory/error-code-aadsts75005-not-a-valid-saml-request
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Azure AD Enterprise Applications/AADSTS7500525 SAML error

symptom: Application published via Azure AD App Proxy overrides robots.txt configuration: internal robots.txt shows 'Allow: /' but external access via App Proxy returns 'Disallow: /'.
rootCause: Design behavior - Azure AD App Proxy intentionally blocks web crawlers and bots to protect published applications. App Proxy is designed only for Line of Business apps for licensed users, not for publishing websites publicly on the internet.
solution: This is an unsupported scenario with no workaround. Azure AD App Proxy is not a method to publish websites on the public internet. Every user accessing via App Proxy must have a license. Web crawler/SEO scenarios are explicitly blocked by design. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/app-proxy/application-proxy-security#remote-access-as-a-service
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Azure AD Enterprise Applications/Azure AD App Proxy - Robots.txt

symptom: SAML token does not include group claims when user is a member of more than 150 groups; a link to the Microsoft Graph endpoint is included in the token instead of the group list.
rootCause: Azure AD limits the number of groups emitted in a token to 150 for SAML assertions and 200 for JWT. When a user exceeds this limit (including direct and transitive/nested group memberships), an overage indicator with a Graph link is included instead.
solution: Use Microsoft Graph transitiveMemberOf API to retrieve full group membership: GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users/{userId}/transitiveMemberOf/$count with header 'ConsistencyLevel: Eventual'. Handle pagination via @odata.nextLink. Docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/hybrid/how-to-connect-fed-group-claims#options-for-applications-to-consume-group-information
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Azure AD Enterprise Applications/Group Attributes  Claims in the SAML Token

symptom: Unable to manage user and group assignments on an Enterprise Application created using Graph SDK; the assignment UI option is not visible in Azure AD Portal.
rootCause: Service Principal created via Graph SDK is missing the built-in tag 'WindowsAzureActiveDirectoryIntegratedApp'. Without this tag, the Azure AD Portal does not display the user/group assignment management UI.
solution: Add the missing tag to the Service Principal: Update-ServicePrincipalId "{SP-ObjectId}" -Tags "WindowsAzureActiveDirectoryIntegratedApp". After adding the tag, the user/group assignment management option becomes available in the Azure AD Portal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Azure AD Enterprise Applications/Unable to manage users and groups assignment on Enterprise which was created using Graph SDK

symptom: Customers are unable to load Azure AD Sign In Logs in the Azure portal.
rootCause: Known issue with Sign-In Logs UX caused by MSGraph throttling affecting the Sign-In Logs endpoint.
solution: Known issue with no confirmed workaround at time of documentation. Reference the Known Issues page: https://supportability.visualstudio.com/AzureAD/_wiki/wikis/AzureAD/812934/Known-Issues?anchor=issue-4:signins-ux/msgraph-throttling
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Compliance & Reports/Unable to load the Azure AD Sign In Logs

symptom: Customer wants to create a Conditional Access policy targeting only Azure AD Joined devices (not Hybrid Azure AD joined, not Intune compliant) but cannot find such a control in CA policy access controls.
rootCause: Feature not available by design. Simply having a device registered/joined is not considered a strong enough security control. PG confirmed no plans to introduce a standalone Azure AD Join device control in CA.
solution: Use existing CA access controls: 'Require device to be marked as compliant' (requires Intune management) or 'Require Hybrid Azure AD joined'. There is no standalone Azure AD Joined device control. For Azure AD joined devices, Intune compliance is the recommended enforcement mechanism.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Conditional Access/Control for 'Azure ad join device' in Conditional access policy

symptom: Excluded users list in a Conditional Access policy does not enumerate in the Azure portal; unable to add or remove excluded users from the policy.
rootCause: Duplicate user object IDs in the excluded users list cause the portal UI enumeration to fail.
solution: 1. Find CA policy ID: GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/conditionalAccess/policies. 2. Get policy details: GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/conditionalAccess/policies/{id}. 3. Identify duplicate object IDs in excludeUsers. 4. PATCH to remove duplicates: PATCH https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/conditionalAccess/policies/{id} with only conditions.users.excludeUsers in the body. HTTP 204 = success. Excluded users list will then enumerate normally in portal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Conditional Access/Excluded users list does not enumerate in Conditional Access policy, unable to add or remove excluded users
