symptom: Named Locations created via PowerShell or in the Preview Named Locations portal view do not appear in the Classic Named Locations view, and vice versa.
rootCause: Two distinct Named Locations views exist in the Azure AD Portal (Preview and Classic). Named Locations created in the Preview view or via PowerShell only appear in the Preview Named Locations view; those created in Classic view only appear in Classic view.
solution: Access Named Locations in the Preview Named Locations view if they were created via PowerShell or Preview portal. This is expected behavior due to two parallel UX implementations. Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/conditional-access/location-condition#api-support-and-powershell
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Conditional Access/Troubleshooting Preview Named Locations

symptom: Azure AD Join fails with error code 8018000a 'The device is already enrolled', even after removing the work or school account from Windows Settings.
rootCause: Stale enrollment registry GUIDs remain under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Enrollments\ after a previous work/school account was registered as 'Azure AD registered'. These leftover keys block new Azure AD Join enrollment.
solution: 1. Export backup: reg export HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Enrollments\ Enrollment.reg. 2. Delete all GUID subkeys under HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Enrollments\ EXCEPT: Context, Ownership, Status, ValidNodePaths. 3. Reboot. 4. Retry Azure AD Join. Note: If a stale device object appeared in AAD portal after the error, verify its timestamp and delete it before retrying.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Devices/AAD Join fails with error code 8018000a 'The device is already enrolled'

symptom: Device enrollment fails with error 80180014 (MENROLL_E_DEVICENOTSUPPORTED): MDM server reports platform not supported; user cannot enroll device into Intune
rootCause: User does not have permission to enroll personally owned devices; Intune enrollment restriction 'Personally owned' is set to Block for the device type
solution: In Microsoft Endpoint Manager Admin Center (endpoint.microsoft.com) → Devices > Enrollment restrictions > Device type restrictions > All Users > Properties > Edit Platform settings → set 'Personally owned' to Allow for the required device type → Review + Save
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Devices/Azure AD Device Join Errors

symptom: HAADJ device has no PRT (Primary Refresh Token) after user logs in; device status shows valid Hybrid AAD join or pending state but authentication relying on device PRT fails
rootCause: Login delay on HAADJ device (user logs in after many days) causes the device/application not to send PRT during sign-in to validate device status; PRT is absent even though device join status appears valid
solution: Ask user to perform a Password Reset from the device; this invalidates the old/missing PRT and forces issuance of a new PRT
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Devices/How to Invalidate or Generate new PRT on the device

symptom: Incorrect SAM account name (e.g. AzureAD\OldUsername) displayed at Start > Settings > Accounts > Other users on Azure AD Joined device even after account rename or removal and re-add
rootCause: Stale registry keys at HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\IdentityStore\LogonCache (Name2Sid, SAM_Name, Sid2Name GUIDs) and IdentityStore\Cache retain old SAM account mapping; these keys require SYSTEM context to delete and re-adding the account simply re-links to the previous SAM account
solution: 1. Remove account from Other Users; 2. Download PSExec and run 'Start-Process -FilePath cmd.exe -Verb Runas -ArgumentList /k PsExec.exe -i -s powershell.exe'; 3. Use reg delete to remove 3 keys under IdentityStore\LogonCache (Name2Sid, Sid2Name, SAM_Name for the affected user); 4. Open regedit, delete SID entries referencing old user under IdentityStore\Cache; 5. Restart device and re-add account
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Devices/Incorrect username on Azure AD Joined device

symptom: Customer wants to prevent users from registering personally owned or all devices with Azure AD
rootCause: Default Azure AD configuration allows all users to register devices; no blocking policy configured
solution: Azure AD > Devices > Device settings → modify 'Users may register their devices with Azure AD' (note: greyed out if Intune MDM is configured). For Intune environments: configure Device type restrictions in Microsoft Endpoint Manager Admin Center → set 'Personally owned' to Block for target device platforms
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Devices/Prevent devices from registering with Azure AD

symptom: Windows 10 devices fail to complete Hybrid Azure AD Join in a targeted deployment; devices remain unjoined or stuck in pending state
rootCause: Three compounding issues: (1) user account missing Intune license, (2) GPO for SCP (Service Connection Point) and Intune auto-enrollment endpoints not configured, (3) devices not in an OU selected by Azure AD Connect for sync
solution: 1. Apply Intune license to affected user accounts; 2. Create GPO with SCP client-side registry keys and Intune auto-enrollment GPO, assign to computer OUs; 3. Run gpupdate on devices to apply GPO; 4. In Azure AD Connect, select the OU containing target devices; 5. After first AAD Connect sync, devices appear as HAADJ (may be in pending state until user logs in)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Devices/Targeted HAADJ deployment

symptom: Error 'AADSTS50011: The reply URL specified in the request does not match the reply URLs configured for the application' when connecting to Exchange Online PowerShell
rootCause: Microsoft updated the reply URL list for Exchange Online PowerShell; older EXO PowerShell module versions (pre-2.0.4) reference outdated reply URLs that no longer match the registered application configuration
solution: Uninstall old Exchange Online Management module and install version 2.0.4 or later: Install-Module -Name ExchangeOnlineManagement -MinimumVersion '2.0.4'. Note: this is an EXO module issue, not an AAD configuration issue.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Knowledge Base/Changes to Reply URL in Exchange online PowerShell

symptom: User cannot sign in to Fortinet SSL VPN using MFA verification codes (Microsoft Authenticator app code or SMS code); receives 'Access deny' error after entering the code
rootCause: VPN authentication method not configured to use PAP or CHAPv2; the NPS extension for Azure AD MFA requires PAP to support all authentication methods (TOTP, SMS, app-based)
solution: Change the authentication method in the Fortinet SSLVPN client/server settings to PAP (Password Authentication Protocol) or CHAPv2; PAP enables the NPS extension to pass the secondary factor verification to Azure AD MFA
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Multi-Factor Authentication/Azure MFA with Fortinet SSL VPN

symptom: Batch Explorer sign-in fails for users using Microsoft Authenticator App; both authentication prompts appear identical (same account) even though they are for different tenants, causing user confusion or denial
rootCause: Batch Explorer must authenticate the user into all accessible tenants simultaneously, generating multiple auth requests that appear identical in the Authenticator App
solution: Option 1: Ensure all tenant accounts are registered in the Authenticator App. Option 2: Modify the aadURL in Batch Explorer > Environment > Edit custom environments to target only the specific tenant the user needs
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Multi-Factor Authentication/Batch Explorer with MFA using the Authenticator App

symptom: User has 5 Microsoft Authenticator apps or hardware tokens registered and can no longer log in or register a new Authenticator app; receives message to 'contact their Administrator'; admin sees no option in Azure AD portal UI to delete individual authenticator app registrations
rootCause: Azure AD enforces a hard limit of 5 Microsoft Authenticator app registrations per user account; there is no portal UI for admins to remove individual authenticator app method entries
solution: Admin uses Microsoft Graph API: (1) GET https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/users/{upn}/authentication/microsoftAuthenticatorMethods to list devices; (2) DELETE the same URL with appended /{id} to remove the old registration. Requires UserAuthenticationMethod.ReadWrite.All permission in Graph Explorer
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Multi-Factor Authentication/TSG - You can't setup Microsoft authenticator because you already have 5 authenticator apps

symptom: User is unable to set up or use MFA; MFA prompts blocked or not appearing
rootCause: User account has been added to the MFA Block list in Azure AD (Security > MFA > Block/Unblock User)
solution: Go to Azure Portal > Azure Active Directory > Security > MFA > Block/Unblock User, remove the user from the blocked list, reset the user's password, and have the user attempt MFA registration again
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Multi-Factor Authentication/User is unable to setup MFA

symptom: User is unable to register for MFA; Azure AD audit logs show 'Update User' activity with exceptions unrelated to strong authentication (e.g., proxy address exceptions)
rootCause: Another Azure AD object has the same proxy address value as the user trying to register MFA; the duplicate proxy address prevents the user object from being updated during MFA registration
solution: Check Azure AD Audit Logs for the user (AAD > Users > Audit Logs, filter by UPN, look for 'Update User' with exceptions in Modified Properties tab on non-authentication attributes like proxy addresses); identify and resolve the duplicate proxy address conflict on the colliding object
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Multi-Factor Authentication/User is unable to setup MFA

symptom: Creating a Distribution list in Exchange Online / Azure AD sync fails with error: 'The value specified for property Alias is incorrect. Reason: ContainsBlockedWord'; affected aliases include reserved words such as admin, administrator, security, postmaster, webmaster, root, ssl-admin
rootCause: Exchange Online and Azure AD restrict a set of reserved words (abuse, admin, administrator, hostmaster, majordomo, postmaster, root, secure, security, ssl-admin, webmaster) from being used as primary email aliases for security and operational reasons
solution: Create the distribution list first with a temporary non-blocked alias (e.g., 'dl-temp'), then update the primary alias to the desired blocked word after the object is created in Exchange/AAD
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Sync between Azure Active Directory and Exchange Online - The value specified for property Alias is incorrect. Reason: ContainsBlockedWord

symptom: Unable to get past login screen on macOS 26 with Authenticated Guest Mode; warning dialog appears during login with no message displayed when using Platform SSO (Password auth method)
rootCause: Authenticated Guest Mode introduced in macOS 26 has not been validated or supported by Microsoft Entra ID. Feature is Apple-side and Microsoft has not tested it.
solution: Do not use Authenticated Guest Mode with Microsoft Entra ID at this time. No timeline for official support. Customers must wait for Microsoft validation before deploying in production or test environments.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Apple Devices/MacOS Platform SSO Extension Authenticated Guest Mode

symptom: Platform SSO Kerberos integration not working - TGTs not being used or Kerberos authentication fails after Platform SSO PRT is acquired on macOS
rootCause: Either TGTs were not successfully issued by Entra ID during PSSO PRT acquisition, or MDM Kerberos Extension configuration is incorrect (usePlatformSSOTGT/allowPlatformSSOAuthFallback/performKerberosOnly settings)
solution: 1. Run 'app-sso platform -s' to verify TGTs are issued (check tgt_ad/tgt_cloud importSuccessful: true). 2. Verify customer's Kerberos MDM config matches Microsoft docs sample. 3. If TGTs issued and config correct but still failing → open ticket with Apple (Apple owns the Kerberos Extension, Microsoft only issues the TGTs).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Apple Devices/MacOS Platform SSO Troubeshooting Kerberos

symptom: Access to Azure Files Storage via smb:// command on macOS fails when using Platform SSO cloud Kerberos TGT
rootCause: IdentifierURIs in Azure Files app manifest in Entra portal uses uppercase 'CIFS/' but Apple's Kerberos Extension requires lowercase 'cifs/'
solution: Check the IdentifierURIs in the app manifest of the Azure Files Storage app registration in Entra portal. Change 'CIFS/filesharename' to lowercase 'cifs/filesharename'. This fixes the smb:// file share access issue with cloud Kerberos TGT.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Apple Devices/MacOS Platform SSO Troubeshooting Kerberos

symptom: Using deprecated v1 AADLoginForLinux VM extension (device code flow for SSH) on Azure Linux VMs; extension deprecated August 15, 2021 and no longer supported by CSS or PG
rootCause: v1 AADLoginForLinux VM extension uses device code flow which was deprecated. v2 AADSSHLoginForLinux uses SSH certificate-based authentication and is the supported replacement.
solution: 1. Uninstall the AADLoginForLinux VM extension. 2. Enable System Assigned managed identity on the VM/VMSS. 3. Install the new AADSSHLoginForLinux VM extension. See Azure AD SSH Sign-in for Azure Linux VMs wiki for full guidance.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Archived Wikis/Azure AD Sign-in for Azure Linux VMs (Deprecated don't use)

symptom: Unexpected Hybrid Azure AD Join occurs even when GPO 'Register domain joined computers as devices' is set to DISABLED
rootCause: GPO race condition during GPO refresh temporarily changes autoWorkplaceJoin registry value to 1, triggering the Workplace Join Automatic-Device-Join scheduled task before it is set back to disabled
solution: Follow controlled validation of hybrid Azure AD join documentation. Fix available in Windows 10 Build 19042.962 (20H2) and later (KB for v2004/v1909/v1903/v1809). Ensure customers apply cumulative update. Until patched, implement controlled validation per Microsoft docs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Azure AD Device Registration KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: Hybrid Azure AD Joined (DJ++) devices do not get a PRT when VPN is not connected; users experience broken SSO after coming back online
rootCause: Hybrid devices off corporate network rely on user-authenticated VPN to reach DC. Kerberos has no visibility to DC without VPN, so PRT update is not scheduled after Windows logon.
solution: Workaround: 1. Unlock device, 2. Enable VPN, 3. Lock and unlock again to trigger network logon and PRT update. Permanent fix: Use machine-authenticated VPN that stays active when users are not logged in.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Azure AD Device Registration KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: dsregcmd /status shows stale 'AzureAdPrtUpdateTime' - the timestamp does not reflect when the PRT was actually last renewed by WAM
rootCause: PRTReceivedTime (shown as AzureAdPrtUpdateTime) is only updated during network logon, not during RefreshToken flow (WAM PRT refresh). This is by design per code comment to trigger network logon with user credentials every 4 hours.
solution: Known bug reported to PG (IcM 198241657, Bug 28243502). ETA fix in Windows version 21H1 (2106). The fix adds a separate field to track actual PRT update time vs network logon time.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Azure AD Device Registration KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: Dollar sign ($) character appears in the HAADJ device name in Azure AD
rootCause: Specific behavior related to how AD DS appends $ to computer account names being carried into HAADJ device records
solution: See Microsoft Tech Community blog post for explanation and solution: https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/core-infrastructure-and-security/the-mysterious-case-of-the-a-k-a-dollar-sign-character-in-hybrid/ba-p/768326
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Azure AD Device Registration KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: Error 0xcaa70007 (ERROR_INET_DOWNLOAD_FAILURE) / 0xCAA5001C Token broker operation failed during WAM SSO; CAPI2 logs show certificate chain verification failures from 3rd party process
rootCause: Third-party security software (Tumbleweed Desktop Validator/tmwdcapiclient.dll or Axway Desktop Validator) interferes with WAM SSO flow by intercepting certificate chain verification during token acquisition
solution: Check CAPI2 logs at the time of the 0xCAA70007 error for certificate chain verification failures from 3rd party apps. Identify if Tumbleweed Desktop Validator or Axway Desktop Validator is installed. Work with vendor or exclude security software from intercepting WAM/AAD certificate operations.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Azure AD Device Registration KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: Devices in pending registration state with error 0x801c005a in 'dsregcmd /status' output during managed HAADJ flow
rootCause: The 'cloudUserCertificate' attribute was excluded from the sync scope in AAD Connect Azure AD Attributes wizard
solution: In AAD Connect, ensure the 'cloudUserCertificate' attribute is NOT excluded from the Azure AD Attributes sync scope. Re-include it in the sync configuration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Azure AD Device Registration KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: Unexpected MFA prompts on Windows platform when PRT is signed with RSA device key during PRT renewal at Windows Sign-in
rootCause: ESTS only carries MFA claim from previous PRT when PRT is signed with SessionKey or ECCDeviceKey. When PRT is signed with RSADeviceKey, MFA claim is dropped, causing re-authentication prompts.
solution: Engineering fix in progress (Work item: identitydivision/Engineering#3260136). ETA June 2025. Until fixed: verify PRT signing key type (SessionKey vs DeviceKey) and check if device is eligible for ECC key upgrade. ICM reference: 611248028.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Azure AD Device Registration KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: Devices unjoin from Entra ID after HP OneAgent update 1.2.50.9581 (released Oct 21, 2025); MS-Organization-Access certificate deleted; Intune enrollment also affected
rootCause: HP OneAgent 1.2.50.9581 runs a script that deletes certificates where subject/issuer/friendly name contains '1E'. MS-Organization-Access cert on affected devices matches this pattern. HP stopped OTA update by Oct 22, 2025 10AM CST.
solution: 1. Check if HP OneAgent 1.2.50.9581 is installed. 2. HAADJ devices: self-heal on next scheduled task run (or run 'dsregcmd /join' or reboot). 3. Entra Joined devices: manual re-enrollment via Settings>Accounts>Access Work or School>Connect (if local admin exists) or reinstall via OOBE. 4. Intune cert: re-enroll if Entra cert still intact, otherwise full device re-provision. Contact HP for cleanup tool.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Azure AD Device Registration KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: [Resolved] Sign-in logs in Entra Portal show Join Type as a digit (number) instead of text string (Microsoft Entra Joined / Hybrid Joined / Registered)
rootCause: Engineering bug in Sign-in logs rendering of Join Type field
solution: [Resolved] Engineering fix released November 4, 2025. Reference: IcM 701609121, Feature 3412709.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Azure AD Device Registration KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: Windows devices running HP OneAgent experience RDP/Windows 365 Cloud PC authentication failures; TPM authorization errors (Event ID 23, 21); WHfB failures; error 'dictionary attack mitigation is triggered'
rootCause: HP OneAgent consumed nearly all available TPM Non-Volatile (NV) Handles, preventing TPM from allocating handles for WHfB, Entra device registration, and TPM2_CC_Create operations. msedgewebview2.exe repeatedly creates TPM-backed keys, incrementing the lockout counter.
solution: 1. Run HP cleanup tool to delete orphaned NV handles (preferred). 2. Uninstall HP OneAgent. 3. Update to fixed HP OneAgent version. 4. TPM Reset (last resort - may require WHfB reprovisioning). After cleanup, devices resume normal WHfB and authentication. Reference: ICM for HP OneAgent TPM issue Feb 2026.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Azure AD Device Registration KNOWN ISSUES

symptom: 用户密码过期后，现有 AAD refresh token 未被自动吊销，用户仍可继续访问资源
rootCause: AAD 设计上密码过期不触发 token 吊销。Refresh token 仅在明确 revoke 操作、凭据更改或管理员/用户主动操作时才失效
solution: 使用 Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK：Connect-MgGraph (Scopes: User.Read.All, Directory.ReadWrite.All) → 查询 accountEnabled=true 用户的 LastPasswordChangeDateTime → 超过最大允许密码年龄则执行 Revoke-MgUserSignInSession。注意：PHS 同步用户云端密码策略默认永不过期，需另行评估
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Useful PowerShell Scripts for AAD/Revoke Refresh Token for users with Expired Password

symptom: 需要立即撤销全部或指定用户组的 AAD refresh token（账户泄露、员工离职、内部威胁等紧急场景）
rootCause: Access token 默认有效期 1 小时，refresh token 在未明确吊销前持续有效；管理员需主动执行 revoke 操作
solution: 单用户：Azure Portal/O365 Portal 点击 Revoke sessions，或 Revoke-MgUserSignInSession -UserId $userId；批量：从 CSV/TXT 读取 UPN 列表循环执行；全部活跃用户：Get-MgUser -All 后循环执行；操作后可在 Azure AD 审计日志查到 Revoke Refresh Token 事件
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Useful PowerShell Scripts for AAD/Revoke Refresh tokens all users or Group of Users

symptom: Android 设备点击 'face, fingerprint, PIN or security key' 后页面停滞/超时，底部出现 'sign in another way' 小对话框；Broker 日志出现 'User canceled the request' 取消异常
rootCause: 设备上找不到与账户匹配的 passkey（work profile 用户常见）；Credential Manager 将对话框超时或关闭注册为用户主动取消
solution: 1) 点击底部小对话框展开，选择安全密钥/跨设备选项；2) 确认 Authenticator 已保存 passkey（work profile 需在工作配置的 Authenticator 中分别保存）；3) 重启设备；4) 最终方案：重新注册 passkey
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Android Devices/Passkey FAQ

symptom: Samsung Android 设备使用 YubiKey 进行 passkey 认证时，CredMan 返回取消错误（用户未主动取消），passkey 页面跳回认证选项
rootCause: Samsung 设备已知问题：安全密钥连接对话框期间若检测到带有 YubiOTP 启用的 YubiKey 插入，CredMan 错误返回取消；正由 YubiCo 和 Samsung 联合调查
solution: 临时方案：在 YubiKey 上禁用 YubiOTP；长期方案：等待 YubiCo/Samsung 官方修复
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Android Devices/Passkey FAQ

symptom: Android 设备无法使用 NFC YubiKey 进行 passkey 认证
rootCause: Android Credential Manager API 平台限制：仅支持 USB 连接方式，不支持 NFC，且短期内无添加计划
solution: 通知客户仅支持 USB 连接方式使用 YubiKey；NFC 为已知平台限制
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Android Devices/Passkey FAQ

symptom: iOS unmanaged broker (app flip) 场景：用户在第三方 MFA app（如 Duo）完成审批后直接打开 Outlook 等目标应用，导致显示 unknown error
rootCause: Auth 流程需在 Authenticator 中闭环：Duo 批准后需手动返回 Authenticator，由 Authenticator 完成流程后自动回跳目标应用
solution: 引导用户：第三方 MFA 批准后先返回 Microsoft Authenticator app，等待 Authenticator 自动回跳到 Outlook 等目标应用，不能直接打开目标应用
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Apple Devices/Entra ID device registration and broker scenarios on iOS and macOS

symptom: iOS 18.1.x 设备使用 NFC YubiKey 认证时，顶部通知提示检测到 YubiKey 并要求打开 Safari，但无密码输入弹窗，用户无法完成认证
rootCause: iOS 18.1.x 已知 bug（Yubico 报告）：NFC YubiKey 在该版本无法正常工作
solution: 升级设备 iOS 至 18.2.x 或更高版本（Apple 已在 18.2.x 中修复）
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Apple Devices/Entra ID device registration and broker scenarios on iOS and macOS

symptom: macOS Chrome/Edge 使用 web native bridge 流程时报错 AADSTS50210: This web native bridge call resulted in a non-retriable error from the operating system
rootCause: 浏览器通过 native bridge 调用 broker 时，broker 返回 PERSISTENT_ERROR；最常见原因是 associated domain 验证失败
solution: 1) 检查并确保 associated domain 验证完整通过；2) 确认 SSO extension 正确配置；3) 若 SSO extension 故意未启用，需同步禁用/移除 Microsoft Single Sign-On Chrome 扩展
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Apple Devices/Entra ID device registration and broker scenarios on iOS and macOS

symptom: macOS PSSO 已注册设备 Device Auth challenge 失败，UI 提示重新注册，或 PSSO 注册返回 unexpected error；客户有 SSL Inspection/防火墙/代理
rootCause: 3rd party MDM 注册产生 deviceless PRT 无法满足 CA Device Auth 要求；协议切换到 PKeyAuth 需要 x-ms-PkeyAuth+ header，但防火墙/代理删除了该 header
solution: 通过网络抓包确认 /token 请求中 x-ms-PkeyAuth+ header 是否存在；若缺失，要求客户联系防火墙/代理厂商配置放行该 header
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Apple Devices/Entra ID device registration and broker scenarios on iOS and macOS

symptom: macOS 启用 se_key_biometric 策略的 PSSO 用户持续被提示重新注册
rootCause: Apple API 使用 Secure Enclave key 签名时失败（Unexpected ACM requirement: 8），返回 POLoginResultPermanentFailure，触发强制重新注册
solution: 引导客户联系 Apple 支持处理，属于 Apple 侧 API 问题
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Apple Devices/Entra ID device registration and broker scenarios on iOS and macOS

symptom: iOS 用户频繁收到交互式登录提示；Broker 日志大量 prompt=login + MAM resource 请求；MAM 日志 AuthenticationEnabled=true
rootCause: Intune MAM 非活动超时策略（AuthenticationEnabled=true）与 AAD Sign-In Frequency CA 策略互不感知，同时触发时在同一会话产生两次独立登录提示
solution: 移除其中一个策略；建议保留 AAD SIF CA 策略，移除 MAM 的 AuthenticationEnabled=true 非活动策略
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Apple Devices/Entra ID device registration and broker scenarios on iOS and macOS

symptom: iOS/macOS SSO extension 无法启动，OneAuth 报错 1001/1000/1003，sub status 6008，日志出现 'Sso Extension is returning unexpected error'
rootCause: Apple framework 在调用 SSO extension 前执行 associated domain 验证失败（App 请求 → Apple 验证 associated domain → 失败 → 未到达 SSO extension）
solution: 检查并修复 associated domain 配置，确保验证完整通过；参考 SSO extension troubleshooting 文档检查网络设置和 associated domain 配置
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Apple Devices/Entra ID device registration and broker scenarios on iOS and macOS

symptom: macOS 原生应用认证失败（error -1205）；环境有 Defender for Cloud Apps 并上传了自定义 Root cert；浏览器认证正常
rootCause: 原生应用响应客户端证书挑战时未提供完整证书链，Defender for Cloud Apps 远端 Root cert 验证失败；浏览器自动提供完整链
solution: 要求开发者将完整证书链（非单一 Root cert）上传至 Defender for Cloud Apps；客户端修复追踪于 work item 3138696
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Apple Devices/Entra ID device registration and broker scenarios on iOS and macOS

symptom: Intune-Portal on Ubuntu 20.04/22.04 fails to register device with 'An unexpected error has occurred' on-screen. After closing, user sees 'Error Something went wrong [1001]'. journalctl shows 'URI has no auth code (code) query parameter' and PasswordResetRegistrationRequiredInterrupt / ErrorNo 50125.
rootCause: User is in a tenant with Self-Service Password Reset (SSPR) enabled and has never signed in before. On first sign-in they are immediately redirected to mysignins to register required authentication methods, which interrupts the Intune enrollment flow (JavaFX WebView fails to process the redirect).
solution: Workaround: Open a browser on any device and sign into the Azure portal. Complete MFA/auth method registration at mysignins.microsoft.com. Once registration is complete, return to the Linux machine and retry Intune-Portal enrollment.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Linux Devices/Enterprise SSO for Linux Desktops/Known Issues

symptom: Edge on Linux does not show trace information (Request ID) when a Conditional Access policy's grant controls are not satisfied. This differs from Windows behavior where the trace is displayed to the end user.
rootCause: By design — Edge on Linux does not currently surface end-user trace information for CA policy failures, unlike the Windows client.
solution: Instruct the user to enable browser trace logging in Edge to discover the Request ID: launch Edge with '--enable-logging -v=1 --oneauth-log-level=5 --oneauth-log-pii' flags to capture detailed OneAuth logs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Linux Devices/Enterprise SSO for Linux Desktops/Known Issues

symptom: During Intune enrollment on Linux, user sees blank screen (no password prompt) after entering UPN. journalctl for microsoft-identity-broker.service shows: 'Unable to save to Keyring. Likely because there is no default keyring set on the machine.'
rootCause: No default keyring/password has been set on the machine prior to running Intune enrollment. MSAL fails to save tokens to the keyring because no default keyring exists or it is locked.
solution: Open Edge browser — a 'Choose password for new keyring' prompt will appear. Set a keyring password. Then launch Microsoft Intune Portal and proceed with enrollment as usual. Note: PG is tracking a bug to have microsoft-identity-broker.service trigger the keyring prompt automatically.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Linux Devices/Enterprise SSO for Linux Desktops/Known Issues

symptom: User sees blank screen during Linux sign-in or Intune enrollment. Issue is caused by outdated Nvidia GPU drivers rather than keyring or authentication issues.
rootCause: Nvidia drivers are out of date, causing display/rendering issues that result in blank screens during authentication WebView rendering.
solution: Run: sudo ubuntu-drivers autoinstall
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Linux Devices/Enterprise SSO for Linux Desktops/Known Issues

symptom: On Ubuntu 23.xx, Intune enrollment freezes/fails to launch after entering credentials. microsoft-identity-broker.service fails with: 'ERROR: JAVA_HOME is set to an invalid directory: /usr/lib/jvm/java-11-openjdk-amd64'. Service restart loops and fails with exit-code 1/FAILURE.
rootCause: microsoft-identity-broker has a hard dependency on JDK 11, but Ubuntu 23.04+ ships with Java 17 as the default JRE. The broker's systemd unit references a hardcoded path to java-11-openjdk that does not exist on Ubuntu 23.xx. Ubuntu 23.xx is NOT officially supported.
solution: Install OpenJDK 11 manually and set JAVA_HOME in the broker service environment: Environment='JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-11-openjdk-amd64'. Run 'apt install intune-portal' to pull in the java11 dependency. Note: Ubuntu 23.xx is unsupported — recommend using Ubuntu 20.04 or 22.04.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Linux Devices/Enterprise SSO for Linux Desktops/Known Issues

symptom: 'Certificate validation failed' error during Linux Intune enrollment on Ubuntu 20.04/22.04/23.04 or RHEL 8.x/9.x. Error appears after entering credentials and attempting device enrollment.
rootCause: Passkey is configured as an authentication method for the user account, but the Linux broker/Intune stack does not correctly handle passkey as an auth method, causing certificate validation to fail.
solution: Option 1: Remove passkey auth method from another device (go to account.microsoft.com/account → Security info, remove passkey, ensure phone authenticator is available). Then on Linux: stop microsoft-identity-device-broker, clear all cached credentials (rm -rf ~/.cache/intune-portal ~/.cache/Microsoft ~/.cache/microsoft-edge ~/.config/intune ~/.config/microsoft-edge ~/.config/microsoft-identity-broker ~/.Microsoft ~/.local/share/intune-portal and sudo rm -rf /var/lib/microsoft-identity-device-broker), reboot, wait 5 min after login, then re-enroll. Option 2: Update microsoft-identity-broker to latest version.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Linux Devices/Enterprise SSO for Linux Desktops/Known Issues

symptom: Device is marked as compliant in intune-portal, but Edge on Linux cannot access Teams or other 2FA-gated resources. User sees 'Set up your device to get access' prompt with error code 530003 and 'Device State unregistered'.
rootCause: Stale browser cookies or session state in Edge causes the device state to appear unregistered to the resource despite Intune compliance status being correct.
solution: Clear cookies in Edge and restart the browser.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Linux Devices/Enterprise SSO for Linux Desktops/Known Issues

symptom: User encounters an authentication error with error tag '48yve' on Linux Enterprise SSO. With broker version 2.0.3, the error tag may show as '4ii4e'.
rootCause: ESTS (server-side authentication service) change caused the 48yve error. The server-side fix was rolled out by the ESTS team (reference: Incident-698745556). For 4ii4e with broker 2.0.3, further log collection is required to determine root cause.
solution: Ask the user to retry the operation — ESTS server-side fix should have resolved the 48yve error. If 48yve persists or user sees 4ii4e with broker 2.0.3, collect JavaBroker logs for further investigation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Linux Devices/Enterprise SSO for Linux Desktops/Known Issues

symptom: 排查时需要找到 Linux 设备在 Azure DRS 的 Device ID（aad_device_hint）
solution: 运行 `cat ~/.config/intune/registration.toml`，输出中的 aad_device_hint 字段即为 Azure Device Registration Service 的 Device ID
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Device Registration/Linux Devices/Enterprise SSO for Linux Desktops/Troubleshooting/Get Device Id

symptom: 无法验证 Linux 设备上 Primary Refresh Token (PRT) 的当前状态或有效性
rootCause: Linux 平台尚未实现 PRT 状态可视化/查询功能，该功能仍属于待开发的 feature enhancement
solution: 目前只能通过 `cat ~/.config/intune/registration.toml` 确认设备注册状态（间接验证 PRT 前提）；Linux PRT 有效期 90 天，设备活跃时每 4 小时自动续期，不活跃时仅 14 天有效
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Device Registration/Linux Devices/Enterprise SSO for Linux Desktops/Troubleshooting/PRT Troubleshooting

symptom: Linux 设备上 Conditional Access (CA) policy 针对 device compliance 执行异常，或 PRT 行为与 Windows/Mac 不同
rootCause: Linux 不支持 deviceless PRT；PRT 仅在安装了 Intune Company Portal（broker）时可用；目前仅 Microsoft Edge for Linux 支持 device-bound tokens 和 CA enforcement
solution: 确认设备已安装 Intune Company Portal 和 Microsoft Edge；CA policy 的 device-based 控制仅对 Edge Browser 有效；其他 native app（非 Edge）不受 device-bound tokens 保护
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Device Registration/Linux Devices/Enterprise SSO for Linux Desktops/Troubleshooting/PRT Troubleshooting

symptom: MSAL Python 使用 enable_broker_on_linux 标志在 WSL 和 standalone Linux 行为不同，不清楚依赖差异
rootCause: WSL 使用 Windows Authentication Manager (WAM) 作为 broker（无需额外依赖）；standalone Linux 需要安装 Intune Portal 才能启用 broker；若 standalone Linux 未安装 broker 会自动 fallback 到 non-broker 认证流
solution: WSL 环境：启用 enable_broker_on_linux 标志无需额外依赖（WAM 默认提供）；standalone Linux：需安装 Intune Portal 作为 broker；若只希望在 WSL 启用 broker（不影响 standalone），可在 Azure CLI 代码中仅在 WSL 检测时才设置 enable_broker_on_linux；未安装 broker 时自动 fallback，不会报错
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Device Registration/Linux Devices/Enterprise SSO for Linux Desktops/Windows System for Linux (WSL) Support/MSAL.Python Integration with WSL

symptom: Admin consent workflow returns AADSTS90097 error: 'An error occurred during admin consent processing. Review should have at least one decision maker.'
rootCause: No reviewer is configured in the Admin consent workflow settings. The workflow requires at least one designated reviewer (Global Admin, Cloud App Admin, or App Admin role).
solution: Navigate to Entra Portal → Enterprise Applications → User Settings → Admin consent requests → add at least one reviewer with Global Admin, Cloud App Admin, or App Admin role.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/How to/Azure AD Admin consent workflow

symptom: AADSTS65004 error occurs during admin consent workflow: 'User declined to consent to access the app.' even though the user didn't explicitly decline.
rootCause: User clicked the 'Back to app' button during the Approval required / consent request screen. This is by-design — Entra ID generates AADSTS65004 and sends it to the app's redirect URI.
solution: Expected behavior. Instruct the user to click 'Request approval' on the Approval required screen instead of 'Back to app'. The AADSTS65004 error is expected and sent to the redirect URI when the user abandons the flow.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/How to/Azure AD Admin consent workflow

symptom: User receives 'Need admin approval' error (AADSTS90094): 'This operation can only be performed by an administrator. Sign out and sign in as an administrator or contact one of your organization's administrators.' when trying to consent to an app.
rootCause: Risk Based Step-up Consent flagged the OAuth permission request as risky and blocked the non-admin user from granting consent. Microsoft evaluates permission requests and blocks those deemed high-risk (possible consent phishing).
solution: 1. Check Azure AD Audit Logs (ApplicationManagement / Consent to application / Failure) for StatusReason='Risky application detected'. 2. Use KQL: AuditLogs | where ResultDescription contains "Risky application detected" or ResultDescription contains "Microsoft.Online.Security.UserConsentBlockedForRiskyAppsException" | project TargetedUsers=InitiatedBy, TimeGenerated, TargetResources. 3. Admin should carefully evaluate the app before granting tenant-wide admin consent. 4. If admin consent workflow is enabled, user can submit a request for admin review.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/How to/Risk Based Step Up Consent

symptom: AADSTS90095 appears in Fiddler/HAR trace during consent flow (user sees no explicit error but is redirected to admin consent request workflow).
rootCause: Risk Based Step-up Consent redirected user through admin consent workflow. AADSTS90095 is the step-up variant — it differs from AADSTS90094 in that the admin consent workflow IS enabled, so the user gets a request flow. IfxUlsLogs will show ExceptionType=Microsoft.Online.Security.UserConsentBlockedForRiskyAppsException for tagId=03jo.
solution: Distinguish from AADSTS90094: 90095 means admin consent workflow is active and user can submit a request. To investigate: query ASC Kusto — GlobalIfxUlsEvents | where env_cloud_role == 'restdirectoryservice' | where tagId == '03jo' | where internalCorrelationId == '<correlationId>' | project env_time, internalCorrelationId, tagId, message. Check AuditLogs for 'Risky application detected' audit event.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/How to/Risk Based Step Up Consent

symptom: Applications using Azure AD Graph API (https://graph.windows.net) stop working or return errors after June 30, 2025 (Stage 1 retirement). ADAL to MSAL Recommendations API also retired December 15, 2025.
rootCause: Azure AD Graph API Stage 1 retirement commenced June 30, 2025 (announced June 2020). Applications still using AAD Graph instead of Microsoft Graph are affected. ADAL to MSAL Recommendations API (adalToMsalMigration) retired December 15, 2025 per MC1151678.
solution: Migrate applications to Microsoft Graph API (graph.microsoft.com). Refer to internal wiki: 'Unblock AAD Graph APIs in App Registrations'. For ADAL→MSAL migration, see Microsoft Graph migration guidance. Message Center post MC1151678 was sent to affected tenants September 11, 2025.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application Experiences Feature Announcement Reference

symptom: When reconnecting an RDP session to an AADJ machine, logonui shows 'The user name or password is incorrect. Try again.' but initial connection works fine
rootCause: Windows OS Bug 31608330: PKU2U credential provider incorrectly maps the user account from the RDP client when reconnecting to multiple AADJ devices from the same Windows client without the AzureAD\ prefix.
solution: Launch RDP connection using format AzureAD\username@contoso.com. This signs directly into all VMs without error and PKU2U trace shows '[pku2u] Pku2uGetCertFromCred got cert from IDP'. Until OS fix is released, always use the AzureAD\ prefix for AADJ RDP.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Device Registration/Window Devices/Microsoft Entra Join/Troubleshooting RDP to Azure AD Joined machines

symptom: Device enrollment error 80180014 (MENROLL_E_DEVICENOTSUPPORTED): MDM server doesn't support this platform/version; actually indicates user has no permission to enroll personally owned device into Intune
rootCause: The user does not have permission to enroll personally owned devices into Intune; Intune enrollment restrictions block personally owned device enrollment by default
solution: In Microsoft Endpoint Manager Admin Center > Devices > Enrollment restrictions > Device type restrictions > All Users > Properties > Edit Platform settings > Set Personally owned to Allow for required device categories > Review + Save
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Devices/Azure AD Device Join Errors

symptom: No PRT (Primary Refresh Token) on HAADJ device; device shows valid HAAD join status or pending state but has no PRT; sign-in to Azure AD-protected resources fails or doesn't use device identity
rootCause: User logged into HAADJ device after prolonged absence (many days); during sign-in the device/application does not send PRT to validate device status, leaving device without a valid PRT
solution: Ask user to perform a Password Reset from the machine; this invalidates the stale state and forces the system to issue a new PRT to the device
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Devices/How to Invalidate or Generate new PRT on the device

symptom: Incorrect SAM account (AzureAD\OldUsername) displayed for Azure AD user at Start > Settings > Accounts > Other users after account rename; re-adding the account still shows old SAM account name
rootCause: Stale SAM account mapping cached in registry under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\IdentityStore\LogonCache (Name2Sid, SAM_Name, Sid2Name keys) and IdentityStore\Cache; these keys require SYSTEM context to delete; removing and re-adding the account re-links to old cached entries
solution: 1) Remove account from Other Users. 2) Use PsExec (Sysinternals) to open PowerShell as SYSTEM: Start-Process -FilePath cmd.exe -Verb Runas -ArgumentList '/k PsExec.exe -i -s powershell.exe'. 3) Use reg delete to remove the 3 keys under IdentityStore\LogonCache (Name2Sid\<GUID>, Sid2Name\<SID>, SAM_Name\<UserName>). 4) In regedit delete all SID keys with reference to old username under IdentityStore\Cache. 5) Restart machine. 6) Re-add account - correct SAM name will now display
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Devices/Incorrect username on Azure AD Joined device

symptom: Customer wants to prevent users from registering personal or corporate devices with Azure AD; 'Users may register their devices with Azure AD' setting is greyed out in portal when Intune is in use
rootCause: When Microsoft Intune or MDM is configured, the device registration setting in AAD portal becomes unavailable and enrollment must be managed through Intune enrollment restrictions instead
solution: Without Intune: AAD portal > Devices > Device settings > set 'Users may register their devices with Azure AD' to None/selected users. With Intune: Endpoint Manager > Devices > Enrollment restrictions > Device type restrictions > configure Personally owned to Block for target device types
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Devices/Prevent devices from registering with Azure AD

symptom: Hybrid Azure AD Join (HAADJ) not completing for Windows 10 devices; devices remain in Pending state or do not appear as Hybrid AAD Joined in Azure AD portal
rootCause: Multiple root causes: (1) user account lacks Intune license if Intune enrollment intended; (2) no GPO configured for SCP (Service Connection Point) registry keys; (3) target devices are not in an OU selected for sync by Azure AD Connect
solution: 1) Verify user has Intune license if Intune enrollment is required. 2) Create GPO with SCP registry keys and Intune auto-enrollment policy targeting all target computers; run gpupdate. 3) In AAD Connect, select the OU containing target devices and run a sync. 4) Devices will appear as HAADJ (pending state resolves after user logs in)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Devices/Targeted HAADJ deployment

symptom: Azure AD Premium trial license for internal lab/test tenant is near expiry and requires extension
rootCause: Trial licenses have fixed expiration and require manual extension request for internal lab use
solution: Email aadpdemo@microsoft.com with CC to manager and abigail.brown@microsoft.com; include tenant name and tenant ID; submit request at least 30 days before license expiry
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Extend Azure Active Directory trial license

symptom: AADSTS50011 error when connecting to Exchange Online PowerShell: 'The reply URL specified in the request does not match the reply URLs configured for the application'
rootCause: Microsoft updated the reply URLs list for Exchange Online PowerShell; older versions of the ExchangeOnlineManagement module use outdated reply URLs that no longer match the registered application URLs in Azure AD
solution: Uninstall the old Exchange Online PowerShell module and install the latest version (minimum 2.0.4): Install-Module -Name ExchangeOnlineManagement -MinimumVersion '2.0.4'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Knowledge Base/Changes to Reply URL in Exchange online PowerShell

symptom: Need to find Azure AD device ID (aad_device_hint) or Intune device ID on a Linux desktop
solution: Run `cat ~/.config/intune/registration.toml` — aad_device_hint = Azure AD Device ID (from Azure DRS), device_hint = Intune Device ID, account_hint = signed-in account UPN
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Linux Devices/Enterprise SSO for Linux Desktops/Troubleshooting/Get Device Id

symptom: Unable to view or verify PRT (Primary Refresh Token) details on a Linux device — no equivalent to Windows dsregcmd /status
rootCause: Linux does not yet support deviceless PRT and has no client-side tooling to inspect PRT state. This is a known platform limitation / feature gap.
solution: Indirectly verify device registration via `cat ~/.config/intune/registration.toml` — presence of aad_device_hint indicates device is registered. Confirm SSO works via Edge Browser. Feature enhancement is planned.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Linux Devices/Enterprise SSO for Linux Desktops/Troubleshooting/PRT Troubleshooting

symptom: Linux device PRT expires unexpectedly or SSO fails after period of device inactivity
rootCause: On Linux, PRT is valid 90 days and renewed every 4 hours only when device is in use. If unused, PRT is only valid for 14 days. Linux only integrates Intune and Edge Browser as native apps with broker support.
solution: Ensure device is used regularly to trigger PRT renewal (every 4 hours when active). Only Edge Browser is supported for device-bound tokens and CA enforcement on Linux. If SSO fails after inactivity, re-authenticate via Intune Portal to obtain a new PRT.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Linux Devices/Enterprise SSO for Linux Desktops/Troubleshooting/PRT Troubleshooting

symptom: `sudo apt install intune-portal` fails with 'E: Unable to locate package intune-portal' on Ubuntu/Debian
rootCause: Microsoft package repository not added to apt sources, or package tree error preventing apt from resolving the package.
solution: Manually download .deb from https://packages.microsoft.com/ubuntu/20.04/prod/pool/main/i/intune-portal/ (Ubuntu 20.04) or https://packages.microsoft.com/ubuntu/22.04/prod/pool/main/i/intune-portal/ (Ubuntu 22.04). Click 'main' > 'i' > 'intune-portal', then install with `sudo dpkg -i intune-portal_<VERSION>_amd64.deb` and resolve dependency errors.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Linux Devices/Enterprise SSO for Linux Desktops/Troubleshooting/Package Install Failures

symptom: `dpkg -i intune-portal` fails with dependency errors: missing msalsdk-dbusclient, microsoft-identity-broker, libsdbus-c++1, or default-jre
rootCause: Linux SSO package stack has a strict dependency chain: intune-portal → msalsdk-dbusclient → (microsoft-identity-broker + libsdbus-c++1) → default-jre. Packages must be installed in correct order.
solution: Install dependencies in order: 1) `sudo apt install default-jre` 2) `sudo apt install libsdbus-c++1` 3) `sudo dpkg -i microsoft-identity-broker_<VERSION>_amd64.deb` 4) `sudo dpkg -i msalsdk-dbusclient_<VERSION>_amd64.deb` 5) `sudo dpkg -i intune-portal_<VERSION>_amd64.deb`. Run `sudo apt --fix-broken install` if apt reports unmet dependencies. For RHEL/Fedora use dnf equivalents.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Linux Devices/Enterprise SSO for Linux Desktops/Troubleshooting/Package Install Failures

symptom: Need to list Entra ID broker accounts present on Linux device, or microsoft-identity-broker service is not running
rootCause: microsoft-identity-broker.service is not started, or accounts need to be queried via D-Bus interface for diagnostics
solution: 1) Check service status: `systemctl --user status microsoft-identity-broker.service` 2) If not running, restart: `systemctl --user restart microsoft-identity-broker.service` 3) Query accounts via D-Bus: `dbus-send --type=method_call --print-reply --dest=com.microsoft.identity.broker1 /com/microsoft/identity/broker1 com.microsoft.identity.broker1.getAccounts string:'1.0' string:'3d44a50b-2e62-4dd7-b946-7b63d940a78f' string:'{"clientId":"CUSTOMERS_CLIENTID","redirectUri":"CUSTOMERS_REDIRECT_URI"}'`
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Linux Devices/Enterprise SSO for Linux Desktops/Troubleshooting/Retrieve broker accounts present

symptom: Need to verify Linux device Entra ID registration and Intune enrollment status from the client side
solution: Check `~/.config/intune/registration.toml`: (1) account_hint present = user signed into Intune; (2) aad_device_hint present = device registered with Entra ID (Azure DRS); (3) device_hint present = device enrolled in Intune MDM
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Linux Devices/Enterprise SSO for Linux Desktops/Troubleshooting/Verify Enrollment Status

symptom: Intune compliance check fails on Linux due to Drive Encryption or Password Complexity policy not met
rootCause: Compliance policy evaluation for Drive Encryption and Password Complexity is managed by Intune, not Entra ID. These are Intune-side requirements.
solution: Route to Intune support team. For Password Complexity: configure pam_pwquality (min 12 chars, uppercase, lowercase, digit, symbol) in /etc/pam.d/common-password. For Drive Encryption: configure LVM encryption during Ubuntu installation (Advanced Options > Use LVM > Encrypt). See Walk-Through guides for full setup.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Linux Devices/Enterprise SSO for Linux Desktops/Troubleshooting/Verify Enrollment Status

symptom: MSAL.Net application running via WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux) fails to access keyring or secret storage
rootCause: MSAL.Net on Linux/WSL requires libsecret for GNOME keyring-based secret storage used by the authentication flow
solution: Install required keyring packages: `sudo apt install libsecret-tools seahorse`
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Linux Devices/Enterprise SSO for Linux Desktops/Windows System for Linux (WSL) Support/MSAL.Net with WSL

symptom: MSAL Python app needs broker authentication on WSL or standalone Linux, or app falls back to non-broker auth flow unexpectedly
rootCause: enable_broker_on_linux flag not set in MSAL Python, or on standalone Linux the Intune Portal (Linux broker) is not installed. On WSL, WAM (Windows Authentication Manager) serves as broker; on standalone Linux, Intune Portal provides the broker.
solution: Set `enable_broker_on_linux=True` in MSAL Python config. For WSL-only activation, conditionally enable only when running under WSL (no extra dependencies needed — WAM is available by default on Windows). For standalone Linux: install Intune Portal for broker support. Without broker on standalone Linux, MSAL gracefully falls back to non-broker auth flow.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Linux Devices/Enterprise SSO for Linux Desktops/Windows System for Linux (WSL) Support/MSAL.Python Integration with WSL

symptom: Entra Hybrid Join using Entra Kerberos fails with error DSREG_TOKEN_MISSING_ON_PREM_ID (0x801c0095) - the token doesn't contain an on-premises ID
rootCause: The Kerberos ticket from the on-premises Kerberos authority doesn't contain information required by Microsoft Entra ID; domain controllers running Windows Server 2025 have not been configured with EnableKerbHaadj.exe
solution: On every domain controller that runs Windows Server 2025 within the Active Directory domain, run the tool EnableKerbHaadj.exe and restart the domain controller
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Window Devices/Microsoft Entra Hybrid Join/Entra Hybrid Join using Entra Kerberos

symptom: Entra Hybrid Join using Entra Kerberos fails with SEC_E_NO_AUTHENTICATING_AUTHORITY (0x80090311) - no authority could be contacted for authentication
rootCause: No functional domain controller running Windows Server 2025 can be contacted in the Active Directory domain; KDC service may not be running or DC is not reachable by the client
solution: Install at least one domain controller running Windows Server 2025 build 26100.6905 or later; run EnableKerbHaadj.exe and restart; ensure KDC service is running; run dcdiag.exe to confirm DC is advertising itself and reachable
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Window Devices/Microsoft Entra Hybrid Join/Entra Hybrid Join using Entra Kerberos

symptom: Entra Hybrid Join using Entra Kerberos fails with SEC_E_LOGON_DENIED (0x8009030c) / Kerberos event log KDC_ERR_NULL_KEY (0x9) - 'No KerberosKeyInformation Keys found'
rootCause: Kerberos key for the Microsoft Entra Device Registration Service principal (AppId: 01cb2876-7ebd-4aa4-9cc9-d28bd4d359a9) has not been configured; the tag KerberosPolicy:ExchangeForJwt is missing from the service principal tags
solution: Add the tag 'KerberosPolicy:ExchangeForJwt' to the ADRS service principal using Microsoft Entra PowerShell: connect with Application.ReadWrite.All scope, then Set-EntraServicePrincipal -ObjectId $drsSP.Id -Tags $tags (after appending the tag to existing tags list)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Window Devices/Microsoft Entra Hybrid Join/Entra Hybrid Join using Entra Kerberos

symptom: Hybrid Entra Joined device remains in 'Pending' state; device cannot complete authentication, acquire PRT, or apply device-based Conditional Access policies
rootCause: Device cannot connect to the Azure AD device registration service or cannot reach the on-premises AD domain controller to discover the SCP object; or no synced user has signed into the device to trigger automatic registration
solution: Verify network connectivity to Microsoft endpoints under the system account (per https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/devices/how-to-hybrid-join#network-connectivity-requirements); configure WPAD or WinHTTP Proxy Settings GPO if using an authenticated outbound proxy
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Window Devices/Microsoft Entra Hybrid Join/Pending Devices - Microsoft Entra Hybrid Join

symptom: Hybrid Entra Joined device shows as 'Pending' after device object was deleted or moved out of sync scope in Entra ID; device locally still appears HAADJ but cannot authenticate
rootCause: Device was removed from Entra ID (moved out of Entra Connect sync scope, deleted by admin, or VDI golden image re-registration issue), but the device locally still thinks it is HAADJ and will not re-trigger the Hybrid Join registration process on its own
solution: 1) Run 'dsregcmd /leave' at elevated command prompt; 2) Wait for/confirm device object is removed from Entra portal; 3) Reboot the device; 4) For managed env: run 'Start-ADSyncSyncCycle -PolicyType Delta' then 'dsregcmd /join'; for federated env: run 'dsregcmd /join' directly (no sync cycle needed)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Window Devices/Microsoft Entra Hybrid Join/Pending Devices - Microsoft Entra Hybrid Join

symptom: Slow logon (~21 second delay) on Hybrid Entra Joined devices on first sign-in; ESTS sign-in event shows four device auth attempts; issue resolves after dsregcmd /leave
rootCause: Device authentication failing during PRT acquisition (DoGetToken step in aadcloudap.dll); the device object was deleted from Entra ID by admin (e.g., removed from sync scope, deleted while cleaning stale devices); CloudAP retries 3 times at 3-second intervals on first logon
solution: 1) Collect auth trace, find AAD Operational event 1250 (DoGetToken) correlation ID; 2) Check via ASC > Sign-ins > Diagnostics to confirm device auth failure with ~21s delay; 3) Search Entra audit logs by device name as target to find deletion event; 4) Fix: run dsregcmd /leave then rejoin; if deletion was intentional: run dsregcmd /leave only
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Window Devices/Microsoft Entra Hybrid Join/Slow Logon On Hybrid Joined Devices

symptom: 'Users may register their devices with Azure AD' setting controls are grayed out in Azure AD Portal Device Settings blade; admin cannot disable device registration
rootCause: Microsoft Intune enterprise application (AppId: 0000000a-0000-0000-c000-000000000000) is enabled in the tenant and is enforcing the device registration setting to on, preventing modification
solution: Locate Microsoft Intune enterprise application in Azure portal (Enterprise Applications) and set AccountEnabled to false; or use PowerShell: 'Update-MgServicePrincipalByAppId -AppId 0000000a-0000-0000-c000-000000000000 -AccountEnabled $false' after connecting with Application.ReadWrite.All scope
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Device Registration/Window Devices/Microsoft Entra Join/Device registration_Troubleshooting Windows 10 Add Work or School Account

symptom: Users see AADSTS90094 (admin consent workflow enabled) or AADSTS90095 (workflow disabled) when trying to consent to Files.Read.All, Files.ReadWrite.All, Sites.Read.All, or Sites.ReadWrite.All after Microsoft's July 2025 default consent policy rollout
rootCause: Microsoft changed the default user consent policy (SFI initiative) starting July 2025. These 4 high-risk SharePoint/OneDrive delegated permissions now require admin consent even when AdminConsentRequired=false. Rollout affects tenants over 2–4 weeks from end of July 2025.
solution: (1) Admin grants consent for the application in the Entra portal. (2) Admin opts out by setting user consent to 'Low impact' or 'Off' in Entra admin portal. (3) Admin creates a custom consent policy to revert changes. See troubleshooting at https://supportability.visualstudio.com/AzureAD/_wiki/wikis/AzureAD/1377253
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/How to/User Consent Settings -  Let Microsoft Manage Your Consent Settings

symptom: After the August 2025 default consent policy rollout, tenants that had user consent turned OFF report that users can consent to MORE permissions than they should be able to
rootCause: Side effect of applying the new updated default consent policy — some tenants with user consent turned OFF had their setting accidentally enabled during the rollout
solution: Issue was mitigated August 16–17, 2025. If still occurring after mitigation: escalate with ICM #669687504 (https://portal.microsofticm.com/imp/v5/incidents/details/669687504/summary) and add the tenant to the ICM. Do NOT proactively communicate this issue to customers unless they open a case for this specific behavior.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/How to/User Consent Settings -  Let Microsoft Manage Your Consent Settings

symptom: After an administrator approves a pending Admin Consent request or directly grants Admin Consent to an application, that administrator is automatically added to 'Users and groups' of the Enterprise Application
rootCause: By design (confirmed by Product Management) — as part of the Admin Consent flow, the user who grants consent is automatically assigned to the application. This behavior is not configurable and cannot be suppressed.
solution: This is by design. Manually remove the administrator from Users and groups after testing if application access is not required for that admin account.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Labs/LAB - Approving Admin Consent Results in Automatic User Assignment

symptom: AADSTS1003031 error 'Misconfigured required resource access in client application registration' when using the tenant-wide admin consent URL to add a multi-tenant application to another tenant
rootCause: The multi-tenant app registration in the home tenant has no API permissions configured. The tenant-wide admin consent URL requires at least one RequiredResourceAccess entry to be present.
solution: In the home tenant, add the default Microsoft Graph Delegated permission 'User.Read' (or any required permission) to the app registration under API Permissions. Then retry the admin consent URL.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Labs/LAB - Reproducing the AADSTS1003031 error

symptom: Adding API permissions to an Agent Identity Blueprint app fails in the Microsoft Entra ID portal; required permissions not visible or 'Add a permission' operation fails for Agent Blueprint objects
rootCause: The Entra ID portal uses Microsoft Graph v1.0 for permission management, but Agent Identity Blueprint operations are only supported via Microsoft Graph beta API. The portal cannot list or modify permissions for Agent Blueprint service principals.
solution: Add API permissions directly using Microsoft Graph beta endpoint. Use Graph Explorer or PowerShell with the beta API: POST https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/oauth2PermissionGrants or POST https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/servicePrincipals/{id}/appRoleAssignments. Do NOT use the Entra portal for Agent Blueprint permission management — it is expected to fail by design.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Labs/LAB AddpermissionstoanAgentBlueprintapp

symptom: Users receive AADSTS90094 or AADSTS90095 'Need admin approval' / 'Approval required' even when admin consent has already been granted for the application
rootCause: The application's authorization request to Entra ID includes 'prompt=consent' parameter. Entra ID honors this parameter and forces the consent prompt regardless of existing admin consent grants.
solution: Ask the app vendor/owner to remove 'prompt=consent' from the authorization request to Entra ID. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/v2-oauth2-implicit-grant-flow
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Troubleshooting/Dynamic Consent. AADSTS90094 or AADSTS90095 error

symptom: AADSTS90094 error triggered for a specific app — Audit Logs show 'Risky application detected' under ApplicationManagement > Consent to application
rootCause: Microsoft detected risky permissions requested by the application during the consent process. Entra ID automatically elevates to requiring admin consent when a risky application signature is found.
solution: Check Audit Logs → ApplicationManagement → 'Consent to application' → filter for 'Risky application detected'. Review the risk detection and approve the admin consent request if the application is trusted. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-apps/application-sign-in-unexpected-user-consent-error#requesting-not-authorized-permissions-error
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Troubleshooting/Dynamic Consent. AADSTS90094 or AADSTS90095 error

symptom: AADSTS90094 error when 'User assignment required' is enabled on the Enterprise app but admin consent has not been granted for all permissions in the request scope
rootCause: Enterprise app Properties → 'User assignment required' is set to 'Yes'. With this setting, users cannot consent themselves and admin consent must be explicitly granted for all permissions in the authorization request.
solution: Option 1: Disable 'User assignment required' in Enterprise app → Properties. Option 2: Grant admin consent for all required scope permissions. Use the /authorize endpoint (NOT the portal 'Grant admin consent' button which only covers registered permissions): navigate to https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/v2.0/authorize?client_id={AppID}&response_type=code&redirect_uri={RedirectURI}&response_mode=fragment&scope={all-required-scopes}&state=12345&nonce=12345&prompt=consent as Global Admin and check 'Consent on behalf of your organization'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Troubleshooting/Dynamic Consent. AADSTS90094 or AADSTS90095 error

symptom: AADSTS90094/90095: Users cannot sign in because one or more permissions in the 'scope' parameter have not been admin consented. Note: Files.Read.All, Files.ReadWrite.All, Sites.Read.All, Sites.ReadWrite.All always require admin consent even when AdminConsentRequired flag is false.
rootCause: One or more permissions in the 'scope' parameter of the authorization request are not admin consented. The portal 'Grant admin consent' button only consents permissions listed in App Registration → API Permissions, NOT permissions dynamically requested via the scope parameter.
solution: Grant admin consent via the /authorize endpoint directly. As Global Admin, navigate to: https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/oauth2/v2.0/authorize?client_id={AppID}&response_type=code&redirect_uri={RedirectURI}&response_mode=fragment&scope={all-required-scopes}&state=12345&nonce=12345&prompt=consent and check 'Consent on behalf of your organization'. Verify success in Enterprise app → Permissions or ASC Tenant Explorer → Application → Permissions → Admin Consent tab.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Troubleshooting/Dynamic Consent. AADSTS90094 or AADSTS90095 error

symptom: Non-admin users still receive 'Need Admin Approval' / AADSTS65001 / AADSTS90094 even when tenant is configured to 'Allow user consent for apps from verified publishers, for selected permissions' with low-risk permissions. App is from a verified publisher with only profile, openid, User.Read permissions.
rootCause: The 'offline_access' permission (Graph EntitlementId: 7427e0e9-2fba-42fe-b0c0-848c9e6a8182) is NOT included in the 'microsoft-user-default-low' consent RBAC policy by default. When the app requests 'offline_access' in its scope, admin consent is required even though other permissions are classified as low-risk. DPX log shows: [ConsentPolicyRbac:ApplicablePolicies][offline_access EntitlementId][ApplicablePolicies][microsoft-application-admin, microsoft-company-admin] — missing microsoft-user-default-low.
solution: Add 'Microsoft Graph → offline_access' to the low-risk permission classification: Azure AD → Enterprise applications → Permission classifications → Add permissions → Microsoft Graph → offline_access. Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/manage-apps/configure-permission-classifications
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Troubleshooting/Troubleshoot unexpected admin consent in Low Risk Consent flow

symptom: AADSTS65001: 'The user or administrator has not consented to use the application with ID {App-Id}'. Application not found in Enterprise applications when searching by App ID or name.
rootCause: Service principal for the application does not exist in the tenant. The app has never been consented to in this tenant, so no service principal object was created.
solution: Perform admin consent to create the service principal and grant permissions. Admin navigates to the admin consent endpoint: https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/adminconsent?client_id={client-id} or use the /authorize endpoint with prompt=admin_consent. Alternatively, add the application from the Enterprise applications gallery.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Troubleshooting/Troubleshooting Consent in Azure AD

symptom: User is prompted for consent twice during sign-in: after accepting the first consent prompt and being redirected to login, the user is again prompted for consent with fewer scopes visible in the second prompt.
rootCause: Application is sending dynamic scopes in the authorization request. By design, during the first call to Entra ID only existing graph delegations are evaluated, and dynamic scopes are evaluated during a second call. This is a known by-design behavior (ICM 250523706).
solution: This is by-design behavior in Entra ID. Work with the application vendor to send all required scopes in a single authorization request upfront rather than using incremental/dynamic consent across multiple requests.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Troubleshooting/Troubleshooting Consent in Azure AD

symptom: AADSTS650056: 'Misconfigured application. This could be due to one of the following: The client has not listed any permissions for AAD Graph in the requested permissions in the client's application registration. Or, The admin has not consented in the tenant. Or, Check the application identifier in the request to ensure it matches the configured client application identifier.'
rootCause: One or more of: (1) Application registration has no API permissions configured, (2) Admin has not consented in the tenant, or (3) The client_id in the authorization request does not match the App ID registered in Azure AD.
solution: 1. Check App Registration → API Permissions — add at minimum User.Read delegated permission. 2. Verify the client_id in the request matches the App ID in the App Registration. 3. Grant admin consent for required permissions in Enterprise application or via admin consent endpoint.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Troubleshooting/Troubleshooting Consent in Azure AD

symptom: AADSTS90008: 'The user or administrator has not consented to use the application with ID {id}. This happened because application is misconfigured: it must require access to Windows Azure Active Directory by specifying at least Sign in and read user profile permission.'
rootCause: The application registration does not have User.Read (or openid) delegated permission added to its API Permissions. This is the minimum permission required for user sign-in.
solution: Add Microsoft Graph 'User.Read' delegated permission (or at minimum 'openid') to the Application Registration → API Permissions. Then consent to the permission — either user consent (if allowed by tenant settings) or admin consent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Troubleshooting/Troubleshooting Consent in Azure AD

symptom: AADSTS900941 or AADSTS900981: 'Administrator consent is required. App is considered risky' or 'An admin consent request was received for a risky app'. Users cannot grant consent and must wait for admin approval.
rootCause: Microsoft has classified the application as potentially risky based on app reputation and security analysis signals. Entra ID automatically requires admin consent for risky apps tenant-wide.
solution: Admin reviews and approves/denies the pending admin consent request from: Azure AD → Enterprise applications → Admin consent requests. The admin should review the permissions requested and the app's reputation before approving. If the app is trusted, the admin approves the request to allow users to access it.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Troubleshooting/Troubleshooting Consent in Azure AD

symptom: All users receive 'Need admin approval' for every application regardless of the app's permissions, risk level, or publisher verification status. Tenant-wide user consent appears completely blocked.
rootCause: Tenant setting 'Users can consent to apps accessing company data on their behalf' is set to 'No'. This can be verified in Azure AD → Enterprise applications → User settings, or in ASC → Application → Settings.
solution: Option 1 (preferred): Change the tenant consent setting in Azure AD → Enterprise applications → User settings to allow user consent or configure it to 'Allow user consent for apps from verified publishers, for selected permissions'. Option 2: Admin performs admin consent for each application users need to access via the admin consent endpoint.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_Consent_Experiences/Troubleshooting/Troubleshooting Consent in Azure AD

symptom: Error 76021: 'Application configured to accept only signed SAML requests' — SP-initiated SAML sign-in fails because the application sends an unsigned authentication request when the tenant requires signed requests
rootCause: Application object has 'isSignedRequestRequired: true' configured (ADFS2AAD SAML request signing feature), but the application is sending unsigned SAML authentication requests to the SAML2 endpoint. Note: /common endpoint is not supported for LOB applications with SAML request signing.
solution: Application must sign its SAML authentication requests using its private key before sending to Azure AD. Alternatively, if signed requests are not required, set 'isSignedRequestRequired' to false on the application object. Ensure the app uses the /saml2 endpoint (not /common) for authentication requests.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/ADFS2AAD Collected Features 2022

symptom: Error 76022: 'Missing application certificate for SAML request signature verification' — SAML request signing fails because no verification certificate is configured on the application
rootCause: Application object has 'isSignedRequestRequired' enabled but no public certificate with usage 'Verify' has been uploaded to the application configuration in Azure portal. Azure AD cannot verify the signature without the public key.
solution: Upload the public certificate (used to sign SAML authentication requests) to the Application Registration in Azure portal: App Registrations → Certificates & secrets → Certificates section, set certificate usage to 'Verify'. This is the ADFS-equivalent of uploading the signing certificate's public key.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/ADFS2AAD Collected Features 2022

symptom: Error 76023: 'SAML requests signature verification failed' — Azure AD cannot verify the cryptographic signature of the received SAML authentication request
rootCause: The public certificate configured in Azure AD does not match the private key used to sign the SAML request, OR the signature algorithm is RSA-SHA1 and 'allowedWeakAlgorithms' is not configured. Use ESTS Kusto DiagnosticTracesIfx EventId 19929 to investigate.
solution: 1. Ensure the public certificate uploaded to Azure AD exactly matches the private key used to sign SAML requests. 2. If using RSA-SHA1 signing algorithm, set 'allowedWeakAlgorithms' property to include 'rsaSha1' on the application object via Microsoft Graph API. Treat as potential security incident — verify application is configured correctly and the signing key has not been compromised.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/ADFS2AAD Collected Features 2022

symptom: New app creation fails with AppId URI (identifierUri) validation error - URI not part of tenant verified domain list
rootCause: Change introduced October 2021 enforces all AppId URIs must use a verified tenant domain or api:// scheme. Does not affect existing apps; only new apps or existing apps adding a new URI.
solution: Use api://{appId} or api://{tenantId}/{string} scheme, or set URI to https://<tenantInitialDomain>.onmicrosoft.com/<string>. Validate whether app actually needs an AppId URI. Remove wildcard URIs (e.g. https://*.mydomain.com). Add domain to verified domain list if needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Entra ID App Registrations

symptom: Soft-deleted app registration does not immediately appear in the 'Deleted apps' tab in App Registrations portal
