rootCause: Known caching issue - recently deleted app may not show up immediately in the Deleted applications list.
solution: Refresh the page. The deleted app should appear after a page refresh.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Entra ID App Registrations

symptom: Restoring or permanently deleting a soft-deleted app registration fails
rootCause: Either (1) user does not have required permissions, or (2) app was deleted more than 30 days ago (restore only). Restore requires Owner or Global Admin. Permanent delete requires Owner, Global Admin, App Admin, Cloud App Admin, or Hybrid Identity Admin.
solution: Verify user has correct role. For restore: must be Owner or Global Admin AND deletion must be within 30 days. For permanent delete: Owner, Global Admin, App Admin, Cloud App Admin, or Hybrid Identity Admin.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Entra ID App Registrations

symptom: App registration created via MS Graph API has unexpected signInAudience - defaults to AzureADMyOrg instead of expected AzureADandPersonalMicrosoftAccount
rootCause: Starting March 2024, POST https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/applications changed default signInAudience from AzureADandPersonalMicrosoftAccount to AzureADMyOrg, aligning with the Azure/Entra portal default.
solution: When creating apps via MS Graph API, explicitly set signInAudience to the desired value in the request body. This is not a bug - it is an intentional default change announced in the Entra blog.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Entra ID App Registrations

symptom: Multi-tenant app-only authentication (client credentials flow) suddenly fails after July 2024 for cross-tenant token acquisition
rootCause: Entra ID blocked SP-less authentication starting July 8, 2024. SP-less occurs when a multi-tenant app requests an app-only token from a tenant where the client has no service principal. These tokens lack {oid}, {role}, {group} claims. Entra no longer issues them. SP-less clients appear in sign-in logs with Service Principal ID = 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000.
solution: Tenant admin must create an enterprise application (service principal) for each affected multi-tenant client app. Use Application ID from sign-in logs. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-apps/create-service-principal-cross-tenant. Currently active SP-less apps include: MIP Platform, SQL Security, Xstore, Azure Log Analytics, ADRS, Dynamics 365, MDCA, AKS, Dataverse, Auditing, M365 encryption platform.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Entra ID will no longer supports Service Principal Less Authentication

symptom: Sign-in logs show Service Principal ID as '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000' for an app accessing tenant resources
rootCause: The client application is authenticating via SP-less pattern - it is a multi-tenant app with no service principal in this tenant. Entra uses all-zeros GUID to indicate missing SP in sign-in logs.
solution: If the app should be trusted: create a service principal (enterprise app) for that client Application ID in the tenant to restore full log visibility and normal authentication. If untrusted: create SP for the resource app, enable 'require assignment', and optionally disable the resource SP to block all unauthorized SP-less clients.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Entra ID will no longer supports Service Principal Less Authentication

symptom: Microsoft internal employee (MSIE) submits Azure support ticket requesting to be added as Owner of an app in the Microsoft internal tenant (microsoft.onmicrosoft.com)
rootCause: MSIEs sometimes submit external Azure support cases for internal Microsoft tenant (microsoft.onmicrosoft.com) app ownership changes, which must be handled through Microsoft's internal IT support system, not Azure external support.
solution: Direct the MSIE to TechWeb portal (https://microsoft.sharepoint.com/sites/TechWeb) to chat with helpdesk and create an internal IT ticket. Close the Azure support case - no further action required from Azure support.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Microsoft employee wants to be added as an Owner to an app - Microsoft Tenant

symptom: App-only token request fails with error: 'The client application {appId} is missing service principal in the tenant {tenantId}. See instructions here: https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2225119'
rootCause: The multi-tenant client application does not have a service principal (enterprise app) installed in the target tenant. Entra ID requires explicit SP presence for cross-tenant app-only (client credentials) authentication. This is also triggered when a resource app blocks SP-less tokens in Step 1 (1P resources, Feb 23) or Step 2 (3P resources, Mar 3) of the enforcement rollout.
solution: Tenant admin creates a service principal for the client app: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-apps/create-service-principal-cross-tenant. May need to consent to additional permissions for the resource app. For bundling consent: use requiredResourceAccess property on the application or use admin consent URL.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Product updates to Service Principal less Authentication

symptom: All users (including assigned users) cannot sign in to enterprise application and cannot see it in My Apps
rootCause: The enterprise application property 'Enabled for users to sign in' is set to No. This disables all user sign-ins regardless of assignment or visibility settings.
solution: In the enterprise application Properties blade, set 'Enabled for users to sign in' to Yes. Review all three settings together to achieve desired access pattern: Enabled for sign-in, User assignment required, Visible to users.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/User sign in experience depending on application sign in settings

symptom: Unassigned user cannot sign in to enterprise application even though the app appears enabled
rootCause: 'User assignment required' is set to Yes on the enterprise application. Only users/groups explicitly assigned in the Users and Groups blade can sign in.
solution: Either (1) assign the user or group to the enterprise application in Users and Groups blade, or (2) set 'User assignment required' to No to allow all tenant users to sign in. Refer to sign-in behavior matrix: Enabled(Yes)+Assignment(Yes)+Visible(Yes)=Assigned can sign in; Enabled(Yes)+Assignment(No)+Visible(Yes)=All can sign in.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/User sign in experience depending on application sign in settings

symptom: Get-MgUser -Filter 'signInActivity/lastSignInDateTime le ...' returns 400 BadRequest: 'Number of included identifiers cannot exceed 1000'
rootCause: Reporting API incorrectly pages the response and generates more than 1000 results to MSODS; MSODS returns 400 and terminates the call
solution: Use Invoke-MGGraphRequest with explicit pagination ($top=500) and nextLink loop instead of Get-MgUser with lastSignInDateTime filter directly
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Applications Experience PowerShell scripts and MG Graph queries examples

symptom: AADSTS650059: The application is not configured for use in tenant (tenantId). The value AzureADMyOrg set for application property SignInAudience is limiting its use in the tenant
rootCause: App was previously configured as multi-tenant but later converted to single-tenant (AzureADMyOrg). Service Principals provisioned in external tenants can no longer obtain tokens after this change.
solution: If app should be multi-tenant: update signInAudience on the app registration (home tenant) to AzureADMultipleOrgs or AzureADandPersonalMicrosoftAccount. If single-tenant is correct: delete Service Principals in external tenants. Deadline for 211 whitelisted apps is 2025-07-14.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Authentication Method Policy

symptom: App management policy to restrict password secret lifetime (maxLifetime) is not enforced; older apps can still create secrets with any lifetime despite the policy being enabled
rootCause: If the app was created before the date in restrictForAppsCreatedAfterDateTime, the restriction does not apply. Also, if maxLifetime > 730 days, none of the shorter lifetime options will be blocked.
solution: Set restrictForAppsCreatedAfterDateTime to an earlier date to cover affected apps, or create a per-app custom policy (appManagementPolicy) targeting specific application objects
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Authentication Method Policy

symptom: Error creating app registration: 'SignInAudience value AzureADMultipleOrgs not allowed as per assigned policy'. Unable to create multi-tenant app from Entra portal or Graph API
rootCause: Tenant admin has configured an App Management Policy (audienceRestriction type: allowedAudience) that blocks creation of multi-tenant or personal Microsoft Account apps
solution: Request exception from tenant admin via a custom appManagementPolicy with azureAdMultipleOrgs state=enabled, or update the default policy (/beta/policies/defaultAppManagementPolicy) to allow the required signInAudience
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Authentication Method Policy

symptom: Non-admin App Owners cannot manage or modify the apps they own in the Entra ID / Azure AD administration portal
rootCause: The 'Restrict access to Azure AD administration portal' user setting is set to Yes, which blocks all non-admin users from portal access regardless of their app ownership
solution: Set 'Restrict access to Azure AD administration portal' (Azure AD > Users > User settings > Administration portal) to No, OR assign the app owner to the Directory Readers role to grant portal access without additional permissions. Note: PG is aware; fix planned when AAD supports customizing default user permissions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Management Roles

symptom: Group claim missing from SAML/JWT token when using group name regex transform; TraceCode 25729 in logs: 'Overage limit has been hit for groups'
rootCause: User has 1000+ group memberships; token group overage limit is reached before the transform can be applied — the transform is silently ignored
solution: Apply group name filtering in the claims configuration to reduce the number of groups below the overage threshold before the transform runs
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Migration Features Update

symptom: Only the first custom group claim transform appears in the token; subsequent group claim transforms are silently ignored; TraceCode 25721 in logs
rootCause: Only one custom group-based claim is permitted per token. Multiple custom group claims configured via a claims mapping policy have subsequent transforms ignored.
solution: Consolidate to a single custom claim sourced from group claims. The Entra portal enforces this limit; the issue only occurs when configuring multiple group-sourced claims directly via policy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Migration Features Update

symptom: Custom claim from additional user attribute (extension attribute) is missing from the token; TraceCode 25701: 'The user claim with ExtensionID XXX failed to emit because property was missing from collection'
rootCause: The source attribute is empty or not populated on the user object. For synced users, the attribute may not be configured in the sync rules or not populated on the on-premises object.
solution: Check user object via Graph Explorer or Entra portal UI to verify the attribute has a value. For synced attributes: verify sync configuration and confirm the on-premises user object has the attribute populated.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Migration Features Update

symptom: Multivalued claim transform is only applied to the first value in the collection, not all values, even when 'apply to all values' is configured; TraceCode 29934
rootCause: JWT tokens on implicit grant flows have a limited token size. When shouldLimitOutputTokenSize is true, only the first element of a multi-value attribute is transformed.
solution: Switch from implicit grant flow to authorization code flow to avoid token size limits. Alternatively, reduce number of values in the multivalued claim. Note: the portal prevents this misconfiguration in the UI — it only occurs via claims mapping policy with multiple multi-valued sources.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Migration Features Update

symptom: Multi-tenant app authentication fails; sign-in logs show Service Principal ID as '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000'. Error: 'The client application {appId} is missing service principal in the tenant {tenantId}. See instructions here: https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2225119'
rootCause: Entra ID blocked SP-Less authentication (app-only tokens issued without a Service Principal for the client app in the target tenant). Effective July 8, 2024, multi-tenant 3P apps acting as clients cannot obtain tokens from tenants where they have no service principal. SP-Less tokens lack {oid}, {role}, and {group} claims.
solution: Create an Enterprise Application (Service Principal) in the target tenant using the Application ID from sign-in logs. Admin can use portal or MS Graph API: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-apps/create-service-principal-cross-tenant?pivots=msgraph-powershell — List of currently still-allowed SP-Less apps (MIP Platform, SQL Security, Xstore, Azure Log Analytics, ADRS, Dynamics 365 Commerce, Defender for Cloud Apps, etc.) per wiki; these are in an allowlist.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Entra ID will no longer supports Service Principal Less Authentication

symptom: App-to-app authentication (OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials flow) fails cross-tenant. Error includes link https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2225119. Some apps affected by the block, others still working (appear in allowlist). Tenant admins see apps with no SP in sign-in logs.
rootCause: Entra ID SP-Less auth block rolled out in two phases: Step 1 (Feb 23) blocked for all 1P resource apps except allowlisted; Step 2 (Mar 3) blocked for all 3P resource apps except dependent resource apps in allowlist. Exceptions: 3P clients can still auth in their own home tenant; 1P client apps can still auth anywhere.
solution: (1) App developers: request tenant admin to install app as Enterprise App or grant consent. Use requiredResourceAccess property to bundle consent requests for multiple resource apps. (2) Tenant admins: create SP for client and resource apps; add client app as User of resource app and enable 'require assignment'. (3) If SP-Less tokens appearing in logs with all-zeros SP ID for MS Graph: this is normal for legacy MS Graph pattern — no action needed, not a security issue; optionally create SP for these client IDs for log consistency.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Product updates to Service Principal less Authentication

symptom: After October 2024 update, Entra Enterprise App Provisioning settings (Admin Credentials / Tenant URL / Secret Token, Attribute Mappings) cannot be found in their previous locations on the Provisioning blade. Options appear to have moved or disappeared.
rootCause: UI migration from Knockout API to React API for the Entra Enterprise Apps Provisioning blade. Provisioning options moved to dedicated sub-blades. Initial rollout to custom (non-gallery) enterprise applications only; Gallery apps roll out over subsequent 3 months.
solution: Find settings in new dedicated blades: (1) Tenant URL and Secret Token → new 'Connectivity (Preview)' blade; (2) Attribute Mappings → new 'Attribute Mapping (Preview)' blade; (3) Email notification, deletion threshold, provisioning scope → new 'Overview (Preview)' Properties tab. No functional changes — same settings in new locations. Both old and new experiences available until January 2025. A banner on Overview (Preview) page informs of upcoming changes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Entra Enterprise App Provisioning Options Moving To Dedicated Blades

symptom: Error when creating a new app registration or updating identifier URIs (AppId URI / identifierUri). New app cannot be created with the desired URI. Existing apps with unverified domains in their AppId URI are unaffected.
rootCause: October 2021 change: all AppId URIs for new app registrations or URI updates must use domains in the tenant's verified domain list. Wildcard URIs (e.g. https://*.mydomain.com) are also blocked as a security risk.
solution: Use valid URI patterns: api://{appId}, api://{tenantId}/{appId}, api://{tenantId}/{string}, api://{string}/{appId}; or https://{tenantInitialDomain}.onmicrosoft.com/{string}, https://{verifiedCustomDomain}/{string}, https://{string}.{verifiedCustomDomain}, https://{string}.{verifiedCustomDomain}/{string}. Add the domain to the verified domain list if needed. The tenant's initial .onmicrosoft.com domain is always valid. Reference: https://aka.ms/AAe22df
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Entra ID App Registrations

symptom: New app registrations created programmatically via MS Graph API (POST /v1.0/applications) no longer allow personal Microsoft accounts (hotmail.com, outlook.com, msn.com) to sign in. signInAudience unexpectedly defaults to single-tenant.
rootCause: March 2024 change: signInAudience for new apps created via MS Graph (POST https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/applications) now defaults to 'AzureADMyOrg' (single-tenant), previously defaulted to 'AzureADandPersonalMicrosoftAccount'. This aligns with the Entra portal default. Existing apps are not affected.
solution: Explicitly set the signInAudience parameter in the POST /v1.0/applications request body to the desired value (e.g. 'AzureADMultipleOrgs' or 'AzureADandPersonalMicrosoftAccount') if multi-tenant or personal account support is needed. Announcement: https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/microsoft-entra-blog/what-s-new-in-microsoft-entra/ba-p/3796394
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Entra ID App Registrations

symptom: Deleted app registration doesn't immediately appear in Deleted applications list. App restore fails. Confusion about who has permission to restore vs permanently delete a soft-deleted app registration.
rootCause: (1) Soft-deleted apps may not immediately appear in Deleted applications list due to caching. (2) Restore permission is restricted: only Owners and Global Admins can restore — App Admins, Cloud App Admins, and Hybrid Identity Admins cannot restore. (3) App can only be restored within 30 days of deletion.
solution: Refresh the page if soft-deleted app doesn't appear (caching issue). To restore: ensure user is an Owner or Global Admin. Restore window: 30 days from deletion. To permanently delete: Owners, Global Admins, App Admins, Cloud App Admins, and Hybrid Identity Admins can permanently delete. Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/develop/quickstart-restore-app
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Entra ID App Registrations

symptom: Users unexpectedly can or cannot sign in to an Enterprise App; assigned users cannot sign in; unassigned users can sign in; app visible to users who should not have access; app not visible to users who should see it.
rootCause: Enterprise App behavior depends on the combination of three settings in App Properties: (1) 'Enabled for users to sign in', (2) 'User assignment required', and (3) 'Visible to users'. Admins may have set unexpected combinations. Key rules: if 'Enabled for users to sign in = No' → no user can sign in regardless of other settings; 'Visible to users' only controls My Apps portal visibility, not actual sign-in access.
solution: Check all three settings in Enterprise App → Properties. Behavior matrix: [Enabled=Yes, Assignment=Yes, Visible=Yes]: Assigned→can see+sign in; Unassigned→cannot see, cannot sign in. [Enabled=Yes, Assignment=Yes, Visible=No]: Assigned→cannot see but can sign in; Unassigned→cannot see, cannot sign in. [Enabled=Yes, Assignment=No, Visible=Yes]: Assigned→can see+sign in; Unassigned→cannot see but can sign in. [Enabled=Yes, Assignment=No, Visible=No]: all users→cannot see but can sign in. [Enabled=No]: all users→cannot see, cannot sign in. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/enterprise-apps/application-properties
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/User sign in experience depending on application sign in settings

symptom: Receive support ticket from a Microsoft internal employee (MSIE) requesting to be added as an owner of an application in the Microsoft tenant (microsoft.onmicrosoft.com). Employee believes Azure Support can fulfill this request.
rootCause: This is an internal Microsoft IT/helpdesk request, not an Azure customer support issue. Microsoft employees needing app ownership changes in the Microsoft corporate tenant should use internal IT channels, not Azure Support.
solution: Direct the Microsoft employee to TechWeb portal (https://microsoft.sharepoint.com/sites/TechWeb). That portal allows them to chat with helpdesk and create an internal IT ticket. Case can be closed after relaying this information — no further action required on the Azure support case.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Microsoft employee wants to be added as an Owner to an app - Microsoft Tenant

symptom: App disablement/deactivation fails or does not take effect when performed by the App lifecycle manager agent
rootCause: Agent may lack app owner consent, app may be excluded from the batch, admin permissions missing on the app registration, or service-side errors reported by the agent
solution: 1) Verify app owner consented to agent disabling the app; 2) Verify app was not excluded from the batch; 3) Verify the admin can disable the app from the App registration in Entra portal; 4) Examine medenia trace logging for service-side errors reported by the agent
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Application Deactivation (Disablement)

symptom: App disablement/deactivation fails when admin performs it via Entra portal, Microsoft Graph API, or PowerShell
rootCause: Service-side error during PATCH request on the App registration isDisabled property
solution: Capture a browser trace (Fiddler/HAR) of the failure to set Deactivate on the app registration and observe the API call failure; escalate to AAD Applications → Triage if service-side error confirmed
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Application Deactivation (Disablement)

symptom: PowerShell script using Get-MgServicePrincipal with PreferredSingleSignOnMode filter returns an incomplete list of SAML apps — some SAML-configured apps are missing
rootCause: Apps with null PreferredSingleSignOnMode are not returned. This includes: apps created before the PreferredSingleSignOnMode API update, OAuth apps with SSO disabled, or apps that previously had SAML SSO but now have it disabled. No full SAML app list API exists (PG confirmed, planned without ETA).
solution: Use PreferredSingleSignOnMode filter for recent apps; also check samlSingleSignOnSettings.relayState attribute for apps that previously had SAML SSO. Accept that a 100% complete list cannot be obtained at this time.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Applications Experience - Get all SAML apps from tenant with PowerShell

symptom: Get-MgUser fails with 400 BadRequest 'Number of included identifiers cannot exceed 1000' when filtering users by lastSignInDateTime
rootCause: When querying users with signInActivity filter, the reporting API is called first. The reporting API incorrectly pages the response generating more than 1000 results to MSODS, which returns 400. PG is aware but no fix timeline.
solution: Use Invoke-MGGraphRequest with $top=500 and manual pagination instead of Get-MgUser filter: `Invoke-MGGraphRequest -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users?$select=userPrincipalName,displayName,signInActivity&$top=500' -Headers @{ConsistencyLevel='eventual'}` then paginate via @odata.nextLink
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Applications Experience PowerShell scripts and MG Graph queries examples

symptom: Error AADSTS650059: 'The application (appId:{appId}) is not configured for use in tenant (tenantId:{tenantId}). The value AzureADMyOrg set for application property SignInAudience is limiting its use in the tenant.' — users from external tenants fail to authenticate
rootCause: App was previously configured as multi-tenant (AzureADMultipleOrgs), users in external tenants had accessed it creating Service Principals there. Later, the app's signInAudience was changed to AzureADMyOrg (single-tenant). The SP in external tenants still exists but the home tenant app registration now only allows home tenant sign-ins. 211 whitelisted apps had July 14, 2025 deadline.
solution: If app needs to support multiple tenants: change signInAudience of the app registration in home tenant to AzureADMultipleOrgs or AzureADandPersonalMicrosoftAccount. If no access to home tenant: contact app owner to update signInAudience. If app should only work in home tenant: no action needed but external SPs will fail after enforcement date.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Authentication Method Policy

symptom: App Management Policy to block password secrets exceeding maxLifetime is not enforced — apps can still create secrets with any lifetime despite the policy being configured
rootCause: If the app or service principal was created before the date specified in restrictForAppsCreatedAfterDateTime for the passwordLifetime restriction type, the policy does not apply. Also if maxLifetime > 730 days, none of the options to create secrets less than 24 months are blocked.
solution: Ensure restrictForAppsCreatedAfterDateTime is set to a date that covers target apps. Set maxLifetime to 730 days or less for the restriction to be effective on secret creation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Authentication Method Policy

symptom: Creating an app registration fails with error: 'SignInAudience value AzureADMultipleOrgs not allowed as per assigned policy {policyId}. Set the application to use single-tenant audience of AzureADMyOrg or other allowed values. Contact your tenant admin to request an exception.'
rootCause: Tenant default App Management Policy (defaultAppManagementPolicy) is configured with audiences.azureAdMultipleOrgs.state = disabled, blocking creation of multi-tenant applications in that tenant.
solution: Either: (1) create the app as single-tenant (AzureADMyOrg) if multi-tenant is not required; or (2) contact tenant admin to request an exception via a custom appManagementPolicy with allowedAudiences configured and assigned to the specific app/SP object; or (3) tenant admin modifies the defaultAppManagementPolicy to allow the desired signInAudience.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Authentication Method Policy

symptom: Non-admin application owners cannot manage their registered applications in the Azure AD portal even though they are listed as app owners
rootCause: The 'Restrict access to Azure AD administration portal' setting is configured to 'Yes' under Azure AD → Users → User settings → Administration portal. This setting blocks all non-admin users from accessing the portal, including app owners.
solution: Set 'Restrict access to Azure AD administration portal' to 'No', or assign the user to the Directory Readers role. This gives portal access without granting additional permissions beyond the default user role. Note: Known issue — PG is aware; permanent fix planned when AAD supports customizing default user permissions (no ETA).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Management Roles

symptom: Group transform in SAML/JWT tokens fails silently with TraceCode 25729: 'Trying to emit groups as part of transform. Overage limit has been hit for groups.' — custom groups claim is not emitted in token
rootCause: User is a member of 1000+ groups, hitting the group overage limit. When overage limit is hit, the group transform is ignored. Token group limits apply even when transforms are used.
solution: Use group filtering to reduce the number of groups included in the claim (configure filter in the portal to include only a subset of user's groups). Note: if user has 1000+ groups even filtering may not help — consider restructuring group membership or using roles instead.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Migration Features Update

symptom: Only the first custom group claim is emitted in tokens; additional custom group claims configured via policy are missing. TraceCode 25721: 'Trying to emit more than 1 Custom Groups Claim as part of transform. Multiple Custom Groups Claims not permitted.'
rootCause: Azure AD enforces a limit of only one group-based custom claim per application. Any group transform after the first is ignored. The portal UI only allows one custom claim sourced from group claims; multiple would only occur via policy configuration.
solution: Configure only one custom claim sourced from group claims per application. Remove or consolidate any additional group claims transforms in the policy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Migration Features Update

symptom: Multi-value claims transformation only applies to the first value, not all values. TraceCode 29934: 'Limiting transforms on ID because shouldLimitOutputTokenSize marked as true'. Also TraceCode 34016 for Join transformation failure on multi-valued inputs.
rootCause: JWTs on implicit grant flows have limited token size; only first value is transformed to meet size constraint. For TraceCode 34016: Join transformation with multiple multi-valued input claims is not supported (multi v multi not allowed, only single v multi).
solution: Avoid implicit grant flows when using large multi-valued attributes. Switch to explicit grant flows (authorization code flow) which support larger tokens. For Join transform: ensure only one input claim is multi-valued. Review UI configuration as portal prevents multi v multi configurations.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Migration Features Update

symptom: Non-admin App Owner cannot manage their owned apps in Azure AD portal even after being explicitly assigned as App Owner
rootCause: The 'Restrict access to Azure AD administration portal' setting (Azure AD → Users → User settings → Administration portal) is set to Yes, blocking all non-admin users including App Owners from portal access
solution: Set 'Restrict access to Azure AD administration portal' to No, OR assign the user to the Directory Readers role to grant portal access without additional permissions. PG is aware but no ETA for a full fix.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Application Deactivation (Disablement)

symptom: Get-MgUser with signInActivity/lastSignInDateTime filter fails with 400 Bad Request: 'Number of included identifiers cannot exceed 1000'
rootCause: Reporting API incorrectly pages its response, generating more than 1000 results to MSODS which then returns 400. Known Product Group bug with no fix yet.
solution: Use Invoke-MGGraphRequest with Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users?$select=userPrincipalName,displayName,signInActivity&$top=500' and manually follow @odata.nextLink pagination. Avoid Get-MgUser -Filter on signInActivity.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Applications Experience PowerShell scripts and MG Graph queries examples

symptom: External tenant users receive AADSTS650059 'The application is not configured for use in tenant (tenantId)' when signing in to a previously accessible app
rootCause: App registration was converted from multi-tenant (AzureADMultipleOrgs) to single-tenant (AzureADMyOrg) AFTER Service Principals were already provisioned in external tenants. ESTS now blocks token issuance for those SPs.
solution: Change signInAudience on the app registration (in home tenant) to AzureADMultipleOrgs or AzureADandPersonalMicrosoftAccount. If customer does not own the app, contact the app owner/developer. 211 known affected apps whitelisted until 2025-07-14.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Authentication Method Policy

symptom: App Management Policy passwordLifetime restriction does not block creation of secrets beyond maxLifetime on existing apps; secrets of any lifetime can still be created
rootCause: (1) If the app/SP was created before restrictForAppsCreatedAfterDateTime, the policy logic does not apply to it. (2) If maxLifetime > 730 days, the portal options for creating secrets under 24 months will not be blocked.
solution: Set restrictForAppsCreatedAfterDateTime to a date BEFORE the target app's creation date to cover existing apps. Ensure maxLifetime is set to 730 days or fewer. Note: these policies block creation/update of credentials but do NOT block existing token issuance.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Authentication Method Policy

symptom: 'Failed to add identifier URI. All newly added URIs must contain a tenant verified domain, tenant ID, or appId, as per tenant policy' error after May 19 2025; or 'Failed to create application - SignInAudience value not allowed as per assigned policy'
rootCause: Microsoft auto-enabled the uriAdditionWithoutUniqueTenantIdentifier restriction in the tenant default app management policy on May 19 2025. Non-default identifier URIs that don't contain tenant verified domain/ID/appId are blocked.
solution: Use default URIs api://{appId} or api://{tenantId}/{appId}. Alternatively configure app for v2.0 tokens, or use SAML SSO. Admins can exempt specific apps via GrantAppExemption.ps1 or disable tenant policy via DisableIdentifierUriProtection.ps1 (not recommended). Opt-out: run DisableIdentifierUriProtection.ps1.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Authentication Method Policy

symptom: Group transform claim (TraceCode 25729) omitted from token: 'Overage limit has been hit for groups'
rootCause: User has 1000+ group memberships. When group transform is applied, the overage limit is triggered and the transform is ignored. Token group limits apply even when transforms are configured.
solution: Group filtering may not help when user has 1000+ groups. Reduce user's group membership if possible. Consider using app role assignments instead of group claims for large group memberships. Token group overage limits cannot be bypassed with transforms.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Migration Features Update

symptom: Custom claims using extension attribute not emitted in token (TraceCode 25701): 'The user claim with ExtensionID failed to emit because property was missing from collection'
rootCause: Source attribute is empty or not populated on the user object. For synced users, the attribute may not be configured for sync in AAD Connect or the on-premises user object doesn't have the value.
solution: Check user object via Graph Explorer or portal UI. For synced attributes, verify AAD Connect sync configuration includes the attribute and that the on-premises user object has the value populated. Claim will be omitted (not error) if source is empty.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Migration Features Update

symptom: After configuring Join() transformation for NameID claim, the token shows persistentId instead of the expected joined value
rootCause: Either (a) both source attributes are unpopulated on the user object (if only one is present, it's used as-is), or (b) the Join() result is in email format but the domain portion is not in the tenant's verified domains list.
solution: Check user attributes in Graph Explorer for both source attributes. Verify the domain used in the Join result is in the tenant's verified domain list (Azure AD → Custom domain names). Try using a different NameID format or a verified domain in the join.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Migration Features Update

symptom: Multi-valued claims transformation only applies to first value in token; TraceCode 29934 'Limiting transforms on ID...shouldLimitOutputTokenSize marked as true'
rootCause: JWT on implicit grant flows has a limited token size. When token size constraint is hit, only the first value of multi-valued attributes is transformed. Also occurs if multi-value flag is not explicitly enabled in claims configuration.
solution: Explicitly enable the multi-value checkbox in claims configuration for the attribute. Avoid implicit grant flows (use auth code flow) for apps requiring full multi-valued claim transforms. For ClaimsMappingPolicy, ensure only one input attribute is marked as multi-valued.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Migration Features Update

symptom: App deactivation/disablement change not taking effect or failing when performed by the App lifecycle manager agent
rootCause: Agent may not have been granted consent by app owner; app may be excluded from the batch; or service-side errors reported by the agent (visible in medenia trace logs)
solution: (1) Verify app owner consented to the agent disabling the app; (2) Verify the app was not excluded from the batch; (3) Verify the admin can manually disable from the App registration in Entra portal; (4) Examine medenia trace logging for service-side errors. If change is performed by admin directly: capture a browser trace of the failure to observe the API call error.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Application Deactivation (Disablement)

symptom: Cannot retrieve a complete list of all SAML-enabled apps from the tenant; PowerShell filter on PreferredSingleSignOnMode misses apps
rootCause: Apps with PreferredSingleSignOnMode=null are not returned: this includes apps created before the API update that implemented the attribute, OAuth apps, and apps with SSO explicitly disabled. No API currently provides a truly complete list. Per PG, this is a known limitation with no current workaround (planned without ETA).
solution: Use PowerShell script filtering on PreferredSingleSignOnMode eq 'saml' for apps with explicit SAML SSO; for potentially-SAML apps with null, additionally inspect samlSingleSignOnSettings attribute (presence of relayState suggests prior SAML SSO). Per PG, no complete enumeration feature is available.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Applications Experience - Get all SAML apps from tenant with PowerShell

symptom: Get-MgUser -Filter on signInActivity/lastSignInDateTime returns 400 BadRequest error: 'Number of included identifiers cannot exceed 1000'
rootCause: When filtering users by sign-in activity, the reporting API is invoked first and may incorrectly page the response, generating a result set exceeding 1000 identifiers to MSODS. MSODS then returns 400 BadRequest and terminates. Reporting PG is aware but no fix committed.
solution: Use Invoke-MGGraphRequest with -Uri 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users?$select=userPrincipalName,displayName,signInActivity&$top=500' with ConsistencyLevel=eventual header and implement manual pagination loop via @odata.nextLink instead of using Get-MgUser -Filter on signInActivity.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Applications Experience PowerShell scripts and MG Graph queries examples

symptom: Error AADSTS650059: 'The application (appId) is not configured for use in tenant (tenantId). The value AzureADMyOrg set for application property SignInAudience is limiting its use in the tenant'
rootCause: The app was previously configured as multi-tenant (AzureADMultipleOrgs), creating Service Principals in external tenants. The app registration was later changed to single-tenant (AzureADMyOrg). Starting July 14, 2025, ESTS enforces blocking of authentication for these SPs in external tenants. The signInAudience property on the Service Principal is read-only and cannot be updated directly.
solution: If app needs multi-tenant support: change signInAudience on the app registration in home tenant to AzureADMultipleOrgs or AzureADandPersonalMicrosoftAccount; if the app is no longer needed: delete it; if intended to be single-tenant: no action needed but SPs in external tenants will fail after 7/14/2025. If customer does not own the backing app, they must contact the app owner to make changes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Authentication Method Policy

symptom: App management policy with passwordLifetime restriction fails to block creation of secrets exceeding the configured maxLifetime
rootCause: Two conditions: (1) If the app or SP was created before the date in restrictForAppsCreatedAfterDateTime, the restriction logic is not satisfied and any lifetime is allowed; (2) If maxLifetime is greater than 730 days, none of the options to create secrets less than 24 months will be blocked by the policy.
solution: Ensure restrictForAppsCreatedAfterDateTime is configured to cover the apps in scope; ensure maxLifetime is set to 730 days or less for the passwordLifetime restriction to function correctly.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Authentication Method Policy

symptom: Non-admin App Owners cannot access or manage their own app registrations or enterprise applications through the Azure AD portal
rootCause: The 'Restrict access to Azure AD administration portal' setting is set to 'Yes' in Azure AD > Users > User Settings > Administration portal. This blocks all non-admin users from the portal, including users who are owners of specific applications. The product group is aware but POR is to address it after default user permissions customization support is added (no ETA).
solution: Option 1: Set 'Restrict access to Azure AD administration portal' to 'No' to allow all users portal access. Option 2: Assign the affected user to the Directory Readers role, which grants portal access without additional permissions beyond the default user role. Note: app owners must still use PowerShell (New-MgServicePrincipalOwnerByRef / New-MgApplicationOwnerByRef) for owner assignments.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Management Roles

symptom: AADSTS50000 'There was an error issuing a token' during sign-in for app using regex-based group transform in claims configuration
rootCause: Regex parse failure in the group transform claims mapping policy configuration. Unlike most claim failures which are silently omitted, regex parse failures cause a hard token issuance error.
solution: Review and fix the regex expression in the group transform claims mapping policy; test the regex against expected group names; ensure the regex is valid and handles edge cases. Check claims mapping policy via Get-MgBetaServiceprincipalClaimPolicy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Migration Features Update

symptom: Group claim transform silently not applied to token; groups missing from token claims; TraceCode:25729 in logs 'Overage limit has been hit for groups'
rootCause: User belongs to more than 1000 groups (overage limit). When overage occurs, the group claim transform is silently ignored and skipped rather than erroring. Token group limits apply even when group transforms are configured.
solution: Apply group filtering in the claims policy to limit which groups are included in tokens (name-based substring filter); reduce user's group memberships below 1000; note that token group overage limits still apply even with transforms enabled.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Migration Features Update

symptom: Custom group-based claim transforms beyond the first are silently ignored in token; TraceCode:25721 'Trying to emit more than 1 Custom Groups Claim'
rootCause: Entra ID only permits one group-based custom claim per application. Configuring multiple group transform claims in a policy exceeds this limit; additional claims are silently dropped. The portal UI enforces this but policy-based configurations may allow misconfiguration.
solution: Reduce to exactly one custom group-based claim per application claims configuration. Review existing claims mapping policies for the application to identify and remove duplicate group claim configurations.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Migration Features Update

symptom: Custom additional user attribute claim missing from token; TraceCode=25701 in logs: 'The user claim with ExtensionID XXX failed to emit because property was missing from collection'
rootCause: The source attribute value is missing or null on the user object. For cloud-only attributes, the attribute may not be populated. For synced attributes, the on-premises user object may not have the attribute populated or sync configuration may be misconfigured.
solution: (1) Check user object via Microsoft Graph or Entra portal to verify attribute is populated; (2) For synced attributes, verify sync configuration in Entra Connect and check on-premises user object; (3) Populate the missing attribute on the user object.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Migration Features Update

symptom: Multi-value claim transform only applied to first value (TraceCode=29934), or join transformation between two multi-value claims fails entirely (TraceCode=34016)
rootCause: TraceCode=29934: JWT tokens in implicit grant flows have limited token size; only the first value of a multi-value attribute is transformed when token size limit applies. TraceCode=34016: multi-value to multi-value join transformations are not supported by design.
solution: For TraceCode=29934: use authorization code flow instead of implicit grant to avoid token size limits. For TraceCode=34016: redesign the claims transformation to use single-value inputs for join operations; multi-value to multi-value joins are unsupported.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD App Migration Features Update

symptom: Cannot add or update credentials (keyCredentials, passwordCredentials) or tokenEncryptionKeyId on a Service Principal object in an external tenant; operation is blocked
rootCause: Application Instance Lock is enabled on the multi-tenant application by the app developer/owner in the home tenant (servicePrincipalLockConfiguration.isEnabled=true). This prevents any external tenant admin from modifying the locked sensitive properties on the Service Principal.
solution: Contact the app developer/owner in the home tenant to modify the locked properties on their side. Verify Instance Lock is the cause by running GET /beta/applications/{appObjectId} and checking servicePrincipalLockConfiguration. Sensitive properties that can be locked: keyCredentials (sign/verify usage), passwordCredentials (sign/verify usage), tokenEncryptionKeyId. ICM: Owning Service=AAD Applications, Team=AAD Application model.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/How to/Azure AD Application Instance Lock

symptom: SSO sign-in fails with AADSTS50105: User is not assigned to a role for the application; 'Assignment required' is enabled but user/group is not assigned to the Enterprise App
rootCause: The 'Assignment required' setting is enabled on the Enterprise Application but the signing-in user (or their group) has not been assigned to the app. Note: changes to this setting may not appear in Azure portal audit logs (Bug 3424404 - missing info in audit logs for 'update application' activity).
solution: 1. Assign the user or group via Enterprise Apps > Users and Groups > Assign user/group. 2. Alternatively, disable 'Assignment required' in Enterprise Apps > Properties. 3. To audit assignment changes use Kusto: GlobalIfxRestBusinessCommon | where internalCorrelationId in ("<correlationId>") | project env_time, operationName, actorId, httpStatusCode. For property-level changes use GlobalIfxUlsEvents | where tagId == "05je" | project env_time, tagId, message.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/Troubleshooting/User assignment troubleshooting flow

symptom: App registered with Personal Microsoft Account (MSA) is not visible or manageable by administrators in the customer's Entra ID directory; admins cannot find it in App Registrations or Enterprise Applications
rootCause: App was created using a Personal Microsoft Account (e.g., outlook.com, hotmail.com). Before June 2024, MSA-created apps were not tied to any organizational tenant directory — owned solely by that personal account. The App Owner Tenant ID shows as 9188040d-6c67-4c5b-b112-36a304b66dad (well-known Microsoft personal account tenant). These apps have an Application Object but no Service Principal by default.
solution: 1. Sign in to Azure portal using the MSA account that registered the app. Navigate to Entra ID > App registrations > 'Applications from personal accounts'. 2. To create a Service Principal: Connect-MgGraph -TenantId '<TENANT_ID>' then New-MgServicePrincipal -AppId '<APP_ID>'. 3. To confirm MSA ownership: Get-MgServicePrincipal -ServicePrincipalId '<SP_ID>' | FL — if AppOwnerOrganizationId is 9188040d-6c67-4c5b-b112-36a304b66dad, app is MSA-owned. 4. In sign-in logs: check App Owner Tenant ID (9188040d...) and Resource Tenant ID to confirm. Note: as of June 2024 new apps created via MSA are associated with customer's directory.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/Troubleshooting/Troubleshooting Applications Registered with Personal Microsoft Account

symptom: Customer cannot edit or rename the display name of an Enterprise Application in Azure Portal; the 'Name' field under Enterprise Applications > Properties is greyed out and cannot be modified
rootCause: The application is a multi-tenant app registered in an external (publisher's) tenant. Only the Service Principal (Enterprise Application) exists in the customer's tenant; the Application Object (App Registration), which holds the 'name' property, resides in the publisher's tenant. Since the App Registration is not local, the customer cannot modify the display name.
solution: 1. Confirm via ASC > Application > search by name > Application Object tab — if 'No data available', App Registration does not exist locally. 2. Alternatively: search the App ID under App Registrations — if it only appears under Enterprise Applications (not App Registrations), confirms no local App Object. Workarounds: (a) Register a new application in the customer's tenant to create a local App Registration with full control. (b) Add a non-gallery application manually: Enterprise Applications > + New application > Create your own application > 'Integrate any other application you don't find in the gallery (Non-gallery)' > configure SAML/OIDC manually.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/Troubleshooting/Troubleshooting Why Cannot Edit Enterprise Application Name in Azure Portal

symptom: Global Administrator (or other eligible admin) cannot delete an Enterprise Application; Delete button is greyed out with message 'You can't delete this application because you don't have the right permissions' or 'You can't delete this application because it's a Microsoft first-party application'
rootCause: The application is a Microsoft first-party application. These cannot be deleted from the UI regardless of the assigned role. Not all Microsoft-owned apps show the explicit 'first-party' message.
solution: 1. Check the 1st Party App wiki: https://supportability.visualstudio.com/AzureAD/_wiki/wikis/AzureAD/538815/Microsoft-1st-Party-Applications-List. 2. Verify via ASC: Tenant Explorer > Application > First-Party Application > search by App ID. 3. Advise customer: deleting Microsoft first-party applications is NOT recommended as it may impact service availability and cause disruptions. Confirm customer understands consequences before proceeding.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/Troubleshooting/Troubleshooting why Global Administrator not able to delete an Enterprise App

symptom: Enterprise Application Delete button is greyed out and Properties page shows message: 'This application has been disabled by Microsoft. Learn more about how to protect your organization against malicious applications'
rootCause: Microsoft has disabled the application for security reasons — it was created by a compromised user or linked to malicious activity across the Microsoft ecosystem. These apps cannot be deleted and cannot be used for authentication while in disabled state. The PG will NOT share exact detection details for security reasons.
solution: No customer-actionable deletion path exists for Microsoft-disabled apps — they are locked to protect the customer and Microsoft. The app cannot be deleted or re-enabled via the portal, PowerShell, or Graph API. Communicate to customer that the app was flagged due to security concerns and the restriction is intentional. Reference: AppExp Known issue wiki https://supportability.visualstudio.com/AzureAD/_wiki/wikis/AzureAD/471724/Azure-AD-Applications-KNOWN-ISSUES
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/Troubleshooting/Troubleshooting why Global Administrator not able to delete an Enterprise App

symptom: Cannot delete an Enterprise Application (Service Principal) from the Enterprise Apps blade; Delete button is greyed out and app shows an Azure Resource ID in its properties
rootCause: The application is associated with a Managed Identity (system-assigned or user-assigned). Managed Identity service principals are tied to an Azure resource and cannot be deleted from the Entra ID Enterprise Apps blade — they must be managed at the Azure resource level.
solution: 1. Verify via ASC: Tenant Explorer > Application > Managed Identities > search by App Name or App ID. Expanding shows the Resource ID path. 2. Guide customer to navigate to the resource via the Resource ID path (subscription > resource group > resource) to manage or delete the Managed Identity. 3. For orphaned Managed Identities (resource already deleted but SP remains): engage Entra ID Account Management team with SAP 'Azure\Managed Identities for Azure Resources\System Managed Identity'. Account Management team files an IcM to Managed Identity PG for deletion. Resource ID format example: /subscriptions/{subId}/resourcegroups/{rg}/providers/Microsoft.ManagedIdentity/userAssignedIdentities/{name}
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Application_and_Service_Principal_Object_Management/Troubleshooting/Troubleshooting why Global Administrator not able to delete an Enterprise App

symptom: SAML Token Encryption certificate upload fails in Azure Portal or via Graph API; error occurs when trying to configure token encryption on an Enterprise Application
rootCause: Certificate is in the wrong format. The public key certificate must be in X.509 format saved as a .cer file (Base64 encoded). Common issues: PEM vs DER encoding mismatch, private key included in the upload file, or certificate chain included instead of leaf certificate only.
solution: 1. Ensure the certificate is in .cer format (X.509, Base64 encoded public key only — no private key). 2. For Graph API upload: encode the certificate as Base64 and set type='AsymmetricX509Cert', usage='Encrypt'. 3. Generate a key pair using cryptography tools if needed; only upload the public key (.cer) to Entra ID. 4. Use Fiddler to inspect the upload request if portal upload fails. 5. Note: Token Encryption menu is only available for apps set up from Enterprise Apps blade — for App Registration apps, use Graph API PATCH on /beta/applications/{objectId}.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/How to/Azure AD SAML Token Encryption

symptom: SAML token is sent encrypted but the application cannot decrypt it; authentication fails after SAML token encryption is enabled; token encryption appears active but SSO does not work
rootCause: The tokenEncryptionKeyId on the application object is not set correctly, does not match the keyId of the uploaded encryption certificate, or there is a key mismatch between the public key uploaded to Entra ID and the private key stored in the application.
solution: 1. Verify tokenEncryptionKeyId matches the keyId of the active encryption certificate: GET https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/applications/{objectId} and compare tokenEncryptionKeyId with keyCredentials[].keyId where usage='Encrypt'. 2. If mismatch: PATCH tokenEncryptionKeyId with the correct GUID. 3. Verify the application has the correct matching private key installed. 4. Run Kusto EVO diagnostic query filtering by CorrelationId and look for 'TokenEncryptionKeyID' in the Message column to trace encryption usage. 5. Use Fiddler to inspect the SAML response — token content should be encrypted; decrypt using the private key to verify.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/How to/Azure AD SAML Token Encryption

symptom: Third-party application requires the same SAML Identifier (Entity ID / Audience) to be configured across multiple Entra ID SAML Enterprise Applications; Entra ID rejects duplicate Identifier with error
rootCause: By design, Entra ID does not allow the same Identifier (Entity ID) to be used across multiple SAML applications in the same tenant. This is a platform constraint for multi-instance SAML SP scenarios.
solution: 1. Create two separate SAML Enterprise Applications in Entra ID. 2. First app: configure the actual Identifier that matches what the SP sends. 3. Second app: configure a DIFFERENT Identifier (e.g., append '-instance2'), then use Advanced SAML claims settings to OVERRIDE the audience value to match the SP's required audience URI. 4. Modify the metadata file for the second app: (a) Change entityID at top to include the App ID. (b) Change HTTP-Redirect and HTTP-Post binding URLs to include the App ID. This routes SAML AuthnRequests to the specific application regardless of the issuer parameter sent by the SP. Provide the modified metadata to the SP. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/configure-app-multi-instancing and https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/saml-claims-customization#advanced-saml-claims-options
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/How to/SAML Multi-Instancing

symptom: Calling OIDC UserInfo endpoint fails with AADSTS9001014: 'This token was not issued for the UserInfo endpoint. This may have been a token for Graph or another resource.'
rootCause: Token was issued targeting the wrong endpoint version. V1 token used with V2 UserInfo endpoint (https://graph.microsoft.com/oidc/userinfo) or V2 token used with V1 endpoint. The audience (aud) in the token does not match the target UserInfo endpoint.
solution: Match token version to UserInfo endpoint: V1 → obtain token via /oauth2/token, call https://login.microsoftonline.com/common/openid/userinfo; V2 → obtain token via /oauth2/v2.0/token with openid scope, call https://graph.microsoft.com/oidc/userinfo. Use openid-configuration metadata endpoint to discover the correct userinfo_endpoint.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Labs/LAB - Retrieving user information from Entra ID UserInfo Endpoints

symptom: OIDC UserInfo endpoint call fails with AADSTS9002313: 'Invalid request. Request is malformed or invalid.'
rootCause: Authorization header is malformed: double space between 'Bearer' and token value, no space at all, or incorrect header name.
solution: Ensure Authorization header is correctly formatted: 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' with exactly one space between 'Bearer' and the token. Verify header name is exactly 'Authorization'.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Labs/LAB - Retrieving user information from Entra ID UserInfo Endpoints

symptom: Client credentials flow using certificate-based authentication fails with authentication error when acquiring token.
rootCause: Certificate thumbprint specified in the authentication script does not match the thumbprint of the certificate registered in the app registration or present in the certificate store.
solution: Verify thumbprint using PowerShell: Get-ChildItem -Path Cert:\CurrentUser\My\ | Where-Object -Property Thumbprint -EQ "<thumbprint>" or list all certs with Get-ChildItem -Path Cert:\CurrentUser\My\. Ensure the thumbprint used in the script exactly matches the certificate registered in Azure Portal under Certificates & Secrets.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Labs/LAB - Testing Client Credential Flow using Certificate

symptom: Certificate-based client credentials authentication suddenly fails or stops working after previously functioning correctly.
rootCause: Certificate used for authentication has expired or been revoked.
solution: Verify certificate validity: certutil -verify -urlfetch <certificatename> or certutil -URL <certificatename>. If expired/revoked, generate a new certificate, upload the public key to the app registration in Azure Portal under Certificates & Secrets, and update scripts to reference the new certificate.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Labs/LAB - Testing Client Credential Flow using Certificate

symptom: Client credentials flow using certificate fails at token acquisition; JWT client_assertion validation error.
rootCause: Token endpoint URL in the authentication script is incorrect or uses the wrong version; the JWT client_assertion 'aud' claim must match the token endpoint URL used.
solution: Use correct token endpoint format: V2 → https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/oauth2/v2.0/token; V1 → https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/oauth2/token. Ensure JWT client_assertion 'aud' claim matches the endpoint URL exactly (e.g., aud: 'https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/oauth2/token' for V1).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Labs/LAB - Testing Client Credential Flow using Certificate

symptom: User with an active Entra ID SSO session is unexpectedly prompted for re-authentication (password prompt) when accessing a specific SAML application; no specific error is generated and the issue is application-specific.
rootCause: The SAML AuthnRequest from the service provider (SP) contains ForceAuthn="true" parameter, which instructs Entra ID (IdP) to bypass any existing session and force fresh re-authentication regardless of active SSO state.
solution: Capture the SAML AuthnRequest using browser trace, SAML-tracer extension, or Fiddler. Verify ForceAuthn value via Azure Support Center (ASC) Advanced Troubleshooting sign-in analysis. If ForceAuthn="true" is confirmed, advise customer to contact their application team or third-party vendor to set ForceAuthn=false or remove the optional parameter from the SAML AuthnRequest configuration.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Labs/LAB - Understanding The ForceAuthn Parameter In A SAML Request

symptom: AADSTS error related to missing or unrecognized grant_type when using Insomnia application to retrieve an OAuth 2.0 token from Entra ID token endpoint.
rootCause: Insomnia request body defaults to a non-Form Data encoding format; the token endpoint requires parameters to be sent as application/x-www-form-urlencoded (Form Data), and the grant_type parameter is not recognized in other body formats.
solution: In Insomnia, select the request body type dropdown and change it to 'Form Data'. This ensures parameters (including grant_type) are sent in the correct application/x-www-form-urlencoded format expected by the Entra ID token endpoint.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Labs/LAB - how to use Insomnia application to test OAuth2.0 Authentication flows

symptom: Test SAML SSO wizard in Azure Portal shows an error for a SAML-configured application, but actual end-user SAML SSO works correctly. Issue occurs when customer has configured multiple AWS application instances following Microsoft public documentation.
rootCause: Known product issue: the Test SAML SSO wizard produces misleading errors when multiple AWS application instances are configured per Microsoft public documentation. Product Group is aware of the issue (noted Aug 2021), no ETA on fix provided.
solution: Confirm actual SAML SSO functions correctly for end users by testing directly (not via the Test SAML wizard). Reassure customer the Test SAML SSO wizard error is a known cosmetic/tool issue and does not impact real SSO functionality. Escalate to PG if urgent.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Labs/LAB - using Test SAML settings button in Azure Portal

symptom: Custom authentication extension sign-in fails with error 1003005 (CustomExtensionTimedOut) — the API did not respond within the allowed timeout
rootCause: Custom extension API failed to respond within the 2-second timeout limit; common causes: API cold-start on non-warm tier, no caching of access tokens or MS Graph data, synchronous downstream calls that should be parallelized
solution: Use always-warm tier for hosted API (e.g., Azure Functions Premium); cache access tokens for downstream services; cache MS Graph data that doesn't need real-time updates; parallelize serial downstream calls; test performance with ROPC+Postman; note: 2-second timeout with ONE retry means two sequential failures = error
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Troubleshooting/Azure AD Custom authentication extension TSG

symptom: Sign-in fails with error 1003002 (CustomExtensionInvalidHTTPStatus / ExternalApiResponseStatusNotSuccess) — custom extension returned invalid/unsupported HTTP status code or response has wrong Content-Type
rootCause: API returned an HTTP status code not in the accepted list for the custom extension type; OR response Content-Type is not 'application/json' — confirmed by error description: 'Make sure the Content-Type is set to application/json'
solution: Ensure the custom extension API returns a valid accepted HTTP status code; set response Content-Type header to 'application/json'; check MaxSupportedContentLength (1MB) is not exceeded
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Troubleshooting/Azure AD Custom authentication extension TSG

symptom: Sign-in fails with error 1003003 (CustomExtensionInvalidResponseBody / ExternalApiResponseParsingError) — 'The @odata.type required field is missing' or failed to parse JSON response
rootCause: Custom extension API response body is missing the required '@odata.type' field, or the response body does not conform to the expected schema for the custom extension type
solution: Add '@odata.type' field to the API response JSON body; validate the complete response schema against the expected contract for the specific event type (e.g., onTokenIssuanceStart); use the troubleshoot guide at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/custom-extension-troubleshoot
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Troubleshooting/Azure AD Custom authentication extension TSG

symptom: Sign-in fails with error 1003006 (CustomExtensionInvalidResponseContentType) — custom extension response Content-Type is not 'application/json'
rootCause: The HTTP response from the custom extension API does not set Content-Type to 'application/json'
solution: Set the Content-Type response header to 'application/json' in the custom extension API response
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Troubleshooting/Azure AD Custom authentication extension TSG

symptom: Custom extension configuration fails with error 1003014 or 1003015 (IntermediateAccessTokenFQDNValidationFailed) — ResourceId format invalid or FQDN mismatch between ResourceId and targetUrl
rootCause: Application ID URI (ResourceId) must be in format 'api://{fqdn}/{appid}' or 'https://{fqdn}/{appid}'; the FQDN in the ResourceId must exactly match the FQDN of the custom extension targetUrl — this prevents a malicious endpoint from obtaining tokens intended for another API
solution: Update the Application ID URI of the resource app registration to use the same FQDN as the targetUrl (e.g., targetUrl: 'https://subdomain.api.com/...' → App ID URI: 'api://subdomain.api.com/{appid}'); IMPORTANT: when changing domain, update the App ID URI FIRST before changing the targetUrl
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Troubleshooting/Azure AD Custom authentication extension TSG

symptom: Custom authentication extension sign-in fails with error 1003021 (CustomExtensionPermissionNotGrantedToServicePrincipal) — service principal lacks required permission
rootCause: The service principal of the custom extension handler app does not have admin consent for the Microsoft Graph 'CustomExtensionsPayload.Receive.All' (CustomAuthenticationExtensions.Receive.Payload) application permission
solution: Grant admin consent for 'CustomExtensionsPayload.Receive.All' (Microsoft Graph) application permission on the service principal of the custom handler app registration; this is required for the app to receive custom authentication extension HTTP requests from Entra ID
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Troubleshooting/Azure AD Custom authentication extension TSG

symptom: Custom authentication extension sign-in fails with error 1003027 (ExternalApiDnsResolutionError) — DNS exception when calling the external API endpoint
rootCause: The hostname in the custom extension's targetUrl cannot be resolved via DNS; typically a misconfigured URL or network/DNS infrastructure issue
solution: Verify the targetUrl in the custom extension endpoint configuration is correct and the hostname is resolvable; have customer check their network DNS configuration for the specific hostname; try resolving the URL from a machine in the same network context
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Troubleshooting/Azure AD Custom authentication extension TSG

symptom: Browser shows 'Script Error: An error has occurred in the script on this page / Line: 51 / Error: Failed to load external resource [aadcdn.msauthimages.net/...]' when attempting to authenticate or register MFA
rootCause: Application is launching authentication within a legacy embedded browser (e.g., an Electron or Win32 app's embedded IE WebView); MFA Registration process is not supported within embedded browsers
solution: Direct the user to complete MFA registration at https://myapps.microsoft.com using a standard web browser (Edge, Chrome) instead of the embedded browser; workaround only: disable script error notifications via Control Panel → Internet Options → Advanced → uncheck 'Display a notification about every script error' — this hides the error but does not fix the underlying issue; if occurring in Edge/Chrome (not embedded), raise an IcM with Entra ID Authentication/App experience team (reference CDN Download errors ICM pattern)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Troubleshooting/Error An error occurred in the script on this page

symptom: Authentication fails with AADSTS50011 error — 'The reply URL / redirect URI does not match the redirect URIs configured for the application'
rootCause: The Reply URL (SAML) or redirect_uri (OAuth2/OIDC) in the authentication request does not match any of the configured values in the app registration; common for misconfigured SaaS apps or when URLs include trailing slashes, different casings, or query strings
solution: For OAuth2/OIDC: align the redirect_uri in the request with app registration Redirect URIs per https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/entra-id/app-integration/error-code-aadsts50011-redirect-uri-mismatch; for SAML: align the Reply URL (ACS URL) per https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/entra-id/app-integration/error-code-aadsts50011-reply-url-mismatch; also check internal wiki AADSTS50011 article (Supportability wiki page 311747) for additional guidance
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD.wiki:/Authentication/Entra ID App Management/Authentication_flows SAML_and_OAuth/Troubleshooting/ReplyURL troubleshooting flow

symptom: Calling OIDC UserInfo endpoint returns AADSTS9001014: 'This token was not issued for the UserInfo endpoint'
