rootCause: Two blades use different APIs with different detection logic: User blade uses /users/{id}/authentication/methods, Registration Details uses /reports/authenticationMethods/userRegistrationDetails
solution: Compare both Graph API endpoints. For phone discrepancies check mobile/telephoneNumber attributes. Escalate to IDX/Reporting or Credential Enforcement/Triage.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Usage & insights/TSG - User Registration details report mismatch with the User Blade - Authentication Methods

symptom: User registered authentication method not appearing in User Registration Details report
rootCause: User Registration Details is not real-time; updates take up to 36 hours, rarely longer
solution: Wait at least 60 hours. Check Audit logs for timestamp. If still missing, submit IcM to IDX/Reporting and Auditing Insights.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Usage & insights/TSG - User Registration details report mismatch with the User Blade - Authentication Methods

symptom: User registered security questions but not detected as registered method for SSPR
rootCause: Number of registered security questions less than NumberOfSecurityQuestionsToRegister in SSPR policy
solution: Advise customer to register sufficient security questions per policy threshold.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Usage & insights/TSG - Why a user is detected as SSPRcapable (or not)?

symptom: User has fewer MethodRegistered than required but is still detected as SSPR capable
rootCause: Phone numbers from mobile, telephoneNumber, OfficeOnlyPhoneNumber count for SSPR but not shown in methodRegistered. String values like N/A or Empty are not null and count as registered.
solution: Check users mobile and telephoneNumber attributes. Remove unexpected N/A or Empty values if they should not count.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Usage & insights/TSG - Why a user is detected as SSPRcapable (or not)?

symptom: userCredentialUsageDetails Graph API returns empty response with no data
rootCause: API only returns SSPR usage data. If tenant has no SSPR activity, response is empty by design.
solution: Verify SSPR activity in Audit logs. If activity exists but API empty, escalate IcM to IDX/Reporting and Audit Insights with TA approval.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Usage & insights/TSG - Why userCredentialUsageDetails API has no returns

symptom: Application uses deprecated VM extension for managed identities and fails to obtain tokens or encounters issues after extension was removed
rootCause: The VM extension for managed identities was officially deprecated in January 2019. Applications relying on it will not work correctly.
solution: Configure applications to call the /identity endpoint of Infrastructure Metadata Service (IMDS) running on the Azure host instead of using the VM extension. IMDS automatically cleans up credentials and identity when the VM is deleted.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Managed Identities (MSI)/Azure AD Managed Identities (MSI)

symptom: Unable to edit or create new Conditional Access policies for workload identities; existing policies cannot be modified
rootCause: Workload Identities Premium license (or 90-day trial) has expired. After expiration, existing CA policies continue to function but cannot be edited, and new ones cannot be created.
solution: Purchase Workload Identities Premium licenses ($3/workload identity/month) or activate a new trial from the Microsoft 365 admin center. Check license status via Graph API: List subscribedSkus.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Managed Identities (MSI)/Microsoft Entra Workload Identities

symptom: System assigned managed identity keeps turning back on automatically after being disabled on a VM via Portal, PowerShell, or Azure CLI
rootCause: An Azure Policy (e.g. Add system-assigned managed identity to enable Guest Configuration assignments on VMs) is enforcing system-assigned managed identity on VMs in the subscription scope.
solution: Check Activity Log on the VM resource for policy-related operations after disabling MSI. Identify the Azure Policy assignment ID enforcing system-assigned identity. Remove the policy assignment or exclude the VM from the policy. Use ARM Kusto query on armprodeus cluster to trace policy evaluation with isComplianceCheck.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Managed Identities (MSI)/TSG - Unable to delete or turn off a System Assigned Managed Identity

symptom: Unable to delete a SystemAssigned Managed Identity Enterprise Application with Insufficient Permissions error
rootCause: SystemAssigned managed identities cannot be directly deleted from Enterprise Applications. Their lifecycle is tied to the Azure resource that created them.
solution: Navigate to the Azure resource blade (VM, App Service etc) > Identity tab > turn off System Assigned identity. If the source resource no longer exists, use ASC Tenant Explorer to find managedIdentityResourceId in SP metadata, verify resource status in Resource Explorer, and file ICM to Managed Service Identity/Triage team for backend removal (requires TA approval).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Managed Identities (MSI)/TSG - Unable to delete or turn off a System Assigned Managed Identity

symptom: Azure Policy remediation task for assigning user-assigned managed identity to VMs fails with AuthorizationFailed error: does not have authorization to perform action Microsoft.Resources/deployments/write
rootCause: The user-assigned managed identity has Managed Identity Operator and User Access Administrator at resource group level but lacks Contributor role at the subscription level where policies are deployed.
solution: Add the user-assigned managed identity to the Contributor role at the subscription level and create a new remediation task on the policy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Managed Identities (MSI)/Use Azure Policy to Assign Managed Identities

symptom: Azure Policy remediation deployment fails with InvalidTemplate error: The name property cannot be null or empty when using Bring-your-own-User-assigned-Managed identity policy
rootCause: The Only show parameters that need input or review checkbox was not unchecked on the Parameters tab during policy assignment, hiding the required User-Assigned Managed Identity Name and Resource Group Name fields.
solution: Edit the policy assignment > Parameters tab > uncheck Only show parameters that need input or review > populate User-Assigned Managed Identity Name and Resource Group Name fields > Review + save > create a new Remediation Task.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Managed Identities (MSI)/Use Azure Policy to Assign Managed Identities

symptom: User account locked out on Azure AD with error code 50053 (IdsLocked); Smart Lockout triggered
rootCause: Azure AD Smart Lockout detected too many failed password attempts. Uses FamiliarLocation algorithm (IP, lat/long, ASN) to maintain separate counters for familiar vs unfamiliar locations. When failure count exceeds threshold, RamRecommenderLockout issues block.
solution: Check Smart Lockout settings in ASC > Tenant Explorer > Password Management > Password Protection. Review sign-in logs filtered by error 50053, click Troubleshoot this Sign-In > RamLogData to see lockout details. Use Kusto query on ESTS clusters with PUID to check RamRecommendedAction/RamRecommender/RamLogData. Default thresholds: 10 failed attempts (Public) / 3 (US Gov), 60s duration. Customization requires AAD Premium P1+.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Password Protection for On-Premise/Azure AD Smart Lockout

symptom: On-premises AD accounts being locked out due to cloud password spray/brute-force attacks when using Passthrough Authentication (PTA)
rootCause: Azure AD Smart Lockout and on-premises AD account lockout policies are not configured per best practices. The AD lockout threshold is not sufficiently higher than AAD Smart Lockout threshold, causing PTA to forward failed attempts to on-prem AD before cloud lockout triggers.
solution: Configure: 1) On-prem AD account lockout threshold to be 2-3x greater than AAD Smart Lockout threshold. 2) On-prem AD lockout duration to be less than AAD Smart Lockout duration. 3) Enable password writeback for smart lockout reset. Check ASC Auth Troubleshooter expert view for CredValidationMthd=PTA and OnPremPasswordValidationInfo to determine lockout source.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Password Protection for On-Premise/TSG: Smart Lockout and Passthrough Authentication

symptom: Downloading sign-in logs via Azure portal fails due to throttling (429). The portal download feature fails the entire download if a single request gets throttled.
rootCause: Portal UX lacks retry logic for throttled requests when downloading sign-in data. Feature request backlogged with no ETA.
solution: Workarounds: 1) Stream sign-in logs to Azure Monitor or Azure Storage and download from there. 2) Use PowerShell or Graph API with built-in throttle handling. 3) Reduce time range to smaller increments (e.g., 7-day or 1-day chunks).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/Known Issues

symptom: Failure Reason in AAD Sign-In logs shows variable placeholders like {tenantName}, {identifier} instead of actual values.
rootCause: By design. Resolving variables requires major integration between Reporting and ESTS teams. No planned work.
solution: Inform customer this is a known limitation. Refer to MS Learn docs on sign-in error codes for additional context.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/Known Issues

symptom: Audit logs for actions initiated by Microsoft Online Services (e.g., Add service principal, Delete external user, Update user) have no "initiated by" field populated.
rootCause: Known issue. These actions are performed by Microsoft Online Services backend and the actor field is not populated.
solution: Inform customer this is a known limitation for system-initiated operations. Feature request tracked (1056502).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/Known Issues

symptom: Sign-in log entries appear as "invisible" in the portal but show up when filtering by specific error code or CA status.
rootCause: By design. The system always surfaces the final error code for a given requestId. Filtering on intermediate interrupt codes will not show the final outcome.
solution: Search by requestId without other filters to get the fully aggregated record showing the final status.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/Known Issues

symptom: Sign-in log shows Success but Conditional Access policy requiring compliant app shows as Failure. Interactive sign-in shows CA not satisfied, but non-interactive sign-in with same correlation ID shows CA success.
rootCause: By design. Compliant app can only be evaluated at the Token endpoint. At Authorize call, there is no way to determine if the app is compliant. The interactive log aggregates the Authorize call (CA unsatisfied), while the Token endpoint (CA satisfied) is aggregated as a separate non-interactive sign-in.
solution: Check non-interactive sign-in logs with the same correlation ID. The CA status should show as success there. Look for ESTS message: "Interactive request for AuthzCode only: deferring MicrosoftEnrollmentId challenge to token endpoint call".
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/Known Issues

symptom: RiskEvent property (riskEventTypes_v2) is empty/null when querying sign-in logs via Graph API or portal, but the same sign-in event in Azure Monitor/Sentinel shows riskEventTypes_v2 populated. riskDetail shows as "hidden".
rootCause: By design for tenants with free (non-premium) subscription. Sign-in logs from Entra will not show risk properties for free tenants. A bug causes risk data to be shipped to Sentinel/Monitor for these tenants.
solution: Verify if customer has a free subscription. If so, this is expected for Entra portal/Graph but a bug for the Sentinel/Monitor side. Add case to Bug 3236474.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/Known Issues

symptom: Service principal sign-in shows CA policy as Not Applied even though a blocking CA policy exists. Two request IDs appear, one with CA info (from EstsRegionalCache) and one without.
rootCause: ESTS-R requests do not evaluate CA directly; they replay from ESTS-G. ALP redaction logic incorrectly drops CA details from ESTS-R requests. Bug 3232779.
solution: Known bug. Work item created to re-evaluate ALP redaction logic with ESTS-X team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/Known Issues

symptom: Downloading CSV for Application Sign In logs produces error: "File download error - Given data uri is not formatted correctly with data uri syntax".
rootCause: Known bug in the new sign-in logs portal UX (Bug 3383961).
solution: Use the link at the top of the blade to switch to the "legacy signin logs experience" where download works. Do not open ICM.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/Known Issues

symptom: Audit logs show incorrect Client IP Address (gateway IP instead of original client IP) or missing IP address.
rootCause: Known issue. The reported IP was sometimes the gateway IP of the Azure Service instead of the original client IP.
solution: Known limitation tracked by Epic 2483429. No current workaround for missing/incorrect IPs in audit logs.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/Known Issues

symptom: Get-AzureADAuditSignInLogs or Graph API filtering by userPrincipalName returns no results when UPN contains uppercase letters.
rootCause: UPN filtering is case sensitive and UPNs are stored in lowercase in the database.
solution: Use all lowercase for UPN filters. PowerShell: $upn = "UPN".ToLower(). Graph API: use tolower() in filter. E.g., $filter=userPrincipalName eq tolower("John.Smith@contoso.com").
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/Known Issues

symptom: Gateway Timeout (HTTP 504) when calling Get-AzureADAuditSignInLogs, loading sign-in logs in portal, or using Graph API. Azure Gateway returns 504 as Reporting workload exceeds 30s timeout.
rootCause: Large queries to the reporting database experience longer processing times, especially with large event volumes.
solution: Modify query filter to reduce returned events. Use shorter createdDateTime ranges. Iterate through timespan. Check Gateway logs: cluster(aadgwwst).database(AADGatewayProd).AllRequestSummaryEvents.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/Known Issues

symptom: Calling users?$filter=signInActivity with large $top value (999 or 600) returns 400 BadRequest: "Number of included identifiers cannot exceed 1000".
rootCause: CosmosDB returns fewer records than $top requests, causing Reporting to send subsequent calls until total exceeds 1000.
solution: Use a lower $top value, ideally under 500. Bug 3173874 tracks the fix.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/Known Issues

symptom: Last sign-in date (signInActivity/lastSignInDateTime) is not getting updated after 24 hours. Users show stale timestamps.
rootCause: During peak traffic, upstream systems have varying delays in logs used for last sign-in calculations. Redesign planned.
solution: Known issue actively tracked. Feature 3379692, Bug 3244861. Full resolution requires service redesign.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/Known Issues

symptom: Filtering sign-in logs by userPrincipalName shows the same requestId appearing for two different users.
rootCause: By design. Sign-in logs are aggregated by correlation. If client presents a rejected token for userA then re-authenticates as userB, the final aggregated event shows userB. Explicit UPN filtering returns intermediate events.
solution: This is expected behavior. Without explicit userName filtering, only the final aggregated event is shown. Same applies to IP address filtering.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/TSG - Duplicated request Ids for different sign-in log of two different users

symptom: Sign-in logs show correct IP address but wrong geographic location (e.g., user in Japan shown as San Jose, California, US).
rootCause: IP-to-location mapping issue. May originate from ESTS source data or from Reporting publishing.
solution: Check ASC > Troubleshoot this sign-in > Expert View > RamLogData tab > search RequestLocationData. If country/city/state match the sign-in log, Reporting published correctly from source. Follow Azure AD IP Location Mismatch wiki for further troubleshooting.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/TSG - Troubleshoot IP Address Location Mismatch

symptom: Customer sees "Set Company Information" audit log entries performed by a Global Admin account without their knowledge.
rootCause: When a Rave engineer runs a troubleshooting flow, a backend process impersonates a random Global Admin to obtain tenant info. The audit trail is incorrectly labeled as "Set Company Information" instead of "Set Application Data".
solution: Verify by running Kusto query on GlobalIfxUlsEvents checking for "SetApplicationData" with matching correlationId and tenant. Look for CN=boxuserimp.m365admin.office.com. If confirmed, provide statement: "Benign internal operation when domain health is checked by support agents." If not accepted, open ICM to Office365 Admin Portal > Support Central.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/TSG - Troubleshoot Set Company Information audit log

symptom: Customer gets "Too Many Requests" (HTTP 429) when querying Reporting API for sign-in logs, audit logs, or auth methods reports.
rootCause: Throttling by Gateway or Reporting API. Limit: 5 requests per 10 seconds for most report types, 10 requests per minute for signInActivity.
solution: Check source using Kusto: cluster(aadgwwst).database(AADGatewayProd).AllRequestSummaryEvents where IsThrottled=true means Gateway throttling. Mitigation: reduce call frequency, add filters, use smaller date ranges. Follow MS Graph throttling guidance.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/TSG - Troubleshoot Throttling Issue

symptom: "Update company" audit log shows company display name changed but no actor ("initiated by" is empty).
rootCause: Known issue. "Update company" event has no actor but has modified properties. "Set Company Information" event has actor but no modified properties. Feature request 1200929.
solution: Check if both "Update company" and "Set Company Information" entries exist at similar timestamps - actor from Set Company Information made the change (via Office portal). If no Set Company Info entry, check Jarvis query (requires SAW/AME). Also check Commerce via Jarvis.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Logs and Reporting/Azure AD Reporting Workflow/TSG - Troubleshoot Update Company audit log

symptom: Error AADB2C90080: Refresh token grant expired
rootCause: Refresh token expired/revoked
solution: Request new auth code
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AADB2C Errors/AADB2C90080

symptom: Error AADB2C90088: Grant not issued for this endpoint
rootCause: Token for different endpoint
solution: Use correct endpoint
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AADB2C Errors/AADB2C90088

symptom: Error AADB2C90167: Signing cert has no private key
rootCause: Cert lacks private key
solution: Upload PFX with private key
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AADB2C Errors/AADB2C90167

symptom: Error AADB2C90240: Token malformed - OIDC signature fail
rootCause: Key mismatch with external IdP
solution: Verify IdP metadata and keys
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AADB2C Errors/AADB2C90240

symptom: Error AADB2C90240: Intermittent signature failure
rootCause: Key rotation/metadata cache
solution: Re-upload policy or wait for refresh
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AADB2C Errors/AADB2C90240

symptom: Error AADB2C90289: IdP connection timeout
rootCause: Network issue to external IdP
solution: Check reachability and firewall
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AADB2C Errors/AADB2C90289

symptom: Error AADB2C90289: IdP TLS handshake failure
rootCause: TLS mismatch or cert chain issue
solution: Ensure TLS 1.2+ and valid chain
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AADB2C Errors/AADB2C90289

symptom: Error AADB2C90289: IdP DNS resolution failure
rootCause: DNS cannot resolve hostname
solution: Verify hostname and DNS
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/AADB2C Errors/AADB2C90289

symptom: B2C SSO not working after password reset - SSO cookie lacks claims
rootCause: Password reset UserJourney missing SM-AAD enlistment
solution: Add OrchestrationStep with CT-EnlistIntoSM-AAD referencing SM-AAD

rootCause: Hidden BOM/space at file start

symptom: Tenant deletion blocked by CMAT Validate+Delete error: Tenant [*] is listed as a partner. Partner mapping prevents tenant deletion.
rootCause: Partner mapping exists on the tenant in OMS/MSODS. The tenant is registered as a CSP partner (BreadthPartnerDelegatedAdmin or ResellerPartnerDelegatedAdmin) with associated relationships.
solution: Verify no CSP relationships exist via CMAT, CST, Partner Center SMRT Tool, and DS Explorer. Remove all customer relationships first (partner must use Partner Center). Then remove Reseller tags in OMS Tenant Console Tags tab. Finally remove partner mapping in OMS Console MSODS view tab. Re-run CMAT Validate+Delete to confirm blocker removed. If CSP partner is non-responsive, follow CSP Policy Decision Tree for TA-assisted removal using Geneva Actions.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Azure AD Directory Deletion/Tenant Deletion - Removing CSP Partner Mapping

symptom: CMAT shows tenant in Release state but tenant still exists in MSODS/AAD and is not deleted. Customer cannot complete self-service tenant deletion.
rootCause: Orphaned subscriptions remain in MSODS directory that block tenant deletion even though commerce layer shows tenant as released. MSODS subscription objects with status != 3 (Deleted) prevent deletion.
solution: Query MSODS for orphaned subscriptions using D2K Kusto (idsharedwus.westus cluster, d2kredacted database) or DS Explorer. Check SubscriptionStatus: 0=Enabled, 1=Warning, 2=Suspended, 3=Deleted. Any subscription NOT in status 3 blocks deletion. Use OMS Admin Console to fast deprovision after customer GA email authorization.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Azure AD Directory Deletion/Tenant Deletion - Resolving Tenant Deletion Blockers using OMS Admin Console

symptom: CMAT shows blocking subscription in Disabled status but Immediate Cancellation is unavailable. Cannot fast deprovision subscription to unblock tenant deletion.
rootCause: Modern commerce subscriptions (CST-managed) cannot be fast deprovisioned through CST. The subscription has not met its deprovision lifecycle date yet.
solution: Use OMS/BOSG Admin Console as last resort. Prerequisites: 1) Verify CMAT cannot fast deprovision, 2) Escalate to ASMS TAs + Azure Commerce EEE team, 3) Verify subscription is in Disabled (not Active) status. Requires SAW + AME.GBL credentials.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Azure AD Directory Deletion/Tenant Deletion - Resolving Tenant Deletion Blockers using OMS Admin Console

symptom: Applications fail to authenticate with AADSTS90002 Tenant not found error after Microsoft Cloud Deutschland (Blackforest) to Global cloud migration. Blackforest endpoints (login.microsoftonline.de) stop working.
rootCause: Tenant migrated to Stage 5 (Phase 10) where blackforest endpoints are decommissioned. Applications still using old endpoints instead of public cloud endpoints. Stage 4 to Stage 5 transition is automatic after 30 days.
solution: Customer must update all Authentication, Azure AD Graph, and MS Graph endpoints to public cloud endpoints. CSS can request rollback from Stage 5 to Stage 4 for 15 additional days. Check migration stage via BF OIDC discovery endpoint: https://login.microsoftonline.de/{tenantId}/.well-known/openid-configuration
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Azure AD Migration from Microsoft Cloud Deutschland (blackforest) to Global cloud

symptom: D2K Kusto query for MSODS subscription objects returns stale/outdated data. Subscription status does not reflect recent changes.
rootCause: D2K Kusto data (idsharedwus.westus/d2kredacted) is a snapshot of MSODS directory state taken once daily at midnight UTC. Not real-time.
solution: For live data, contact Azure Identity TA to check DS Explorer. D2K requires CLD-D2KRedacted-MSIT-R core entitlement.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Azure AD Directory Deletion/Tenant Deletion - Query Kusto for MSODS Subscriptions

symptom: Failed to update user with 403 Forbidden via Microsoft Graph despite having User.ReadWrite.All or Directory.ReadWrite.All, when modifying sensitive properties like accountEnabled, mobilePhone, otherMails for privileged admin users
rootCause: Sensitive user properties (accountEnabled, mobilePhone, otherMails) for users with privileged admin roles require the calling principal to have a higher privileged administrator role. MSODS IfxBECAuthorizationManager log shows task DENIED (e.g. SetUserMobileProperty).
solution: For delegated scenarios: assign Directory.AccessAsUser.All permission and ensure calling user has higher privileged admin role than target user (per 'Who can perform sensitive actions' docs). For app-only scenarios: assign the app a higher privileged admin role. Use MSODS IfxBECAuthorizationManager Kusto query to identify which specific task was DENIED.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/TSG - 403 unable to modify user attribute like accontEnabled, mobilePhone

symptom: 403 Forbidden error when updating onPremisesImmutableId attribute via Microsoft Graph API or Update-MgUser PowerShell cmdlet
rootCause: Updating onPremisesImmutableId is classified as a sensitive action (UpdateSourceAnchorOnDirSyncUser task). For cloud-only users, appropriate admin roles + permissions are needed. For synced users, only Global Administrator can perform this update.
solution: For cloud-only users: use User.ReadWrite.All application permission (app-only) or Directory.AccessAsUser.All delegated permission with appropriate admin role per sensitive actions table. For synced users: only Global Administrator can update. When using Update-MgUser, pass scope Directory.AccessAsUser.All to use UserIdentity flow.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/TSG - 403 when updating onpremisesImmutableId

symptom: Error 'One or more property values specified are invalid' when PATCHing a value to a custom extension property via Microsoft Graph or Update-MgUser. MSODS log shows: 'Invalid value given for a multi-valued String extension value'
rootCause: The extension property (e.g. extension_<appid>_jobGroup) is defined as multivalued (isMultiValued=true), but a single string value is passed instead of an array in the API request.
solution: Pass an array instead of a single string. PowerShell: Update-MgUser -UserId <id> -AdditionalProperties @{'extension_<appid>_prop' = @('value')}. MS Graph REST: pass JSON array in request body for the extension property value.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/TSG - Failed to PATCH value to multivalued Extension Property

symptom: Cloud-only user without Exchange Online license has unwanted proxyAddress that cannot be removed through standard Azure AD portal. User is not mail-enabled (RemoteRecipientType is NULL).
rootCause: ProxyAddress management for cloud-only unlicensed users is not exposed in standard admin UI. ProxyCalc may re-add addresses if UPN-based proxy is removed. v1.0 Graph API does not support PATCH on proxyAddresses; only beta endpoint works.
solution: Option 1 (preferred): Use MS Graph Beta endpoint - PATCH https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/users/{id} with body {"proxyAddresses": ["SMTP:keep@domain.com"]} containing only addresses to keep. Requires Global Admin + User.ReadWrite.All. Option 2: Create dummy user, soft-delete target user, set dummy's mail to unwanted proxy, restore target with Restore-EntraDeletedDirectoryObject -AutoReconcileProxyConflict. Note: verify user has no Exchange plans enabled and RemoteRecipientType is NULL.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/TSG - Remove unwanted proxyAddress from cloud-only user without Exchange license

symptom: 403 Access Denied when updating sensitive user properties (accountEnabled, mobilePhone, otherMails) via MS Graph, even with User.ReadWrite.All or Directory.ReadWrite.All permissions
rootCause: Updating sensitive user properties for users with privileged administrator roles requires a higher privileged admin role. In delegated scenarios, the app also needs Directory.AccessAsUser.All delegated permission. MSODS IfxBECAuthorizationManager logs show the specific task (e.g. SetUserMobileProperty) as DENIED.
solution: Delegated: assign Directory.AccessAsUser.All delegated permission and ensure calling user has a higher privileged admin role per Who can perform sensitive actions doc. App-only: assign a higher privileged administrator role to the service principal. Diagnose with MSODS Global(IfxBECAuthorizationManager) query filtering by internalCorrelationId to identify which task was DENIED.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/TSG - 403 unable to modify user attribute like accontEnabled, mobilePhone

symptom: 403 error when updating onPremisesImmutableId (SourceAnchor) for a user via MS Graph or Update-MgUser
rootCause: Updating onPremisesImmutableId is classified as a sensitive action. For cloud-only users, an appropriate admin role is required per the sensitive actions matrix. For synced users, only Global Administrator can perform the UpdateSourceAnchorOnDirSyncUser task.
solution: Cloud-only users: ensure calling principal has appropriate admin role (Auth Admin/User Admin/Privileged Auth Admin/Global Admin depending on target user role). Use User.ReadWrite.All for app-only flow. Synced users: must use Global Administrator. When using Update-MgUser, pass scope Directory.AccessAsUser.All to use UserIdentity flow.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/TSG - 403 when updating onpremisesImmutableId

symptom: Error 'One or more property values specified are invalid' when PATCHing a value to a directory extension property via MS Graph or Update-MgUser. MSODS log shows 'Invalid value given for a multi-valued String extension value'.
rootCause: The extension property is defined as multivalued (isMultiValued=true), but a single string value was passed instead of an array/list.
solution: Pass an array instead of a single string value. PowerShell: Update-MgUser -UserId <id> -AdditionalProperties @{extension_<appid>_prop = @('value')}. MS Graph API: use JSON array format in the PATCH request body for the extension property value.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/TSG - Failed to PATCH value to multivalued Extension Property

symptom: Failed to update user attributes (accountEnabled, mobilePhone, otherMails) with 403 Forbidden, even though the calling principal has User.ReadWrite.All or Directory.ReadWrite.All permission
rootCause: Updating sensitive user properties for users with privileged administrator roles requires elevated permissions: delegated scenarios need Directory.AccessAsUser.All + higher privileged admin role; app-only scenarios need the app assigned a higher privileged admin role per Who can perform sensitive actions doc
solution: For delegated flow: assign Directory.AccessAsUser.All delegated permission and ensure the calling user has a higher privileged administrator role. For app-only flow: assign the app a higher privileged administrator role. Check MSODS IfxBECAuthorizationManager logs using internalCorrelationId to see which task was DENIED.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/TSG - 403 unable to modify user attribute like accontEnabled, mobilePhone

symptom: 403 error when updating onpremisesImmutableId (sourceAnchor) for a user via Microsoft Graph API
rootCause: Updating onpremisesImmutableId is a sensitive action. For cloud-only users, it requires an admin role per the sensitive actions doc. For synced users, only Global Administrator can update it. PowerShell Update-MgUser requires Directory.AccessAsUser.All scope.
solution: For cloud-only users: ensure caller has appropriate privileged admin role (check sensitive actions permission table). For synced users: use Global Administrator. When using Update-MgUser, pass -Scopes Directory.AccessAsUser.All. For app-only flow, User.ReadWrite.All application permission is required.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/TSG - 403 when updating onpremisesImmutableId

symptom: Failed to PATCH value to multivalued extension property via Graph API or Update-MgUser with error: One or more property values specified are invalid. MSODS log shows: Invalid value given for a multi-valued String extension value.
rootCause: The extension property is defined as multivalued (multiValued=true in extensionProperty definition), but the API call passes a single string value instead of an array/list.
solution: Pass an array/list instead of a single string. PowerShell: Update-MgUser -UserId <userid> -AdditionalProperties @{extension_<appid>_jobGroup = @("Software Developer")}. Graph API: pass ["value"] instead of "value" in the request body.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD User Management/TSG - Failed to PATCH value to multivalued Extension Property

symptom: Blob index tags set during page blob upload are not persisted/visible after upload completes
rootCause: Known limitation: uploading page blobs with index tags does not persist the tags during the upload operation.
solution: Set blob index tags in a separate operation after uploading the page blob, not during the upload.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure RBAC for Resources/ABAC Conditions On Azure RBAC Role Assignments

symptom: Error 'The given role assignment condition is invalid' (HTTP 400 BadRequest, code InvalidCreateOrUpdateRoleAssignmentRequest) when configuring ABAC condition on Azure RBAC role assignment via PowerShell
rootCause: ABAC condition is being scoped to an unsupported level. Azure Storage does not support scoping conditions to a Storage account or Container directly. Conditions must be set at Management Group, Subscription, or Resource Group level.
solution: Move the ABAC condition scope to Management Group, Subscription, or Resource Group level instead of Storage account or Container level.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure RBAC for Resources/ABAC Conditions On Azure RBAC Role Assignments

symptom: Tenant deletion fails in CMAT Validate+Delete tool with blocker: Tenant is listed as a partner. Partner mapping on the tenant prevents deletion even after CSP relationships are removed.
rootCause: Partner mapping exists on the tenant in OMS/Commerce layer. The tenant has or had CSP partner registration (BreadthPartner, ResellerPartner, or DistributionContract) that was not fully cleaned up.
solution: 1) Obtain Global Admin email approval for partner mapping removal; 2) Verify no active CSP relationships in OMS ResellerCustomerInfo tab, CMAT Partner+Customer Relation tab, CST billing accounts, Partner Center SMRT Tool, and DS Explorer Contract container; 3) If relationships found, have CSP remove via Partner Center or follow CSP Decision Tree for non-responsive partners; 4) Use Geneva Actions with PME.GBL to remove relationships (RemoveAdvisorRelationship for BreadthPartner, RemovePartnerCustomerRelationship for ResellerPartner, RemoveIPIRRelationship for DistributionContract); 5) Remove Reseller tags in OMS Tenant Console Tags tab; 6) Remove partner mapping in OMS Tenant Console MSODS view tab; 7) Re-validate in CMAT Validate+Delete.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Azure AD Directory Deletion/Tenant Deletion - Removing CSP Partner Mapping

symptom: Tenant deletion is blocked by subscription objects not visible in Azure Portal or standard tools. Need to identify MSODS subscription objects preventing deletion.
rootCause: Orphaned or non-deleted MSODS subscription objects exist in the directory with SubscriptionStatus other than 3 (Deleted). These objects block tenant deletion but may not be visible through normal admin tools.
solution: Query D2K Kusto cluster (idsharedwus.westus/d2kredacted) with CLD-D2KRedacted-MSIT-R entitlement. Filter by TenantId. Check SubscriptionStatus: 0=Enabled, 1=Warning, 2=Suspended (all block deletion), 3=Deleted (OK). Cross-reference SkuID via Licensing Service Plan Reference doc. Note: D2K data is daily snapshot from midnight UTC; for live data use DS Explorer via Azure Identity TA.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Azure AD Directory Deletion/Tenant Deletion - Query Kusto for MSODS Subscriptions

symptom: CMAT shows tenant status is Released but tenant still exists and is visible to customer in Azure AD/Entra ID. Tenant deletion did not complete despite commerce release.
rootCause: Orphaned subscriptions in MSODS prevent actual tenant deletion after commerce release. OMS subscription history shows: UpdateCompanyProfile: The released tenant on commerce has orphan subscriptions on the directory.
solution: Check OMS Admin Console subscription history for orphan subscription error. Identify orphaned subscriptions using DS Explorer or D2K Kusto queries. Remove orphaned subscriptions through OMS Subscription Console or escalate to Azure Identity TA. Re-run CMAT Validate+Delete after cleanup.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Azure AD Directory Deletion/Tenant Deletion - Resolving Tenant Deletion Blockers using OMS Admin Console

symptom: Tenant deletion blocked because CMAT shows a subscription in Disabled state but Immediate Cancellation is unavailable. Subscription has not reached deprovision lifecycle date.
rootCause: Subscription is in Disabled status but has not completed lifecycle transition to Deleted/Deprovisioned. CMAT cannot fast-deprovision and modern commerce subscriptions in CST also cannot be fast-deprovisioned.
solution: Use OMS/BOSG admin console to fast deprovision as LAST resort. Prerequisites: 1) Verify CMAT unable to fast deprovision; 2) Escalate to ASMS TAs + Azure Commerce EEE to confirm OMS needed; 3) Verify subscription in Disabled (not Active) status; 4) Requires TA/PTA access with SAW + AME.GBL account.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Azure AD Directory Deletion/Tenant Deletion - Resolving Tenant Deletion Blockers using OMS Admin Console

symptom: ABAC condition builder shows 'No options available' error when selecting both Microsoft.Authorization/roleassignment/write and /delete permissions and clicking '+ Add expression' for custom condition on delegated Azure RBAC role assignments.
rootCause: The ABAC condition builder only supports selecting one permission at a time. The write permission applies to Request scope while delete permission applies to Resource scope, making them incompatible in a single condition.
solution: Create two separate Conditions in the same rule: one for Microsoft.Authorization/roleassignment/write (Request) and one for Microsoft.Authorization/roleassignment/delete (Resource). Configure each condition independently. When errors occur in the condition builder, switch to Code editor and press CTRL+D to get debug info for ICM submission.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure RBAC for Resources/ABAC Conditions for Delegated Azure RBAC Role Assignments

symptom: User with inherited RBAC role via Management Groups gets 'Not Authorized' error when creating resources on subscriptions, despite having inherited RBAC membership visible in subscription list.
rootCause: Resource providers calling older versions of the Permissions and RoleAssignments APIs do not support inherited Management Group RBAC membership. The inherited permissions are present (user sees subscription) but the RP fails when using older APIs to authorize the action.
solution: Create direct RBAC role assignments at the subscription level as workaround. PG has released new Permissions API and RoleAssignments API to address inherited permissions. However, resource providers are on various API versions and not forced to update immediately.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure RBAC for Resources/Azure RBAC for Management Groups

symptom: Tenant Admin fails to add a subscription to a Management Group with error: 'Owner level access is required on the subscription or its ancestors'. Portal shows 'Owner' under MY ROLE but admin still cannot add subscription.
rootCause: Tenant Admin (Global Admin) is not the same as being assigned to the Owner RBAC role in Access Control (IAM). The MY ROLE display shows Owner but this is the tenant-level admin role, not the Azure RBAC Owner role required for Management Group operations.
solution: Set 'Global admin can manage Azure Subscription' (Elevate Access) to YES in Entra ID Properties. Once elevated, the Tenant Admin gains User Access Administrator at root scope and can then assign themselves the Owner RBAC role at the subscription level via PowerShell or Portal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure RBAC for Resources/Azure RBAC for Management Groups

symptom: Management Group role assignment for a custom role with dataActions fails. Roles containing DataActions or NonDataActions cannot be assigned at the Management Group scope.
rootCause: Azure Management Groups do not support role assignments that include dataActions in their role definition. This is an architectural limitation of the Management Group scope.
solution: Create the role assignment at the subscription scope instead of the Management Group scope. DataActions-based roles (e.g., Storage Blob Data Contributor, VM User Login) can only be assigned at subscription, resource group, or resource scope.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure RBAC for Resources/Azure RBAC for Management Groups

symptom: Deployment of Gateway Manager Service resources (VPN, Application Gateway, ExpressRoute, Firewall, Route Server, vWAN, Managed NVA) fails after Jan 2025 with error: 'does not have authorization to perform action over scope or the scope is invalid'. Affects both custom RBAC roles and built-in roles (Contributor/Network Contributor) scoped to different resource groups.
rootCause: P0 SFI issue: Link Access Check enforcement now verifies ALL permissions for Gateway Manager Service resources. Previously, it did not verify join/action permissions on referenced resources (e.g., public IP address). Custom roles missing join permissions or built-in roles not scoped to referenced resources now fail. Phased enforcement: Phase 1 Jan 21-Feb 4, Phase 2 Feb 5-19, Phase 3 Feb 19-Mar 5 2025.
solution: For custom RBAC roles: add missing join/action permissions per product tables (aka.ms/AzNetCustomRoles, aka.ms/VNGWRoles, aka.ms/AppGwRoles, aka.ms/AzFWRoles, aka.ms/ARSRoles, aka.ms/VWANRoles, aka.ms/ERRoles, aka.ms/NVARoles). For built-in roles: ensure scope covers both the deploying resource AND referenced resources (e.g., Contributor role must be scoped to resource groups of both the Route Server and the Public IP address).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure RBAC for Resources/Link Access Check Enforcement for Gateway Manager Service

symptom: Managed Identity access sporadically fails with HTTP 403 Forbidden error during the first 30 minutes after creating a System-Assigned Managed Identity and assigning it an RBAC role.
rootCause: Global cache for RBAC role assignments validates against different instances that have not been populated yet. Initial replication can take up to 30 minutes globally. RBAC updates in the same region take 2 seconds, and 5 seconds for cross-region propagation if modified in another region.
solution: Wait up to 30 minutes for global RBAC replication to complete. For testing purposes only, use bypass_cache=true parameter in IMDS token request: curl 'http://169.254.169.254/metadata/identity/oauth2/token?api-version=2018-02-01&resource=https%3A%2F%2Fmanagement.azure.com%2F&bypass_cache=true' -H Metadata:true. WARNING: bypass_cache is NOT recommended for production use.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure RBAC for Resources/System-Assigned Managed Identity Integration with VMs

symptom: Subscription role assignment limit exceeded: user gets error RoleAssignmentLimitExceeded when trying to assign RBAC role at subscription level.
rootCause: Azure subscriptions have an architectural limit of 4000 role assignments. When this limit is reached, no more role assignments can be created.
solution: Check current role assignment count via Portal (Access Control IAM > Role assignments > load count). If count is 4000, customer must remove unused role assignments. If count is less than 4000, check Kusto logs in AADPAS cluster and open ICM to Policy Administration Service PG if needed. Consider adopting ABAC conditions to reduce direct role assignments.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure RBAC for Resources/Azure RBAC (Role-Based Access Control)

symptom: MemberOf dynamic groups show incorrect membership or miss adding/removing members in Entra ID
rootCause: MemberOf dynamic groups are still in Preview state and have known processing reliability issues. Groups do not always correctly add or remove members during background processing.
solution: Pause processing on the impacted MemberOf group in the Portal, wait 15 minutes, then resume processing to trigger a full reprocess. Remind customer that MemberOf groups are Preview and not recommended for production.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Dynamic Groups/Known issues and by design behavior

symptom: Multiple MemberOf dynamic groups with the same membership rule show different membership counts
rootCause: MemberOf dynamic groups are processed at different times, and the processing may miss adding or removing members. This mismatch is expected for frequently updated groups but can also occur due to a known bug.
solution: Pause processing on impacted groups in the Portal, wait 15 minutes, then resume processing. This forces a full reprocess. Backlog item: https://identitydivision.visualstudio.com/Engineering/_workitems/edit/2935209
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Dynamic Groups/Known issues and by design behavior

symptom: MemberOf dynamic group targeting an Administrative Unit does not update membership after initial creation
rootCause: When a MemberOf dynamic group points to an Administrative Unit, using group.objectId syntax in the membership rule causes it to update only on initial creation but not subsequently.
solution: Use correct syntax: user.memberof -any (administrativeUnit.objectId -in ['<AU-ObjectId>']) instead of group.objectId. Bug: https://identitydivision.visualstudio.com/Engineering/_workitems/edit/3172007
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Dynamic Groups/Known issues and by design behavior

symptom: Dynamic group membership rule using multi-valued extension attribute (Collection type) does not work as expected
rootCause: Dynamic group rules only support extension attributes as string properties. Multi-valued extension attributes (odata.type #Collection) are not supported in dynamic membership rules.
solution: Verify the extension attribute type via ASC Graph Explorer GET /user/<objectid>. If the attribute is Collection type, it cannot be used in dynamic group rules. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/enterprise-users/groups-dynamic-membership#extension-properties-and-custom-extension-properties
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Dynamic Groups/Known issues and by design behavior

symptom: Guest user is not added to a dynamic Office 365 group despite matching the membership rule
rootCause: If the group is an Office 365 group, the AllowToAddGuests directory setting may block guest addition. A sensitivity label assigned to the group can override AllowToAddGuests to False, making it read-only.
solution: Check audit logs for Assign label to group events. Verify AllowToAddGuests setting at both tenant-level and group-level. If a sensitivity label with guest policy is assigned, AllowToAddGuests becomes read-only. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/group-directory-settings
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Dynamic Groups/Known issues and by design behavior

symptom: Adding a space to the end of a dynamic group membership rule does not trigger the group to reprocess
rootCause: The UX may be trimming trailing whitespace when the membership rule is saved, preventing the change from being detected as an update.
solution: Instead of adding a space at the end, add a space to the middle of the rule, or change all double quotes to single quotes (or vice versa). Either method will be detected as a rule change and trigger reprocessing.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Dynamic Groups/Known issues and by design behavior

symptom: MemberOf dynamic group shows Failed processing status in Entra ID
rootCause: MemberOf dynamic groups fail when they exceed the allowed nesting level of 1. A MemberOf dynamic group cannot define the membership of another MemberOf dynamic group.
solution: Check all Group ObjectIDs in the MemberOf membership rule to verify none are also MemberOf dynamic groups. Also check if any other MemberOf group in the tenant contains the failing groups ObjectID. Remove circular references. If unable to determine cause, escalate to PG via IcM to Groups team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Dynamic Groups/TSG - Dynamic group has incorrect membership

symptom: Need to find and remove direct license assignments that conflict with group-based licensing in Entra ID
rootCause: Direct license assignments on users can conflict with or duplicate group-based license assignments, making license management difficult.
solution: Use the CleanupAADDirectLicenseAssignments.ps1 PowerShell script. Run with -WhatIf for report only, -WhatIf -SaveReport to export CSV, or without flags to remove direct assignments.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Find, report and remove direct license assignments

symptom: Unfamiliar audit log activities appear such as Features_GetFeaturesAsync, GroupsODataV4_Get, Group_GetDynamicGroupProperties in Entra ID
rootCause: These activities are logged by the Self-Service Group Management (SSGM) service. They are triggered by internal service calls when users access MyApps, MyGroups portal, or Azure portal group pages.
solution: These are normal internal service activities and can be safely ignored. Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/monitoring-health/reference-audit-activities#self-service-group-management
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/SSGM - Self Service Group Management/Unfamiliar Group Management related Audit logs

symptom: Old M365 groups expected to expire immediately when LCM expiration policy is first enabled but they get a grace period instead
rootCause: When an LCM policy targets a group whose age exceeds the policy lifetime, the RenewedDateTime is set to today plus 35 days (grace period). If age is less than policy lifetime, RenewedDateTime is set to original RenewedDateTime plus the policy lifetime days.
solution: Inform customer that groups older than the policy lifetime get a 35-day grace period before expiration notifications start (at 30 days before expiry). Groups newer than the policy lifetime get RenewedDateTime + lifetime. Expiration notification emails are sent at 30, 15, and 1 day before expiry if ShouldGroupBeAutorenewed is False.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/SSGM - Self Service Group Management/Behavior when an LCM policy is enabled

symptom: M365 groups are auto-renewed by SSGM despite no Office activities and customer wants to identify inactive groups for cleanup
rootCause: Known issue: SSGM auto-renewal may renew groups even without Office activity. Groups auto-renewed will not expire until the next cycle.
solution: Use Microsoft Graph API to get activity report: GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/reports/getOffice365GroupsActivityDetail(period=D180). Requires Reports.Read.All permission. Follow 302 redirect to download CSV, then filter by Last Activity Date to find inactive groups.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/SSGM - Self Service Group Management/Self-service instruction to get a list groups which do not have Office activities

symptom: Cloud security groups in Entra ID now support soft deletion and restoration (released Nov 2025)
solution: Cloud security groups can now be soft-deleted and restored within 30 days, similar to M365 groups. Previously only M365 groups supported this.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Upcoming Groups Features

symptom: Need to migrate on-premises AD groups to cloud-managed Entra ID groups using Source of Authority change
rootCause: Traditionally groups synced from on-prem AD cannot be managed in the cloud. The new Source of Authority (SoA) feature allows converting these groups.
solution: Use the Change Source of Authority feature to convert on-prem AD groups to cloud-managed groups. Supports Entra Connect Sync with rollback capabilities. Not suitable for environments with complex group management tools or Exchange dependencies. Cloud Sync support planned for future releases.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Upcoming Groups Features

symptom: Users see new configuration options when creating or managing M365 groups in Entra My Groups portal
rootCause: Microsoft introduced improvements to My Groups allowing users to configure additional group, email and security settings during M365 group creation and management.
solution: This is expected new behavior. Users can now set group usage guidelines, email alias, sensitivity labels, Exchange settings, external sender controls, and enable security group functionality. All settings respect existing tenant-level policies. Escalation path: IAM Services -> AAD End User Experiences.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Updated Entra My Groups M365 Group Management Experience

symptom: Customer sees 'Access to Azure Active Directory' subscription in Azure Portal subscription blade with errors: 'Unknown' role, 'Unauthorized', 'Unable to access data', or 'The current subscription does not allow you to perform any actions on Azure resources'
rootCause: Legacy 'Access to Azure Active Directory' subscriptions are deprecated dummy subscriptions created for the classic Azure portal (manage.windowsazure.com) when AAD admins did not have an Azure resource subscription. No longer needed since portal.azure.com separates AAD RBAC and Azure Resource RBAC.
solution: These subscriptions do NOT host Azure AD and are safe to delete. However, they can only be deleted via support ticket with Azure Subscriptions support teams and PayOps engineering. Note: deprecation announced April 2024, existing subscriptions will be disabled and deleted. SAP: Azure > Subscription management > Transfer ownership of my subscription.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure RBAC for Resources/TSG: What is the "Access to Azure Active Directory" subscription

symptom: All Azure RBAC role assignments are lost after an Azure subscription is transferred between Microsoft Entra ID tenants (via Transfer billing ownership or Associate subscription to directory)
rootCause: Any operation that causes an Azure subscription to be transferred between Entra ID tenants deletes ALL RBAC role assignments. Only the user who performed the transfer retains RBAC access. This is by design and documented in Microsoft docs.
solution: After transfer, the user who performed the transfer must re-assign RBAC permissions using principals in the destination tenant. Use ARM Kusto queries (armprodgbl.eastus.kusto.windows.net/ARMProd) to investigate: 1) Query Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments to find when assignments were deleted, 2) Query before/after the transfer timeframe to identify the user who initiated it. For RBAC restoration questions, redirect to ASMS: Azure > Subscription management > Transfer ownership.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure RBAC for Resources/Troubleshooting Azure Subscription Tenant Transfers

symptom: Newly created Entra ID object (user, application, service principal, group membership) returns 'Not Found' immediately after creation, or recently updated properties are not visible on subsequent reads
rootCause: Microsoft Entra uses an eventually consistent directory model with asynchronous replication across replicas. A successful write does not guarantee an immediate read will reflect the change. Reads are served from nearby replicas for performance while writes replicate asynchronously.
solution: This is expected behavior. Client applications should handle eventual consistency explicitly. Reference: https://devblogs.microsoft.com/identity/designing-for-eventual-consistency-for-microsoft-entra/ and https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/architecture/architecture#data-consistency. Breaking change: starting May 31, 2026, default app-only consistency behavior will be disabled for Microsoft Entra Graph APIs. Delegated (user-based) access is not affected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Eventual Directory Consistency in Microsoft Entra

symptom: Access Reviews: Peer outlier (User-to-Group Affiliation) insights are not available when creating or viewing an access review. Warning column shows User-to-Group Affiliation not available.
rootCause: User-to-Group Affiliation insights data is not ready yet. Dependencies on other data flows from other systems must occur first. A hierarchy change (e.g., manager assignment) may take 24-48 hours to be picked up.
solution: Ensure users have a Manager assigned to aid in affiliation discovery. Wait 24 hours for hierarchy changes to propagate, or up to 48 hours due to upstream data flow dependencies. Check the Warning column in Access Reviews for status.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Entra ID Access Reviews

symptom: Access Reviews: Legacy beta Access Reviews APIs (Microsoft Graph beta) stop working. Calls to beta Access Reviews API endpoints return errors after July-September 2025 deprecation.
rootCause: Microsoft retired legacy beta Access Reviews APIs starting July 1, 2025 (tenants not actively using them) through September 1, 2025 (active tenants).
solution: Migrate to v1.0 Graph APIs for Access Reviews: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/resources/accessreviewsv2-overview?view=graph-rest-1.0. Update all code using beta Access Reviews endpoints to the v1.0 equivalents.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Entra ID Access Reviews

symptom: Entitlement Management: Users lose eligible group access while EM shows they are still assigned. Discrepancy between Entitlement Management and PIM for Groups assignment status.
rootCause: Access Package expiration period exceeds the Expire eligible assignments after policy setting in the PIM managed group. PIM assignment expires before EM assignment, causing access loss while EM still shows active assignment.
solution: Ensure Access Package assignment expiration matches or is shorter than the PIM policy Expire eligible assignments after setting. For permanent assignments, set Allow permanent eligible assignments to Yes in PIM group policy and AP expiration to Never.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Entra ID Entitlement Management

symptom: Entra ID Governance features for guest users are blocked. Cannot create/update guest-scoped entitlement management policies, access reviews with inactive user/affiliation helpers, or lifecycle workflows for guest userType.
rootCause: EIG for Guest Add-on enforcement started Jan 30 2026 (EM/LCW) and Mar 31 2026 (Access Reviews). Tenant does not have an Azure subscription linked with the Entra ID Governance for Guest Add-on billing meter.
solution: Link Azure subscription: Entra ID > ID Governance > Dashboard > Guest access governance > Get Started. Pricing: USD 0.75/guest/month. For temporary extension, submit ICM (extensions granted for 3 months through April 2026). MC1225192 for details.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Entra ID Governance for Guest Add on License

symptom: Lifecycle Workflow shows error "This rule contains invalid properties" when viewing workflow execution conditions. Workflow stops running.
rootCause: A Custom Security Attribute (CSA) used in the workflow scope rule was deactivated. When the CSA is deactivated, the workflow cannot evaluate the scope and stops executing.
solution: Re-activate the deactivated Custom Security Attribute, or update the workflow scope to use a different active CSA. Check CSA status at Entra admin center > Protection > Custom Security Attributes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Entra ID Lifecycle Workflows/Lifecycle Workflows - Custom Security Attributes

symptom: Lifecycle Workflows admin cannot see or edit CSA scope rules, cannot view Custom Security Attributes in the Property list when creating workflow. CSA-based trigger options are missing from the UI.
rootCause: Admin is missing the Attribute Assignment Reader or Attribute Assignment Administrator role. Global Administrator and Privileged Role Administrator roles do NOT have permissions to read or add custom security attributes.
solution: Assign the Attribute Definition Administrator or Attribute Assignment Reader role to the admin. Note: Global Admin/Privileged Role Admin roles are insufficient for CSA operations.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Entra ID Lifecycle Workflows/Lifecycle Workflows - Custom Security Attributes

symptom: MemberOf dynamic groups show incorrect membership or different membership counts across groups with identical membership rules
rootCause: MemberOf dynamic groups are preview with known processing limitations. Groups not processed simultaneously may miss member changes. Nesting depth >1 causes failure.
solution: Pause and unpause group processing in Azure Portal (wait 15 min). MemberOf is preview, not for production. For failed state: check if rule references another MemberOf group (nesting >1). Doc: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/users/groups-dynamic-rule-member-of
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Dynamic Groups/Known issues and by design behavior

symptom: MemberOf dynamic group targeting Administrative Unit updates only at creation then stops
rootCause: Wrong syntax: group.objectId used instead of administrativeUnit.objectId for AU targets.
solution: Use: user.memberof -any (administrativeUnit.objectId -in ['<AU-GUID>']). group.objectId is incorrect for AU targets.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Dynamic Groups/Known issues and by design behavior

symptom: Dynamic group rule using extension attribute does not match expected users; multi-valued extension properties ignored
rootCause: Dynamic group rules only support extension attributes as string. Collections (#Collection) are not supported.
solution: Check via Graph GET /users/<id>. If odata.type is #Collection(Edm.String) it is multi-valued and unsupported. Use single-valued string extensions. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/enterprise-users/groups-dynamic-membership#extension-properties-and-custom-extension-properties
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Dynamic Groups/Known issues and by design behavior

symptom: Guest user not added to dynamic O365 group despite matching membership rule
rootCause: AllowToAddGuests=False at tenant/group level. Sensitivity label assignment can silently override AllowToAddGuests when EnableMIPLabels=True.
solution: Check AllowToAddGuests via PowerShell. Check audit logs for 'Assign label to group'. Review guest policy on sensitivity label. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/group-directory-settings
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Dynamic Groups/Known issues and by design behavior

symptom: Adding whitespace to end of dynamic group rule does not trigger reprocess; DirectReports rule with {} brackets fails
rootCause: Portal UX trims trailing spaces. DirectReports with {} brackets around manager_id is invalid syntax.
solution: Add space to MIDDLE of rule or toggle double/single quotes. For DirectReports: remove {} (use 'Direct Reports for manager_id'). Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/azure/active-directory/troubleshoot-dynamic-groups
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Dynamic Groups/Known issues and by design behavior

symptom: Custom role with microsoft.directory/groups/members/update allows manual edit of dynamic group membership
rootCause: RBAC bug: custom roles with groups/members/update permit direct membership changes on dynamic groups (Feb 2025).
solution: Known bug (Bug 3166715, fix in progress). Review custom roles. Monitor audit logs for unauthorized dynamic group membership changes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Dynamic Groups/Known issues and by design behavior

symptom: Dynamic group with guest restriction does not add member users matching the rule; members silently skipped
rootCause: Bug 2076615 (Batch response wrong Content-ID) causes member users to be skipped when guest restriction enabled.
solution: Add to end of rule: 'and (user.userType -ne "Guest")' or 'and (user.userType -eq "member")'. Fix expected with MS Graph migration H1 2026.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Dynamic Groups/Known issues and by design behavior

symptom: Dynamic group membership rule Edit button greyed out despite admin role
rootCause: Group is in restricted management AU (IsManagementRestricted=True). Standard admin roles insufficient.
solution: Check if group is in restricted management AU. Assign role scoped to that AU or remove group from it. Portal shows banner.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/Dynamic Groups/Known issues and by design behavior

symptom: Unfamiliar audit log entries: Features_GetFeaturesAsync, Features_IsFeatureEnabledAsync, Group_GetDynamicGroupProperties, GroupLifecyclePolicies_Get, GroupsODataV4_Get
rootCause: Generated by SSGM service during normal operations (MyApps/MyGroups/Azure Portal access).
solution: Expected internal service activities, safely ignored. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/monitoring-health/reference-audit-activities#self-service-group-management
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/SSGM - Self Service Group Management/"Unfamiliar" Group Management related Audit logs

symptom: Customer enables LCM group expiration policy; worries old groups will be immediately deleted
rootCause: When LCM targets a group, behavior depends on RenewedDateTime vs policy lifetime. Groups older than lifetime get 35-day grace period.
solution: (1) Days since RenewedDateTime < lifetime: expiry=RenewedDateTime+lifetime. (2) Days > lifetime: expiry=today+35 days. Old groups NOT immediately deleted. Notifications start 30 days before expiry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/SSGM - Self Service Group Management/Behavior when an LCM policy is enabled

symptom: M365 groups auto-renewed by SSGM despite no Office activities in reports
rootCause: Known issue in SSGM auto-renewal logic triggering renewal without actual Office activities.
solution: Use Graph API: GET /reports/getOffice365GroupsActivityDetail(period='D180') (Reports.Read.All). Download CSV, filter old Last Activity Date. Groups deleted at next cycle if not renewed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Group Management/SSGM - Self Service Group Management/Self-service instruction to get a list groups which do not have Office activities

symptom: Need to find and remove direct license assignments; migrate to group-based licensing
solution: Use CleanupAADDirectLicenseAssignments.ps1. -WhatIf for report, -WhatIf -SaveReport for CSV, no flags to remove. Download from ADO wiki.

symptom: Cloud security group soft deletion/restoration; converting on-prem AD groups to cloud (Source of Authority)
solution: Cloud security groups support soft deletion/restoration (Nov 2025). SoA converts on-prem AD groups to cloud-managed Entra ID. Supports Entra Connect Sync with rollback. Escalation: IAM Services > AAD End User Experiences.

symptom: Lifecycle Workflow with inactive user trigger: previously in-scope users not removed when workflow scope is changed before execution, causing unintended processing
rootCause: When the inactive user workflow scope is changed prior to the workflow being run, the system does not remove users that were previously considered in scope from the execution queue
solution: Known limitation under investigation. Workaround: create a new workflow with the updated scope instead of modifying existing one, or run on-demand to verify scope before enabling schedule
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Entra ID Lifecycle Workflows/Lifecycle Workflows - Inactive Users Trigger

symptom: Lifecycle Workflow group membership change trigger does not fire when user is added/removed from a nested group
rootCause: Group nesting is not supported for LCW mover triggers. Only direct membership changes to the configured group are monitored, not transitive/nested membership
solution: By design. Configure the workflow to target the specific group where direct membership changes occur. Or use attribute-change trigger instead
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Entra ID Lifecycle Workflows/Lifecycle Workflows - Mover Support

symptom: Lifecycle Workflow mover trigger with attribute change does not process users even though the attribute value appears to match the scope rule
rootCause: Attribute scoping rules in Lifecycle Workflows are case sensitive. E.g. scope rule (department eq Secret) will NOT match department=secret
solution: Ensure exact case match between scope rule and actual attribute value. Check via Graph API: GET /users/{id}?$select=department
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Entra ID Lifecycle Workflows/Lifecycle Workflows - Mover Support

symptom: Lifecycle Workflow with group membership change trigger does not fire promptly for dynamic groups; delay of many hours
rootCause: Dynamic groups may take up to 24 hours to update memberships. LCW only detects the change after the dynamic group membership is actually updated in Entra ID
solution: Expected behavior. For time-sensitive scenarios, use attribute-change trigger or manually add users to a static group
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Entra ID Lifecycle Workflows/Lifecycle Workflows - Mover Support

symptom: Lifecycle Workflow history shows success for delete user task but user still exists in on-premises AD
rootCause: Known bug: LCW does not receive feedback from provisioning agent, so workflow history immediately shows success without confirming on-prem deletion completed
solution: Verify deletion directly in on-prem AD using ADUC. Deletion should occur seconds after workflow runs. If still exists, check provisioning agent logs and gMSA permissions
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Entra ID Lifecycle Workflows/Lifecycle Workflows - OnPrem Task Support

symptom: Lifecycle Workflow fails when attempting to delete a large set of on-premises user accounts in a single run
rootCause: On-prem deletion is subject to accidental deletion prevention threshold of the underlying provisioning service, blocking mass deletions
solution: Process users in smaller batches by adjusting workflow scope. Check provisioning agent accidental deletion prevention threshold (default 500) and consider temporarily increasing it
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Entra ID Lifecycle Workflows/Lifecycle Workflows - OnPrem Task Support

symptom: Creating a new Lifecycle Workflow fails with error ERROR_WORKFLOWS_PER_TENANT_LIMIT_EXCEEDED
rootCause: Tenant has reached the maximum allowed number of workflows (50 per tenant)
solution: Delete one or more existing workflows: DELETE https://graph.microsoft.com/beta/identityGovernance/lifecycleManagement/workflows/{id}
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Identity Governance/Entra ID Lifecycle Workflows/TSGs/CRUD on workflows

rootCause: At least one task in the workflow contains arguments that do not match the corresponding task definition
solution: Query task definition: GET /beta/identityGovernance/lifecycleManagement/taskDefinitions/{taskDefinitionId}. Ensure arguments match definition, then retry

symptom: Lifecycle Workflow error: Workflow scheduling is not supported for disabled workflows; on-demand not available; workflows not running
rootCause: After LCW GA with Entra ID Governance license, tenants from P2 preview were transitioned to read-only mode with isEnabled=false. Workflows remain disabled even after purchasing Governance license
solution: Go to Identity Governance > Lifecycle workflows > Workflows. Select each Workflow > Properties > check Enable > Save
