Alternatively use Microsoft Entra recommendation "Renew expiring application credentials" (Preview) which flags credentials expiring within 30 days and supports email notifications to admins.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Managing Expiring Secrets and Certificates in App Registrations (Without Notifications)

symptom: Compliant Network option is greyed out (unavailable) in the Conditional Access portal when creating or editing CA policies in Global Secure Access.
rootCause: CA signaling is not enabled in the Global Secure Access portal, or the admin lacks required RBAC roles (Conditional Access admin or Global Secure Access admin).
solution: Enable CA signaling in Global Secure Access portal (portal.azure.com -> Global Secure Access -> Global settings -> Adaptive Access). Ensure admin has either Conditional Access admin or Global Secure Access admin role.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra Global Secure Access (ZTNA)/Compliant Network check in CA

symptom: Compliant Network condition in Conditional Access policy is not taking effect. Users not blocked or granted access based on compliant network status despite CA policy being configured.
rootCause: One or more prerequisites not met: CA-signaling toggle not enabled in GSA portal, M365 traffic profile not enabled, or Global Secure Access client disabled/not running on client machine.
solution: 1) Enable CA-signaling toggle in GSA portal (Global Secure Access -> Global settings -> Adaptive Access); wait up to 30 min. 2) Enable M365 traffic forwarding in GSA portal. 3) Ensure Global Secure Access client is installed and running. Note: Compliant Network does not support CAE enforcement yet.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra Global Secure Access (ZTNA)/Compliant Network check in CA

symptom: Error "The CA certificate has already been issued" (HTTP 400 BadRequest) when configuring TLS inspection in Global Secure Access. InvalidPropertyValue error about extendedKeyUsage.
rootCause: Certificate name and Common name in TLS inspection CSR request conflict with a previously used certificate name.
solution: Supply unique values in Certificate name and Common name fields of the CSR request form on the TLS inspection settings blade. Do not reuse names from previously issued certificates.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Microsoft Entra Global Secure Access (ZTNA)/GSA Advanced Threat Protection

symptom: Cannot read property isEnabled of undefined error on Staged Rollout blade in Entra portal even for Global Administrators
rootCause: UI bug when tenant has email as alternate ID enabled for staged rollout along with PTA/PHS and SSO staged rollout features
solution: Manually add passwordHashSync to staged rollout policy via Graph API POST to featurerolloutpolicies endpoint with feature=passwordHashSync and isEnabled=false
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO)/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO) - Known Issues

symptom: Seamless SSO AES ticket processing long latency - user on domain-joined machine gets password prompt instead of DSSO, requires 4-5 retries
rootCause: Windows SSPI API used for AES Kerberos ticket decryption introduces latency; sso request response does not arrive within the 10s timeout
solution: Workaround: switch AZUREADSSOACC to RC4 encryption type and roll over Kerberos keys. Permanent fix requires moving from SSPI API to Kerberos.Net.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO)/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO) - Known Issues

symptom: Seamless SSO does not work on Hybrid Entra Joined or Workplace Joined devices
rootCause: PRT-based SSO takes precedence over Seamless SSO on these device types; Seamless SSO is designed only for domain-joined non-hybrid devices
solution: For non-persistent VDI where Workplace Join interferes, block it via registry: HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WorkplaceJoin BlockAADWorkplaceJoin=dword:00000001
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO)/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO) - Known Issues

symptom: Users cannot benefit from Seamless SSO after Kerberos key rolled over more than once
rootCause: Entra stores only current and previous Kerberos key; rolling over more than once invalidates TGS tickets issued with older key
solution: Wait up to 10 hours for TGS to renew, or flush Kerberos cache with klist purge on affected machines (no admin rights required)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO)/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO) - Known Issues

symptom: Integrated Windows authentication supported only in federation flow error when connecting to Azure SQL via SSMS Entra ID Integrated on managed domain with Seamless SSO
rootCause: ADAL.dll version issue in SSMS client when using integrated authentication against a managed non-federated domain
solution: Validate the adal.dll version per Azure SQL team csswiki on Azure Active Directory integrated authentication
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO)/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO) - Known Issues

symptom: Seamless SSO flow takes longer than 10 seconds causing password dialog in Edge/Chrome with outbound web proxy when internal DNS cannot resolve public names
rootCause: Browser attempts DNS resolution for autologon.microsoftazuread-sso.com before sending Kerberos ticket; DNS delays from Suffix Search List and blocked root hints exceed 10s SSO timeout
solution: Create an empty forward lookup zone named autologon.microsoftazuread-sso.com on the internal DNS server to speed up name resolution
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO)/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO) - Known Issues

symptom: AADSSOTILES cookie forces user to re-enter credentials after signing out from Entra in browser even with Seamless SSO enabled
rootCause: After sign-out AADSSOTILES cookie is set to 1 which forces account selection and credential prompt during next sign-in; cookie persists until first successful sign-in
solution: By-design behavior. Cookie is cleared after first successful sign-in following sign-out. No action needed.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO)/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO) - Known Issues

symptom: Seamless SSO, PTA, and Staged Rollout features do not work in Azure China Mooncake cloud
rootCause: These hybrid authentication features are not supported in China Mooncake cloud; only Password Hash Sync PHS is supported
solution: Use Password Hash Sync PHS instead. PTA, Seamless SSO, and Staged Rollout are not available in Mooncake.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO)/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO) - Known Issues

symptom: Internal error during DesktopSsoNumOfDomains process when enabling Seamless SSO or rolling over Kerberos keys for multiple forests
rootCause: Seamless SSO policy has 25600 character limit in Entra ID; standard Kerberos rollover doubles info stored per domain suffix which can exceed the limit
solution: Options: (1) Remove unused domain suffixes, (2) Use Hybrid Entra Join, (3) Setup forest trust and enable SSO for one forest only, (4) Disable and re-enable SSO (acts as rollover, may disrupt up to 10 hours). Verify policy size via ASC Tenant Policy.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO)/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO) - Known Issues

symptom: Seamless SSO removed from child domain when setting up staging server or enabling SSO for root domain if child domain name does not contain root domain name
rootCause: Bug in backend code matching domain names; when child domain name does not include forest root name SSO config for child domain gets removed
solution: Re-enable SSO on child domain: Import-Module AzureADSSO.psd1 then New-AzureADSSOAuthenticationContext then Enable-AzureADSSOForest with child domain creds then Enable-AzureADSSO -Enable true. May take up to 10 hours or run klist purge.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO)/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO) - Known Issues

symptom: DesktopSsoLookupUserBySidFailed - EvoSTS cannot locate user based on SID extracted from Kerberos ticket
rootCause: User On premises security ID attribute is empty in Entra ID because AAD Connect is not syncing the SID attribute from on-premises AD
solution: Check if user has value in On premises security ID attribute. If empty troubleshoot why AAD Connect does not sync it - collaborate with AAD Connect Sync support.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO)/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO) - Known Issues

symptom: Update-AzureADSSOForest fails with TargetInvocationException during Kerberos key rollover; network trace shows SAMR SamrConnect5 rejected with Status=0x5 Access Denied
rootCause: Domain Controller has RestrictRemoteSam policy/registry key set blocking SAM Remote Protocol connection needed by NetUserSetInfo/SetPassword during key rollover
solution: Remove RestrictRemoteSam registry key from DCs: Remove-ItemProperty -Path HKLM:\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa -Name restrictremotesam. Check GPresult for domain GPO applying this setting. Reboot DCs after removal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO)/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO) - Known Issues

symptom: Update-AzureADSSOForest/Enable-AzureADSSOForest fails with permission error after Granting full control to account admins step when run by delegated admin
rootCause: Without -PreserveCustomPermissionsOnDesktopSsoAccount switch the cmdlet removes inherited permissions from AZUREADSSOACC and grants full control to enterprise admin; delegated admins lack permissions
solution: Re-run with -PreserveCustomPermissionsOnDesktopSsoAccount switch: Update-AzureADSSOForest -OnPremCredentials $creds -PreserveCustomPermissionsOnDesktopSsoAccount
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO)/Azure AD Seamless Single Sign On (SSO) - Known Issues

symptom: Federated users using ROPC (Resource Owner Password Credentials) flow fail authentication after October 24, 2022
rootCause: Security patch deployed globally blocked PTA authentication for federated users using ROPC flow to prevent attack vector
solution: Option 1: Enable direct ROPC via Home Realm Discovery policy (requires PHS enabled). Option 2: Use PTA Staged Rollout for affected users (disable Staged Rollout as primary auth first). Correct fix: stop using ROPC for federated users, use proper auth flow
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Pass-Through Authentication/Azure AD Pass-Through Authentication (PTA) and Staged Rollout - Known Issues

symptom: Staged Rollout option appears grayed out in Entra ID portal and cannot be configured
rootCause: Staged Rollout option is grayed out by design when MFA Staged Rollout or CBA (Certificate-Based Authentication) Rollout feature is enabled
solution: Disable MFA/CBA Staged Rollout via PowerShell: Connect-MgGraph, Get-MgPolicyFeatureRolloutPolicy to find policy ID, then Update-MgPolicyFeatureRolloutPolicy with isEnabled=$false, then Remove-MgPolicyFeatureRolloutPolicy
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Pass-Through Authentication/Azure AD Pass-Through Authentication (PTA) and Staged Rollout - Known Issues

symptom: Disable-PassthroughAuthentication PowerShell cmdlet fails with error: Cannot process argument because the value of argument exception is null (PSArgumentNullException)
rootCause: Bug in PassthroughAuthPSModule cmdlet when processing authentication response
solution: Workaround: Register the PTA agent first, then execute the steps to disable PTA. See ICM 527131499
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Pass-Through Authentication/Azure AD Pass-Through Authentication (PTA) and Staged Rollout - Known Issues

symptom: PTA, Staged Rollout, and Azure AD Seamless SSO features are not available in Azure China (Mooncake) cloud
rootCause: These hybrid authentication features are not supported in China (Mooncake) cloud by design
solution: Use Password Hash Sync (PHS) only. PHS is the only supported hybrid authentication method in Mooncake
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Pass-Through Authentication/Azure AD Pass-Through Authentication (PTA) and Staged Rollout - Known Issues

symptom: PTA authentication fails intermittently with timeout; delay between PTA agent and on-premises AD or Entra ID endpoint causes authentication failure
rootCause: ESTS call timeout for PTA is 7 seconds; any delay between PTA agent and on-premises AD or from PTA agent to Entra ID endpoint exceeding this causes failure
solution: Ensure low-latency network path between PTA agent server and domain controllers, and between PTA agent and Entra ID endpoints. Investigate network issues if auth times approach 7-second threshold
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Pass-Through Authentication/Azure AD Pass-Through Authentication (PTA) and Staged Rollout - Known Issues

symptom: Concern about Azure AD PTA security flaws reported by Secureworks allowing impersonation of PTA agent if attacker gains admin access to PTA server
rootCause: Attack requires attacker to already have administrative access to PTA agent server; vulnerability does not pose additional risk if hardening guidance is followed
solution: Harden PTA agent server as if it were a domain controller per MS guidance. Monitor CAPI key access and key file operations. Compare on-prem AD and Azure AD sign-in logs for PTA logon event discrepancies. Ref: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory/hybrid/how-to-connect-pta-security-deep-dive
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Pass-Through Authentication/Azure AD Pass-Through Authentication (PTA) and Staged Rollout - Known Issues

symptom: PTA user with Must change password on next logon flag or expired password fails to sign in on Azure AD Joined (AADJ) device with error: The sign-in method you are trying to use is not allowed
rootCause: Windows 10 AAD Authentication Client does not support the password change redirect for PTA customers on AADJ devices
solution: User must change password via browser instead of Windows login screen. Alternatively use Hybrid Azure AD Joined (HAADJ) device where the Windows AD prompt for password change works. Tracked by Windows 10 AAD Client Team workitem 411947
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Authentication/Azure AD Pass-Through Authentication/Pass-Through Authentication (PTA) - SSPR

symptom: Azure AD B2C UserInfo endpoint returns string value "System.Collections.Generic.List`1[System.String]" for claims that should be arrays (e.g. roles and groups); StringJoin/StringSplit workarounds also fail
rootCause: Bug in B2C where array-type claims (roles, groups) are serialized as the .NET generic list type string instead of a proper JSON array in the UserInfo endpoint response (ICM 437064342, Bug 1906808)
solution: Issue has been fixed by the B2C product team. Verify the fix is deployed in the customer tenant. If issue persists, escalate via ICM referencing Bug 1906808.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Bugs and Feature Requests

symptom: After disabling TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 for an Azure AD B2C tenant, Qualys SSL Labs report still shows TLS 1.0/1.1 as enabled/supported
rootCause: Qualys SSL Labs only proves that TLS 1.0/1.1 is accessible at the protocol/network layer. Azure AD B2C Gateway disables TLS 1.0/1.1 at the service level - the request does not proceed even if SSL Labs can initiate a connection at the protocol level.
solution: Verify service-level enforcement using curl: `curl https://login.microsoftonline.com/te/{tenant.onmicrosoft.com} --tlsv1.0 --tls-max 1.0` or `curl https://login.microsoftonline.com/cpim?tenantid={tenantId} --tlsv1.1 --tls-max 1.1`. The SSL Labs report is a false positive; TLS 1.0/1.1 is correctly blocked at the B2C service layer.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: Qualys SSL Labs report lists RSA and CBC TLS 1.2 ciphers as WEAK for Azure AD B2C; customer requests these ciphers be disabled
rootCause: RSA and CBC ciphers are flagged as weak but have no known active exploits. Azure AD B2C gateway team has no plans to disable them due to backward compatibility requirements for older B2C clients (as of February 2024).
solution: No native method to disable specific weak ciphers in Azure AD B2C. If customer needs fewer ciphers: implement Azure Front Door in front of B2C and enforce TLS 1.2 - this reduces available cipher set. SSL Labs still gives grade A with these ciphers present. No SLA or timeline for native cipher removal.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: Engineer or customer needs to determine whether a given Entra/AAD tenant is an Azure AD B2C tenant or a standard Entra ID (AAD/B2B) tenant
rootCause: B2C and standard Entra tenants appear similar in the portal and API responses without specific property checks.
solution: In ASC, check the "B2C Enabled" property under Tenant Details > Tenant Config. If true = B2C tenant; if false = standard AAD/B2B tenant. See also: Microsoft Entra External ID overview docs for tenant type comparison.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: Azure Government B2C tenant customer cannot find the "Link a subscription" option in the Azure portal for billing as described in public docs
rootCause: Linking an Azure Government B2C tenant to an Azure subscription is not supported (confirmed 2024-12-12). This is a documented limitation specific to Azure Government vs. global Azure.
solution: This is a known unsupported scenario for Azure Government B2C. Reference: "Compare Azure Government and global Azure - Azure Active Directory B2C" documentation. There is no workaround currently available.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: Azure AD B2C X (Twitter) identity provider sign-in fails with error: Microsoft.Cpim.Common.BadRequestException: The call to https://api.twitter.com/oauth/request_token for a request token returned an error Forbidden
rootCause: Twitter/X added new validations requiring OAuth callback URLs to be strictly case-sensitive. If the B2C tenant name or user flow ID in the callback URL contains uppercase letters, the validation fails.
solution: Ensure the callback URL registered in the Twitter/X developer app uses all lowercase letters for both the tenant name and user flow ID, even if they are defined with uppercase letters in Azure AD B2C. Example: use "b2c_1a_signup_signin_twitter" (all lowercase). Reference: Microsoft docs "Set up sign-up and sign-in with an X account - Azure AD B2C".
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: B2C API connector with Apple ID (preview) configured with trigger "After federating with an identity provider during sign-up" does not receive the email attribute/claim from Apple
rootCause: Apple allows users to choose whether to share their email address. Even if requested, the claim may not be returned based on user choice. Previously deleted users may retain Apple-side privacy settings from earlier approval, so they are not prompted again when re-signing in.
solution: This is by-design Apple IDP behavior and cannot be controlled by B2C. If a specific user needs to change their email sharing preference, they must fully remove the B2C application from their Apple ID account management page to reset the privacy choice, then sign in again to be presented with the sharing prompt.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: HTTP TRACE method appears in Azure AD B2C response headers (Allow or Access-Control-Allow-Methods); customer flags this as a security vulnerability and requests it be disabled
rootCause: B2C servers expose TRACE in allowed methods alongside other standard HTTP methods. This is by-design. Maximum information available via TRACE is already exposed through OPTIONS requests or the OAuth protocol itself, and B2C has no separate exploitable vulnerability that TRACE could chain with.
solution: This is not a security vulnerability in B2C and is by-design behavior. Microsoft has no method to disable the TRACE verb for individual customers and has no plans to add one. Advise the customer that TRACE alone cannot be exploited without a secondary vulnerability, and B2C has mitigations in place. Grade A in security scanners is still expected.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Frequently Asked Questions

symptom: Customer asks about Azure AD External Identities P2 (Identity Protection / risk-based Conditional Access) for B2C; or CA policies using user risk or sign-in risk conditions stop evaluating after March 2026
rootCause: Azure AD External Identities P2 license is retiring: End of Sale for new customers May 1, 2025; full service retirement March 15, 2026. Post-retirement, CA policies with P2 risk conditions remain in place but risk conditions (user risk level, sign-in risk level) are no longer evaluated - risk signals are not sent.
solution: Before March 15, 2026: (1) Identify affected CA policies using PowerShell (Get-MgIdentityConditionalAccessPolicy, filter on UserRiskLevels/SignInRiskLevels). (2) Migrate to partner fraud/identity providers: Deduce, eID-Me, Experian, IDology, Jumio, LexisNexis, or Onfido. (3) To switch to P1 billing only: follow docs "Change your Microsoft Entra pricing tier". After retirement, CA risk conditions silently have no effect.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Conditional Access and Identity Protection

symptom: New customer or engineer unable to create a new Azure AD B2C tenant after May 1, 2025; Azure portal only shows workforce or external tenant options; support request to create new B2C tenant
rootCause: Azure AD B2C reached End of Sale on May 1, 2025. Only tenants that had an Azure subscription linked to a B2C tenant as of the May 1, 2025 snapshot are permitted to create additional B2C tenants. New customers cannot purchase or create Azure AD B2C.
solution: For new customers: B2C is End of Sale; redirect to Microsoft Entra External ID. For existing customers (had subscription linked before May 1, 2025 snapshot): they can create new B2C tenants using same or a new subscription. Dev tenant without prior B2C (but prod has B2C): follow the exception escalation process. Full service continues until at least 2030 for existing customers.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C End of Sale and Azure AD B2C P2 Retirement

symptom: Customer performs load testing against Azure AD B2C in dev/UAT and observes performance issues, throttling, or receives reports of traffic irregularity flags
rootCause: Load testing against the main B2C production service causes sudden traffic spikes triggering upscaling delays and may flag traffic irregularities in B2C service monitoring.
solution: Direct load testing traffic to the B2C feature slice (pre-emptively scaled for load testing) by appending "&dc=BL2&slice=001-002" to the B2C authorize/RunNow URL. Only add these params to B2C endpoints (URLs containing tenantID + policy), not to external URLs. Example: https://{tenant}.b2clogin.com/{tenant}.onmicrosoft.com/oauth2/v2.0/authorize?p={policy}&client_id=...&dc=BL2&slice=001-002
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Load Testing

symptom: Azure AD B2C self-asserted page layout version shown in developer tools does not match the version set in portal; page version appears unchanged after customer updates it
rootCause: Not all page layouts have been updated to the same version; other pages (Local account sign up, MFA email, Blocked pages, password pages) may still have older versions, causing the SelfAsserted page to inherit a mismatched version
solution: Verify all page layouts share the same version: Local account sign up page, MFA email page, Blocked page (CA and API connector), update expired password, forgot password, change password pages. If versions differ, update all to the same version, then re-test.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/TSG - Self-asserted Page Version is not getting updated

symptom: Azure AD B2C SUSI user flow shows "Undefined" error when a user attempts to sign up with an existing account
rootCause: Self-asserted page layout version is lower than 2.1.19, which has a known bug causing undefined errors during sign-up with existing accounts
solution: Update the Local account Sign up page layout version to >= 2.1.19. Update all pages to use the Current page layout version and save changes.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/TSG - Undefined error in SUSI user flow

symptom: Azure AD B2C users with multiple SAML/OIDC/OAuth2 identity providers are redirected to the wrong IDP after session expiry instead of the IDP they originally used
rootCause: Multiple IDP Technical Profiles share the same Session Management Technical Profile name (e.g., SM-Saml-idp). B2C resolves SSO sessions by finding the first alphabetical match, selecting the wrong IDP.
solution: Assign a unique Session Management Technical Profile name to each IDP Technical Profile. Note: the EnforceReferencingSSOTP metadata flag was introduced to fix this but has been discontinued - verify with EEE if still present on tenant.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/TSG -Users get redirected to wrong IDP after session expiry

symptom: Azure AD B2C phone MFA stops working with error: A valid subscription must be linked to the tenant for phone verification
rootCause: May 2023 PG change blocks phone MFA requests for B2C tenants that do not have a valid Azure subscription linked to the tenant
solution: 1) Run Kusto query on AllIfxCPIMBillingRequestEvent to identify linked subscription. 2) If no subscription found, ask customer to link a subscription per https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dynamics365/commerce/create-link-aad-b2c-tenant. 3) If subscription linking fails or suspected bug in check, submit IcM to CPIM team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/TSG  - Phone MFA Subscription requirement

symptom: Azure AD B2C custom policy upload fails with: A required Metadata item with key ApplicationObjectId was not found in the TechnicalProfile
rootCause: TechnicalProfile references custom extension attributes (e.g., extension_requiresMigration) but is missing required Metadata entries: ApplicationObjectId and ClientId of the b2c-extensions-app application
solution: Edit the policy TechnicalProfile XML to add a <Metadata> section with: <Item Key="ClientId">{b2c-extensions-app application ID}</Item> and <Item Key="ApplicationObjectId">{b2c-extensions-app application ObjectId}</Item>. Find these values in Azure portal App registrations for b2c-extensions-app.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C with Custom Policies/A required Metadata item with key ApplicationObjectId was not found in the TechnicalProfile

symptom: B2C logout does not clear SSO cookies; users remain signed in to B2C despite calling the logout endpoint
rootCause: SSO Scope in Custom Policy UserJourneyBehaviors is set to Application or Suppressed; with Application-scoped SSO, B2C requires client_id to be passed, otherwise logout is incomplete and SSO cookies are not cleared
solution: Change SingleSignOn Scope in UserJourneyBehaviors from Application/Suppressed to Tenant. Alternatively, pass the client_id of the app to sign out of when using Application-scoped SSO. Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory-b2c/openid-connect#send-a-sign-out-request
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/Integrating B2C with SAML Identity Provider/Logout Issues SAML IDP

symptom: B2C does not send logout request to SAML IDP during sign-out; SAML IDP session not terminated
rootCause: SAML IDP metadata is missing the SingleLogoutService element for HTTP-POST and HTTP-Redirect bindings, so B2C cannot send logout requests to the IDP
solution: Check the SAML IDP metadata for SingleLogoutService entries. Ask customer to add SingleLogoutService bindings to IDP metadata: <md:SingleLogoutService Binding='urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:bindings:HTTP-POST' Location='...'/>
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/Integrating B2C with SAML Identity Provider/Logout Issues SAML IDP

symptom: B2C does not send logout request to SAML IDP even though SingleLogoutService is present in IDP metadata
rootCause: The SAML IDP Technical Profile in B2C uses SM-Noop (no-op session management), which suppresses the logout request to the identity provider
solution: Change the session management from SM-Noop to another provider such as SM-Social. In the SAML IDP TechnicalProfile, update UseTechnicalProfileForSessionManagement ReferenceId from SM-Noop to SM-Social or appropriate session management provider
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/Integrating B2C with SAML Identity Provider/Logout Issues SAML IDP

symptom: AADB2C90031 error: Policy B2C_1A_TrustFrameworkBase does not specify a default user journey when integrating SAML IDP with B2C
rootCause: The SAML IDP does not send the RelayState parameter back with the SAML Response; B2C requires RelayState to route to the correct user journey
solution: Ask the customer to configure their SAML IDP to include the RelayState in the SAML Response (same RelayState value received in the SAML Request). The IDP vendor/configuration team should resolve this
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/Integrating B2C with SAML Identity Provider/SAML IDP Integration Sign-in Errors

symptom: ResponderException: The service provider is not a valid audience of the assertion during SAML IDP sign-in in B2C
rootCause: The IssuerUri in the SAML AuthnRequest sent by B2C does not match the Audience element in the SAML Response from the IDP
solution: Check the SAML IDP configuration to ensure Audience in the SAML Response matches the IssuerUri configured in B2C. The IssuerUri from B2C SAML request must be set as the Audience (Service Provider EntityID) in the IDP
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/Integrating B2C with SAML Identity Provider/SAML IDP Integration Sign-in Errors

symptom: AADB2C99025 error: The specified SAML Binding method urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:bindings:HTTP-Artifact is not supported in B2C
rootCause: The SAML IDP metadata specifies HTTP-Artifact binding, which Azure AD B2C does not support natively
solution: Configure the SAML IDP to use HTTP-POST or HTTP-Redirect bindings instead of HTTP-Artifact. The IDP metadata should declare SingleSignOnService and SingleLogoutService entries with HTTP-POST or HTTP-Redirect bindings only
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/Integrating B2C with SAML Identity Provider/SAML IDP Integration Sign-in Errors

symptom: ResponderException: The claims provider response has an invalid signature during SAML IDP authentication in B2C
rootCause: The certificate used to sign the SAML token is incorrect or is not found in the IDP metadata URL configured in B2C
solution: Verify the SAML IDP metadata URL is correct and accessible, and that the certificate in the metadata matches what the IDP uses to sign tokens. Update the IDP metadata URL in B2C Technical Profile if incorrect
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/Integrating B2C with SAML Identity Provider/SAML IDP Integration Sign-in Errors

symptom: AADB2C CryptographicException: The specified Uri is not supported in SAML IDP integration; Kusto shows System.Security.Cryptography.CryptographicException
rootCause: The SAML IDP is using AES-GCM encryption algorithm (e.g., aes128-gcm) for the SAML assertion, which Azure AD B2C does not support
solution: Ask the customer to configure their SAML IDP to use AES-CBC 128, 192, or 256 for assertion encryption instead of AES-GCM. B2C only supports AES-CBC variants
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/Integrating B2C with SAML Identity Provider/SAML IDP Integration Sign-in Errors

symptom: SAML AuthnRequest to B2C fails because SP metadata defaults to HTTP-Artifact AssertionConsumerService binding; SAML sign-in flow broken when no ACS/ProtocolBinding in request
rootCause: The SP metadata has HTTP-Artifact as the default AssertionConsumerService binding (isDefault=true). Azure AD B2C does not support HTTP-Artifact and uses the default ACS from metadata when the request does not explicitly specify ACS
solution: Either (1) update SP metadata to set HTTP-POST as default AssertionConsumerService binding, or (2) include both AssertionConsumerServiceURL and ProtocolBinding=HTTP-POST in the AuthnRequest explicitly, or (3) use AssertionConsumerServiceIndex pointing to a non-Artifact binding
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/Integrating B2C with SAML Identity Provider/SAML Protocol Binding and AssertionConsumerServiceURL in SAML Requests to B2C

symptom: SAML AuthnRequest to B2C ignores isDefault=true flag on AssertionConsumerService; wrong ACS URL used when no ACSIndex provided in request
rootCause: B2C assumes ACS Index 0 when no AssertionConsumerServiceIndex is present in the AuthnRequest; if the isDefault ACS has a different index, it gets ignored
solution: Set tenant metadata flag AllowACSIndexAsNull to allow B2C to honor the isDefault flag when no ACSIndex is present in the AuthnRequest
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/Integrating B2C with SAML Identity Provider/SAML Protocol Binding and AssertionConsumerServiceURL in SAML Requests to B2C

symptom: After B2C logout when AAD is a federated identity provider, AAD session cookies are not cleared; user is not fully signed out from AAD and can re-authenticate without credentials
rootCause: B2C sends the logout request to the AAD v2 endpoint which does not clear AAD cookies; only the v1 endpoint clears AAD session cookies
solution: Set the v1 AAD endpoint as the Logout URL in the B2C Technical Profile for the AAD federated IDP. v1 endpoint format: https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/oauth2/logout. Future fix tracked in Bug 1617289 (add support for logout_hint)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/Logout issue with AAD as a federated identity Provider

symptom: B2C Japan GoLocal tenant subscription relink fails with Deployment validation failed: Cannot create resource with location Japan. Please use location Japan to create resource for directory
rootCause: A bug in the Azure Portal subscription relink flow for Japan GoLocal B2C tenants; the standard portal workflow does not properly handle Japan GoLocal tenant resource creation
solution: Use ARM template custom deployment as workaround: (1) In Azure Portal search Deploy a custom template, (2) Build your own template, (3) Paste the provided ARM template with parameters: location=Japan, tenantId, name=<tenant>.onmicrosoft.com, countryCode=JP, isGoLocalTenant=true, displayName. Click Review+create then Create.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/Subscription Relink Japan GoLocal Error

symptom: B2C users receive 403 error You do not have permission to view this directory or page when accessing B2C policy URLs still using login.microsoftonline.com
rootCause: Starting October 31 2022 Microsoft began throttling B2C tenants still using login.microsoftonline.com URLs for B2C policy endpoints; this was a previously announced deprecation from 2019
solution: Update all B2C policy URLs from login.microsoftonline.com format to supported b2clogin.com format: https://{tenantname}.b2clogin.com/{tenantname}.onmicrosoft.com/{policy}/... Use Kusto query TransactionModule.ThrottleMSOLTraffic to confirm throttling. PG has stopped accepting extension requests. See: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory-b2c/b2clogin
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/TSG - Azure AD B2C Deprecation of login.microsoftonline.com

symptom: B2C users or applications receive 426 error This service requires use of the TLS-1.2 protocol after April 30 2023
rootCause: Azure AD B2C enforced deprecation of TLS 1.0, TLS 1.1, and 3DES cipher suite TLS_RSA_WITH_3DES_EDE_CBC_SHA on April 30 2023
solution: Update all client applications to use TLS 1.2 for connections to B2C endpoints. Update federated identity providers to support TLS 1.2. Use Kusto on AADGatewayProd.AllRequestSummaryEvents filtering CryptProtocol == Tls or Tls11 to identify legacy TLS usage. Hard enforcement deadline was May 31 2023.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/TSG - Azure AD B2C Legacy TLS (1.0, 1.1, and 3DES) deprecation [Archived]

symptom: B2C custom page layout label such as Create/Forgot your password not updating even after updating Custom Page URI in user flow; UI still shows old text
rootCause: B2C serves localized strings from the Language template stored in B2C, not from the custom HTML file; the forgotpassword_link value in the Language template overrides what is in the HTML
solution: Update the forgotpassword_link string in the Sign-in page Language template: go to Languages blade, download Sign in page template, update Override/Value for forgotpassword_link, upload back. For custom policies: update LocalizedString for forgotpassword_link in B2C_1A_TrustFrameworkLocalization policy file.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD B2C/Azure AD B2C Troubleshooting/TSG - Custom Page Layout not showing the correct label

symptom: Protected state of a resource does not update after being added to or removed from a Restricted Management Administrative Unit (RMAU)
rootCause: Propagation delay in the system for the isManagementRestricted attribute
solution: Wait up to 30 minutes for the protected state to reflect after adding/removing a resource from an RMAU, or after the RMAU is deleted or changed to non-restricted
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Roles and Administrators/Azure AD Restricted Admin Units

symptom: Tenant-level administrator (e.g., User Administrator) finds Edit/management actions disabled on specific users, groups, or devices but not others; no error shown
rootCause: The target resource is a member of a Restricted Management Administrative Unit (RMAU). Tenant-level admin roles do not inherit permissions to manage resources in an RMAU
solution: Check the resource's Administrative Units blade for 'Restricted Management' = Yes. Assign the admin to the relevant role scoped to the RMAU, or assign a role scoped directly to the specific resource object
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Roles and Administrators/Azure AD Restricted Admin Units

symptom: Group owner is unable to manage a security group; group has isManagementRestricted = true
rootCause: Once a group becomes a member of an RMAU, group owners lose management rights. Only Group Administrators with a role scoped to the RMAU can manage the group
solution: Assign Group Administrator role scoped to the RMAU that the group belongs to, or assign the group owner to Group Administrator with a scope directly on the group object
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Roles and Administrators/Azure AD Restricted Admin Units

symptom: Tenant admin receives error: 'Insufficient privileges to complete the operation. Target object is a member of a restricted management administrative unit and can only be modified by administrators scoped to that administrative unit'
rootCause: Resources in a Restricted Management AU can only be managed by admins whose role is scoped to that specific RMAU. Tenant-level admin roles are explicitly blocked
solution: Add the admin to the relevant role scoped to the RMAU. Global Admins can add themselves to the scoped role to perform the task. For non-GA admins, a scoped role administrator must perform the action
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Roles and Administrators/Azure AD Restricted Admin Units

symptom: Third-party application with a role scoped to a Restricted Management Admin Unit (RMAU) receives 'Insufficient privileges to complete the operation' when managing objects with isManagementRestricted = true
rootCause: Third-party applications lack the Directory.Write.Restricted Microsoft Graph API permission. Only first-party apps are on the allow list that grants this permission automatically
solution: Grant the app registration the Directory.Write.Restricted Microsoft Graph API permission in Azure AD app permissions
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Roles and Administrators/Azure AD Restricted Admin Units

symptom: Applications or scripts that reference Azure AD built-in roles by display name stop returning results or silently fail with empty results after a role rename; no error is thrown
rootCause: Microsoft renamed ten Azure AD built-in role display names for consistency across admin center, Azure AD portal, and Microsoft Graph API. Apps looking up the old display name get empty results, as if the role does not exist
solution: Update the application or script to use the new role display names. For business-critical applications that cannot be updated immediately, the application can be added to an exception list by CSS/PG (exception delays the rename impact for up to ~1 day)
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Roles and Administrators/Azure AD Roles and Administrators UI

symptom: Attempting to add a role-assignable group (isAssignableToRole=true) as a member of another group fails with an error
rootCause: By design - nesting of role-assignable groups inside other groups is deliberately blocked to prevent elevation of privilege. The isAssignableToRole property enforces this immutable restriction
solution: This is by design and expected behavior. Reorganize the group membership structure to avoid nesting role-assignable groups. Use direct role assignments or separate role-assignable groups as needed
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Roles and Administrators/Role-Assignable Groups

symptom: User Account Administrator cannot update the password of a user who belongs to a group where isAssignableToRole = True
rootCause: By design - users in role-assignable groups are protected from password changes by tenant-level User Account Admins to prevent privilege escalation via credential compromise
solution: This is by design. Password management for users in role-assignable groups requires a role scoped directly to the RMAU or a GA/PRA to perform the operation
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Roles and Administrators/Role-Assignable Groups

symptom: DirSync (AADConnect) cannot be disabled because an on-premises directory-synced group is nested inside a role-assignable cloud group (isAssignableToRole=true)
rootCause: Adding an on-premises directory-synced group as a member of a role-assignable cloud group intentionally blocks disabling DirSync. This prevents an unsupported state where a non-role-assignable group would exist inside a role-assignable group after DirSync disable changes source of authority
solution: 1) Use the Locate Nested Groups PowerShell Script to find all on-premises groups nested in role-assignable cloud groups. 2) Remove on-prem groups from the role-assignable groups. 3) Disable DirSync. 4) After AADConnect is working again, re-add the on-premises groups. If still failing after these steps, engage Provisioning support team
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Roles and Administrators/Role-Assignable Groups

symptom: Azure AD tenant deletion fails with 'Directory has one or more Azure subscriptions' or 'Directory has one or more subscriptions to Microsoft Online Services', even though customer already cancelled all subscriptions. Or tenant was previously deleted successfully but was unexpectedly re-activated
rootCause: A subscription became orphaned during its automatic cancellation lifecycle: SubscriptionStatus is Warning/Suspended/LockedOut/Deleted but NextLifeCycleDate is in the past and the Commerce API scheduled deletion never completed. The orphaned subscription in MSODS still blocks tenant deletion and can trigger tenant re-activation
solution: 1) Use MSODS Explorer (or Kusto via 'Query Kusto for MSODS Subscriptions' wiki) to identify orphaned subscriptions (NextLifeCycleDate in past, SubscriptionStatus != Enabled). 2) Copy CommerceSubscriptionContext (GUID1/entitlements/GUID2), OcpSubscriptionId. 3) In OMS Tenant Console, reactivate sub-account if in Release state. 4) In OMS Subscription Console, search by Entitlement ID (GUID2), confirm OCP Status = Deprovisioned, then Re-Sync. 5) Verify subscription deleted in MSODS, then retry Validate+Delete in CMAT
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Azure AD Directory Deletion/TSG: Confirming orphaned subscriptions in MSODS

symptom: Orphaned subscription in Tenant B from a circular subscription transfer (A to B back to A) blocks tenant deletion. Attributes show SubscriptionSuspensionReason=Transition, TransitionSource==TransitionTarget. OMS shows Entitlement TransitionType=SubscriptionTransfer, State=InGracePeriod
rootCause: Pre-2021 bug that left entitlements orphaned in MSODS whenever a subscription was circularly transferred (Tenant A -> B -> A). The commerce footprint in Tenant B is left orphaned in a suspended state. Performing a resync of the entitlement will not remove it
solution: Escalate to Azure Commerce EEE team via ICM to queue 'Azure Commerce EEE/Triage (only for ASMS Team, Sev3/4, Business Hs Support)' requesting ACIS Action: 'Billing > Provisioning Management > Commerce - Delete Transition Source for Subscription'. Provide Entitlement/Subscription GUID and Commerce Account ID (SubAccount). After EE confirms deletion, if still appearing in MSODS check via BOSG -> Subscription console and verify SyncEntitlement failure
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Azure AD Directory Deletion/TSG: Confirming orphaned subscriptions in MSODS

symptom: Azure AD tenant deletion is blocked by 'company account is present in modern' error in CMAT; tenant has a modern billing account (MCA) associated with it
rootCause: A modern billing account (Billing Account/Legal Entity in modern commerce) exists and is associated with the tenant; CMAT's Validate+Delete requires this to be removed before tenant deletion can proceed
solution: CSS SE must: 1) Validate prerequisites in CST portal (billing profiles, subscriptions all deleted, invoices paid, no Azure Credits, IAM only 1 user, 1 invoice section, no active quotes). 2) For IW/Type B accounts: customer must self-delete by hard-deleting associated AAD users. 3) For Organization/Type C accounts: get written Global Admin consent, submit billing account deletion request in CST (Approver: CST_Azure_Identity_CSS_TA_Approvers), TA approves, then add CMAT skipmoderncheck tag after successful deletion. Requires SAW + AME.GBL account for CMAT/CST access
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Azure AD Directory Deletion/Tenant Deletion - Billing Account Deletion Process

symptom: Delete Billing Account button in CST portal is not clickable for an Individual - Type B - CL (IW) account type; CSS cannot delete it on behalf of the customer
rootCause: Individual (IW/Type B) billing accounts are self-service only; CSS does not have permission to delete them. The button exists in the UI but is disabled
solution: Customer must self-delete: 1) Create temp Global Admin (e.g., tempadmin@tenant.onmicrosoft.com). 2) Sign in with temp admin, permanently hard-delete original GA and all other users. 3) Attempt tenant deletion again. If only remaining CMAT blocker is the Type B 'company account in modern', proceed to add CMAT skipmoderncheck tag. If still blocked, escalate to TA
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Azure AD Directory Deletion/Tenant Deletion - Billing Account Deletion Process

symptom: NCE or modern Office 365 subscriptions cannot be immediately/fast deprovisioned; subscription remains active for 90 days after CustomerImmediateCancellation is performed in CST, blocking tenant deletion
rootCause: As of 2025-02, modern/NCE Office subscriptions have a mandatory 90-day deprovision lifecycle by product policy; the CustomerImmediateCancellation option starts the lifecycle but does not bypass the 90-day wait
solution: Raise ICM with the Recurrence team (see Escalation path section in billing account deletion wiki). Track product improvements: workitem 175292 (enabling customer_immediate_cancellation for Office products) and OSGS 56260657 (CST Enable NCE Subscription fast deprovision). In the interim, tenant deletion must wait for the 90-day deprovision period to complete
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/GeneralPages/AAD/AAD Account Management/Azure AD Tenant and Domain Management/Azure AD Directory Deletion/Tenant Deletion - Billing Account Deletion Process

symptom: User sessions and refresh tokens are not revoked when password expires. Users with expired passwords can continue accessing resources without being prompted to change password, because Azure AD does not automatically revoke tokens on password expiry.
rootCause: By design, Azure AD does not revoke existing refresh tokens when a password expires. Refresh tokens are only revoked by specific actions (credential change, user action, or admin action). For PHS-synced users, the cloud password policy is set to never expire, further complicating the behavior.
solution: Use Microsoft Graph PowerShell SDK to programmatically revoke refresh tokens for users whose password age exceeds the tenant's maximum policy: Connect-MgGraph, enumerate users with Get-MgUser filtering by LastPasswordChangeDateTime, compare age against policy threshold, then call Revoke-MgUserSignInSession for affected users. Note: For synced PHS users, check on-prem password policy as cloud policy is set to never expire.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/ACE Identity TSGs/Identity Technical Wiki/Useful PowerShell Scripts for AAD/Revoke Refresh Token for users with Expired Password

symptom: Azure AD SSH login to Linux VM fails with 'Access denied: to sign-in you must be assigned a role with action Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/login/action'. User completes device login flow but is denied access.
rootCause: The user has not been assigned an Azure RBAC role containing the Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines/login/action DataAction permission.
solution: Add the user to the built-in 'Virtual Machine Administrator Login' or 'Virtual Machine User Login' RBAC roles, or create a Custom RBAC role with the login/action DataAction.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Device Registration/Archived Wikis/Azure AD Sign-in for Azure Linux VMs (Deprecated don't use)

symptom: SSH login to Azure Linux VM with AADLoginForLinux extension shows 'Permission denied'. Auth log: pam_aad 'Failed to call back client. Make sure ChallengeResponseAuthentication in /etc/ssh/sshd_config is set to yes'. Device login URL not presented.
rootCause: ChallengeResponseAuthentication is set to 'no' in /etc/ssh/sshd_config, preventing pam_aad from sending device login callbacks. Often reverted to 'no' by Password Reset operations.
solution: Edit /etc/ssh/sshd_config, set ChallengeResponseAuthentication to 'yes', restart sshd. Or remove and reinstall the VM extension.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Device Registration/Archived Wikis/Azure AD Sign-in for Azure Linux VMs (Deprecated don't use)

symptom: SSH login to Azure Linux VM with AADLoginForLinux repeatedly prompts for password instead of device login flow. Uppercase letters in UPN (e.g. username@Contoso.com) cause continuous password prompts.
rootCause: AADLoginForLinux requires the UPN to be entirely lowercase. Any uppercase character causes fallback to password authentication instead of device code flow.
solution: Use all-lowercase UPN: ssh username@contoso.com@<vm-ip> (not username@Contoso.com).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Device Registration/Archived Wikis/Azure AD Sign-in for Azure Linux VMs (Deprecated don't use)

symptom: SSH to Azure Linux VM fails with 'User account has expired for illegal user' despite successful Azure AD auth. PAM shows 'Login granted' but sshd reports account expired. Affects Guest users with MI, or Members with only User Assigned MI.
rootCause: PAS calls Graph using System Assigned MI to check user existence by UPN only (not email). Guest UPN lookup fails. Members with only User Assigned MI (no System Assigned) also fail this path.
solution: For Members: enable System Assigned MI. For Guests: pre-register with 'sudo aaduseradd --comment <name> --oid <objectId> <email>' to add to /etc/aadpasswd. PG deployed service-side fix for email parity.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Device Registration/Archived Wikis/Azure AD Sign-in for Azure Linux VMs (Deprecated don't use)

symptom: Azure Linux VM Sign-In cloud app (AppId ce6ff14a-7fdc-4685-bbe0-f6afdfcfa8e0) not registered in Entra ID tenant. Cannot exclude/target in Conditional Access policies.
rootCause: The Azure Linux VM Sign-In cloud app SP is not auto-provisioned. Must be manually registered.
solution: Global Admin: Connect-MgGraph -Scopes Application.ReadWrite.All; New-MgServicePrincipal -AppId 'ce6ff14a-7fdc-4685-bbe0-f6afdfcfa8e0'
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/Device Registration/Archived Wikis/Azure AD Sign-in for Azure Linux VMs (Deprecated don't use)

symptom: ASP.NET Core web app hosted in multi-instance environment throws 'Unable to unprotect the message.State' or 'The oauth state was missing or invalid' during OAuth/OIDC sign-in flow
rootCause: Multiple app instances use different Data Protection Keys. Instance A encrypts the OAuth state parameter, but after authentication the Identity Provider redirects to Instance B which cannot decrypt with a different key.
solution: Solution 1: Configure ARR Affinity to ensure same instance handles the full flow. Solution 2: Configure a central Data Protection Store (e.g. Azure Blob Storage + Key Vault). Solution 3: Host only one instance. Note: This is an ASP.NET Core middleware issue, not Entra ID — route to ASP.NET support team if customer needs deeper help.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/DotNet/AspNet AspNetCore/Unable to unprotect the message State.

symptom: Office 365 users prompted for credentials instead of seamless SSO when using Alternate ID with domain hint. Office 2016 worked fine but Office 365 with OneAuth does not.
rootCause: OneAuth does not pass the domain hint to WAM during silent requests, causing interactive prompt instead of silent SSO. Feature request tracked in Engineering backlog.
solution: Workaround: Do not use Alternate ID. OneAuth currently does not support passing domain hint to WAM for silent requests.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: Error 'the service cannot be started, either because it is disabled or because it has no enabled devices associated with it' when running Connect-MicrosoftTeams with AccountID on Windows Server 2019.
rootCause: Web Account Manager (WAM) service is not enabled or running on the device.
solution: Enable WAM by running services.msc, locate 'Web Account Manager' service, enable and start it, then retry.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: OneDrive does not silently sign in on Virtual Machines. OneAuth log shows 'Account provider is null' with error tag 5akgo and StatusInternal::InteractionRequired.
rootCause: WAM account provider is missing on the VM, forcing OneAuth to require interactive UI flow to register the provider.
solution: Investigate with WAM team why the provider is missing. The interactive flow attempts to register the provider as part of the UI flow.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: Silent token calls on WAM failing with wamcompat_id_token not present in WAM response on Windows 10 RS1. Error tag 62ubh.
rootCause: Obtaining wamcompat_id_token is only supported starting with Windows 10 RS2 and above. RS1 does not support this feature.
solution: Upgrade to Windows 10 RS2 or later. This is expected behavior on RS1.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: Authentication error 1200 in Teams App with error tag 657rx, error code 2148073494. TPM-related error requiring device recovery.
rootCause: TPM error (TPM_E_PCP_WRONG_PARENT). Windows authentication broker cannot complete request due to TPM issue.
solution: Restart the computer. If issue persists, perform manual recovery per https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/office365/troubleshoot/authentication/connection-issue-when-sign-in-office-2016#manual-recovery
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: Something went wrong [1001] with tags 7q6ch and 5fcl8 after upgrading Office 365 from version 2308 to 2402 on Windows Server 2019 with Citrix.
rootCause: REGDB_E_CLASSNOTREG and ERROR_NOT_FOUND on Windows Server 2019 + Citrix environment. Tag 5fcl8 (ERROR_PROCESS_ABORTED) may indicate antivirus blocking AAD/MSA WAM plugin.
solution: Apply Citrix TSG: https://support.citrix.com/s/article/CTX267071. For 5fcl8 errors, add exclusion in antivirus/protection software for AAD/MSA WAM plugin.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: Users cannot sign in to Office desktop apps with error 1001 and tag 48v35. BrokerPlugin crash and svchost crash on Windows 10 22H2 hosted on AVD.
rootCause: Known Windows OS update bug on Windows 10 22H2 AVD: AppXSvc crash due to uninitialized m_targetUserSidString in OSIntegrationManagerHost::Initialize. Impacts both FSLogix and Broker Plugin.
solution: Engage Windows Performance team via collab. See internal Evergreen KB article for KIR.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: Teams 'No network connection' error 2603. Typically indicates HTTP status 511 STATUS_NETWORK_AUTHENTICATION_REQUIRED.
rootCause: Customer proxy setting is blocking or requiring network authentication. HTTP 511 indicates network authentication required by the proxy. Not an auth library issue.
solution: Review and fix customer proxy settings. Ensure proxy is not requiring additional authentication for Microsoft endpoints.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: Something went wrong 1001 in Outlook after migrating to Exchange Online. Users hitting access denied when connecting to Entra endpoints behind proxy with Kerberos auth.
rootCause: Kerberos TGT (Ticket Granting Ticket) refresh failure behind proxy. After TGT expires, all requests to Entra fail with access denied. AAD/Operational log shows 'On-prem tgt error: On-prem configuration is missing'.
solution: 1) Restart machine to force new TGT acquisition. 2) Whitelist M365 endpoints per https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/enterprise/urls-and-ip-address-ranges. 3) Engage AadCloudAPPlugin/WAM team if issue persists.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: AcquireCredentialInteractively prompts for password instead of going directly to MFA collection when WAM account is unavailable.
rootCause: When WAM account is not available (signed out or deleted), OneAuth calls WAM API with force prompt flag, requiring user to re-enter credentials instead of proceeding to MFA.
solution: Known limitation. OneAuth improvement planned to reduce/remove force prompt flags when UPN is known.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: Users unable to sign-in to M365 apps after installing Windows patch KB5062554. WAM returns AADSTS9002341: User is required to permit SSO.
rootCause: AADSTS9002341 is a by-design DMA (Data Migration Authorization) block. Results in WAM UI being shown, which may fail for other reasons.
solution: Follow WAM SSO TSG DMA error section. If interactive sign-in also fails after user permits SSO, investigate further with WAM team.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: 5z9gd error when signing into Word/Excel from AVD environment. Some AVD pools work while others fail with authorization failures.
rootCause: Server-side URL access restrictions in certain AVD pools. Specific Microsoft cloud URLs not whitelisted in customer network.
solution: Whitelist all required URLs per https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/enterprise/urls-and-ip-address-ranges
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: Teams and OneDrive frequently having sign-in issues caused by invalid/expired Primary Refresh Token. Error tag 657rx, error code 2148073494 (NTE_BAD_KEYSET).
rootCause: NTE_BAD_KEYSET error mapped to InteractionRequired by WAM. WAM has recovery logic but it does not trigger if device is in bad state.
solution: Check device state first and fix if needed. Collect recording and logs, then transfer to WAM team if recovery logic is not kicking in.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: VS Code logs into wrong account for MCP login. After WAM UI CBA flow, wrong account hint shown. Error code -895352830.
rootCause: Confirmed WAM bug: WAM shows wrong account hint after Certificate-Based Authentication (CBA) flow.
solution: Transfer to WAM team. This is a confirmed WAM bug.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Windows/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: iOS users asked to login to multiple Microsoft apps several times a day. Keychain errors -25308 (save) and -25300 (find) in OneAuth-MSAL logs with error tag 4ut12.
rootCause: iOS device keychain is corrupted. OneAuth/MSAL cannot save or retrieve tokens from the keychain, forcing repeated interactive authentication.
solution: Collect sys.diagnose dump from affected device (instructions: https://it-training.apple.com/tutorials/support/sup075). Escalate to msal-objC (Apple) team for investigation.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/iOS/Learnings From ICMs - iOS

symptom: iOS MAM-enabled app with MFA requirement shows multiple authentication prompts during login.
rootCause: MAM integration issue causing duplicate prompts when both MAM enrollment and MFA are required during sign-in flow.
solution: Update Microsoft Authenticator app to the latest version. Collect broker logs from Authenticator if issue persists.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/iOS/Learnings From ICMs - iOS

symptom: iOS Office app login fails with AADSTS50020: User account from identity provider does not exist in tenant after tenant migration.
rootCause: After tenant migration, old tenant information is cached in iOS keychain. Deleting app or Company Portal does not clear the cached tenant data.
solution: 1) Uninstall affected apps. 2) Open Edge, go to edge://signin-internals, click RemoveAllAccounts. 3) In Authenticator, remove all accounts then re-add. 4) Reinstall apps.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/iOS/Learnings From ICMs - iOS

symptom: Edge for iOS 'sign out and forget' does not permanently clear used login accounts. Accounts reappear after signing out from non-broker apps.
rootCause: By design on iOS, non-broker apps cannot permanently remove accounts. Only the Authenticator app (as the broker) has permission to permanently remove accounts from the device keychain.
solution: Sign into Authenticator app with the account, then remove it from Authenticator. This is the only supported way to permanently remove an account on iOS.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/iOS/Learnings From ICMs - iOS

symptom: Teams on iOS unable to sign in after tenant migration. Auth library still requesting resources from previous tenant even after deleting account from Company Portal.
rootCause: Tenant migration leaves stale tenant data in iOS keychain. Deleting account from Company Portal is not sufficient to clear iOS keychain storage.
solution: 1) Sign out from the app, Authenticator, and Company Portal. 2) Open Edge, sign out, go to edge://signin-internals, click RemoveAllAccounts. 3) Re-add account.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/iOS/Learnings From ICMs - iOS

symptom: Power App mobile player v3.25065.4 does not logout when MAM Intune policy is configured. Sign-out confirmation dialog does not appear and app terminates prematurely.
rootCause: App not properly coordinating app data wipe and sign-out with MAM SDK in correct sequence. When MAM is active during sign-out, incorrect ordering prevents proper sign-out flow.
solution: 1) Investigate app MAM SDK integration focusing on sign-out + data wipe sequence. 2) Validate MAM policy behavior with Intune team. 3) Escalate to OneAuth team if others confirm their components work.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/iOS/Learnings From ICMs - iOS

symptom: Users are getting prompted to sign in repeatedly in New Outlook for Mac with OneAuth MSAL error tag 965ya (StatusInternal::InteractionRequired, invalid_grant, token_expired)
rootCause: SIF (Sign-In Frequency) Conditional Access policy causes interactive prompt every 18 hours. The token_expired suberror triggers InteractionRequired, but this is expected CA-driven behavior, not a OneAuth/MSAL library bug.
solution: Review the tenant Conditional Access policies for Sign-In Frequency settings. If the 18-hour prompt interval matches the configured SIF policy, this is by design. Adjust the CA policy if the frequency is undesirable.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Android/Learnings From ICMs - Android

symptom: Work Profile Error encountered after domain password change with OneAuth MSAL error tag 5pa42 indicating SSL handshake process error in Android broker
rootCause: The error originates from the Android broker side during SSL handshake (tag 5pa42). This is not related to the OneAuth/MSAL library itself.
solution: Route to Mobile broker team for diagnosis and resolution. This is an Android broker issue, not OneAuth/MSAL.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Android/Learnings From ICMs - Android

symptom: After the latest Android OS version 14 update, users cannot access Teams, OneDrive with OneAuth MSAL error tag 5ssab (Android broker returns CANCEL)
rootCause: Android broker returns CANCEL status (tag 5ssab) after OS update. This is an Android broker-side issue, not related to OneAuth/MSAL library.
solution: Route to Mobile broker team for investigation. The issue is on the Android broker side, not OneAuth/MSAL.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Android/Learnings From ICMs - Android

symptom: User sees Something went wrong window with OneAuth MSAL error tag 5objn on Android, corresponding to broker returning INVALID_REQUEST
rootCause: Tag 5objn corresponds to Android broker returning INVALID_REQUEST. This can be returned by the broker from the server for multiple reasons and is a broker-side issue.
solution: Route to Android broker team for investigation. Since INVALID_REQUEST comes from the broker, the Android broker team should investigate the root cause first.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Android/Learnings From ICMs - Android

symptom: Android Edge app Conditional Access does not prompt to register device, OneAuth MSAL error tag 5sr96 indicating interaction required error from Android broker
rootCause: Tag 5sr96 indicates an interaction required error from the Android broker. In observed cases, the error was caused by the customer Conditional Access policy, with Logsminer showing error code UserStrongAuthClientAuthNRequired.
solution: Check the customer Conditional Access policies. Use Logsminer to search for the specific error code. The interaction required error is driven by CA policy, not a OneAuth/MSAL bug.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Android/Learnings From ICMs - Android

symptom: Getting invalid account (2201) errors when using SSO to sign into Edge on Android Shared Device Mode (SDM). Edge hits Invalid Account on startup but Teams gets an interrupt allowing login.
rootCause: Under SDM, Edge uses SignInSilently to load the current user profile. The account is in an interrupt state, and token acquisition fails with InteractionRequired, causing Edge to show Invalid account. Teams uses different startup logic that resolves the interrupt first.
solution: Upgrade to OneAuth 6.2.1 or later which introduces partial success status for SignIn* methods, where an account is returned along with an error. Edge should integrate with this new API behavior.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Android/Learnings From ICMs - Android

symptom: AirGap user running into authentication and connection issues testing from an Ubuntu Linux client after RDP client team moved from legacy auth library to OneAuth-MSAL
rootCause: RDP Linux client team removed the existing auth library approximately 6 months ago and moved to OneAuth-MSAL. Authentication does not work from Day 1 for partners using the RDP SDK in production in UsSec/UsNat environments. RDP team has no local test environment in these clouds.
solution: Route to RDP Linux client team to lead the investigation. While OneAuth-MSAL team may assist with fixes, the RDP Linux client team should own the issue since they integrated the SDK.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Linux/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: Linux Broker sign-in silently is not working as expected: device-bound PRT is downgraded by eSTS to a device-less PRT during the call
rootCause: eSTS server bug: the device-bound PRT should not be downgraded to a device-less PRT. In Logsminer, searching the device keyword shows the device-less PRT does not have a DeviceId.
solution: This is a server-side (eSTS) bug. File/reference bug 3300735 in Identity Division Engineering. In Logsminer, verify by searching device keyword to confirm the PRT lacks DeviceId.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/Linux/Learnings From ICMs

symptom: Teams cannot sign-in on macOS due to corrupted keychain with keychain error -25308 during interactive token acquisition
rootCause: A bug in OSQuery (github.com/osquery/osquery/issues/7780) corrupts the macOS keychain. Tools like MS Defender that use OSQuery can trigger this. The indicator is keychain error -25308 in logs and the Local Items keychain in Keychain Access app being locked or empty.
solution: 1) Open Keychain Access, check Local Items keychain - if locked or empty, proceed. 2) Quit all M365 apps and Keychain Access. 3) In Finder, Go To Folder: ~/Library/Keychains. 4) Delete all folders with long hex string names (keep .db files). 5) Restart Mac. 6) Verify Local Items keychain is unlocked, then sign in.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/MacOS/Learnings From ICMs - MacOS

symptom: User is unable to sign back in after signing out on MacOS with OneAuth MSAL error tag 6p73z Account has previously been signed out of this application despite completing a prompt
rootCause: This occurs most often with dual-headed accounts (email/UPN mapped to both an AAD and MSA account). Stale keychain entries prevent re-authentication even after the user completes a sign-in prompt.
solution: 1) Find the GUID of the user tenant ID. 2) Open Keychain Access on the Mac. 3) Search tenant ID GUID, select and delete all results. 4) Search MSA Tenant GUID 4c5b-b112-36a304b66dad, delete all results. 5) Restart Mac and sign in again. NOTE: This removes sign-in state from all MSFT apps and may require device re-enrollment.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/MacOS/Learnings From ICMs - MacOS

symptom: User is asked for a password on MacOS despite the SSO Extension being available and configured with the account. OneAuth/MSAL SignInSilently returns Default account could not be found on Mac.
rootCause: As of early March 2025, this is expected behavior. OneAuth/MSAL SignInSilently API only works on Windows (or iOS/Android in SDM). On MacOS, it returns InteractionRequired. Additionally, SignInInteractively sends prompt=login flags that block the SSO Extension from bypassing the password prompt.
solution: This is a known feature gap. Work is scheduled in CY25 Q2/Q3 to make SignInSilently support Mac SSO Extension and optimize SignInInteractively parameters. Until then, the password prompt on initial sign-in is expected. Share the technical explanation with the customer.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/MacOS/Learnings From ICMs - MacOS

symptom: FIDO key is not recognized in unbrokered passwordless authentication flow on Mac platform. webauthn=1 is being passed in the request but the key is not detected.
rootCause: This is a confirmed existing Apple issue (feedbackassistant.apple.com/feedback/15493539). When webauthn=1 is passed in the request and the expected dialog appears, the OneAuth/MSAL library is working correctly.
solution: Confirm the webauthn dialog appears correctly (library working as expected). The customer needs to work with Apple to fix this issue as it is an Apple platform bug.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Authentication/OneAuth MSAL/MacOS/Learnings From ICMs - MacOS

symptom: ASP.NET Core web app hosted in multi-instance environment throws 'Unable to unprotect the message.State' or 'The oauth state was missing or invalid' during OAuth/OIDC sign-in flow
rootCause: Multiple app instances use different Data Protection Keys. Instance A encrypts the OAuth state parameter, but after authentication the Identity Provider redirects to Instance B which cannot decrypt with a different key.
solution: Solution 1: Configure ARR Affinity to ensure same instance handles the full flow. Solution 2: Configure a central Data Protection Store (e.g. Azure Blob Storage + Key Vault). Solution 3: Host only one instance. Note: This is an ASP.NET Core middleware issue, not Entra ID - route to ASP.NET support team if customer needs deeper help.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Developer Scenarios/DotNet/AspNet AspNetCore/Unable to unprotect the message State.

symptom: Customer encounters ADAL library issues or needs ADAL support for authentication in their application
rootCause: ADAL (Azure Active Directory Authentication Library) is deprecated and no longer supported by Microsoft
solution: Migrate from ADAL to MSAL following official guide: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/msal-migration
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Support Boundaries/ADAL MSAL OWIN Support Boundaries

symptom: MSAL error occurs in first-party Microsoft application (Excel, Outlook, SharePoint) and case routing is unclear
rootCause: MSAL errors generated by first-party applications are owned by the respective workload team, not AAD Developer, as fixes require changes by that application's product group
solution: Route to the first-party application support team (SPO, EXO, etc.). AAD Developer only assists with MSAL token acquisition, not workload-specific MSAL errors. AAD Dev can be a collaboration partner.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Support Boundaries/ADAL MSAL OWIN Support Boundaries

symptom: OWIN or OpenIDConnect middleware error in ASP.NET application using Azure AD authentication, case routing needed
rootCause: OWIN/OpenIDConnect middleware development is owned by IIS Server team, not AAD Developer. AAD Developer only supports pipeline setup for token acquisition.
solution: Route OWIN/OpenIDConnect specific errors to IIS/ASP.NET server-side component support team. AAD Developer can set up the OWIN/OpenIDConnect pipeline for AAD Graph and MS Graph API token acquisition only.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Support Boundaries/ADAL MSAL OWIN Support Boundaries

symptom: Customer wants MSAL.js to not request refresh token or needs to remove offline_access from default OIDC scopes
rootCause: MSAL.js includes offline_access in OIDC_DEFAULT_SCOPES array by default, which always requests a refresh token
solution: Remove offline_access from OIDC_DEFAULT_SCOPES: import { OIDC_DEFAULT_SCOPES } from '@azure/msal-common'; OIDC_DEFAULT_SCOPES.splice(OIDC_DEFAULT_SCOPES.indexOf('offline_access'), 1);
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL For JS/MSAL js How to Scenarios

symptom: MSAL.NET application fails to authenticate when behind a corporate proxy; requests to login.microsoftonline.com time out or get blocked because default MSAL HttpClient has no proxy configured
rootCause: By default, MSAL.NET uses its own internal HttpClient that does not have proxy settings. Applications running behind a corporate proxy cannot reach Azure AD endpoints without explicit proxy configuration.
solution: Implement IMsalHttpClientFactory with a custom HttpClient configured with WebProxy (including credentials if needed), then pass it to PublicClientApplicationBuilder via .WithHttpClientFactory(new CustomMsalHttpClientFactory(httpClient)). Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/msal/dotnet/advanced/httpclient
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/MSAL/MSAL.net/Add custom HttpClient

symptom: MS Graph .NET SDK sends requests to the default v1.0 commercial endpoint but the application needs to target the beta API, US Government cloud (graph.microsoft.us), or a specific partner tenant
rootCause: GraphServiceClient defaults to https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0. Developers must explicitly override the BaseUrl property to target different endpoints (beta, government, or partner tenant).
solution: Set graphClient.BaseUrl to the desired endpoint: https://graph.microsoft.com/beta for beta API, https://graph.microsoft.us/beta for US Government, or https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/{tenant-id} for partner scenarios.
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/DotNet/Microsoft Graph DotNet Customize GraphServiceClient

symptom: MS Graph .NET SDK requests fail or time out when the application is behind a corporate proxy because GraphServiceClient uses default HttpClient without proxy settings
rootCause: The default GraphServiceClient does not configure proxy settings. Applications behind a corporate proxy need to provide a custom HttpClient with HttpClientHandler.Proxy configured.
solution: Create an HttpClient with HttpClientHandler configured with WebProxy (Proxy = new WebProxy(proxyUrl), UseProxy = true), then pass it to new GraphServiceClient(httpClient, authProvider).
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureAD/AzureAD:/Developer/Supported Technologies/Microsoft Graph API/DotNet/Microsoft Graph DotNet Customize GraphServiceClient
